## PAGE 00001: A Use Case scenario and an Activity diagram mapping schema

- page_id: `243968082`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243968082/A+Use+Case+scenario+and+an+Activity+diagram+mapping+schema
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:49.760+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Use Case > Use Case Scenario sketch
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

879969665

879969667

879969666

To understand the Use Case scenario representation in the Activity diagram, see the following table.

| Use Case Scenario | Mapping in Activity diagram | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| One (the first) step | The Activity with the Activity diagram is created under the owning Use Case just after the first basic flow step is created.The name of the created Activity and Activity diagram is the same as the owning Use Case name.In the Activity diagram, the Call Behavior Action is created for the basic flow step.The Initial Node is created before the Call Behavior Action.The Final Node is created after the Call Behavior Action.The Control Flow relationships are created from the Initial Node to the Call Behavior Action and from the Call Behavior Action to the Final Node. |  |
| steps | The Call Behavior Action is created for each basic flow step.The Initial Node is created before the first Call Behavior Action.The Final Node is created after the last Call Behavior Action.The Control Flow relationships are created from the Initial Node to the first Call Behavior Action, between each Call Behavior Action, and from the last Behavior Action to the Final Node. |  |
|  | The Call Behavior Action is created for the basic flow step of the included Use Case.The Call Behavior Action is inserted to the activity basic flow according to the order it was inserted in the basic flow.The Call Behavior Action is connected with the Control Flow relationships. The Call Behavior Action is not named.The Call Behavior Action has the Activity behavior defined. This Activity name corresponds to the name of the included Use Case.The activity (the Behavior or the Call Behavior Action) is owned by the included Use Case.If the included Use Case has its own Use Case scenario, this scenario is represented in the activity; the Activity diagram is created inside the Activity and flows are represented.To represent the included Use Case from the Activity diagram to the Use Case scenario, you must follow all the rules. Additionally, you must connect the including Use Case with the included Use Case with the included relationship in your project. |  |
| (with the extension point) | The extending Use Case with the extension point is added as an alternative flow; the Decision and Merge nodes are created.The Decision node name corresponds the name of the alternative condition.The Call Behavior Action is created for the alternative flow step of the extending Use Case. The Call Behavior Action is not named.The Call Behavior Action has the Behavior defined: the Activity whose name corresponds to the name of the extending Use Case.The Activity (the Behavior or the Call Behavior Action) is owned by the extending Use Case.If the extending Use Case has its own Use Case scenario, this scenario is represented in the Activity diagram; the Activity diagram is created inside the Activity under the extending Use Case.The [else] Guard property is defined for the Control Flow created from the Decision node to the Merge node.To represent the extending Use Case from the Activity diagram to the Use Case scenario, you must follow all the rules described above. Additionally, you must connect the extended Use Case with the extending Use Case with the extend relationship in your project. |  |
| (without any extension point) | The Call Behavior Action is created for the basic flow step of the Use Case scenario.The Call Behavior Action is connected with the Control Flow relationships according to the extending Use Case order in the basic flow.The Call Behavior Action is not named.The Call Behavior Action has the Behavior defined - the Activity whose name corresponds to the name of the extending Use Case.The Activity (the Behavior or the Call Behavior Action) is owned by the extending Use Case.If the extending Use Case has its own Use Case scenario, this scenario is represented in the Activity; the Activity diagram is created inside the Activity under the extending Use Case.To represent the extending Use Case from the Activity diagram to the Use Case scenario, you must follow all the rules described above. Additionally, you must connect the extended Use Case with the extending Use Case with the extend relationship in your project. |  |
| : alternative condition, alternative flow step | The alternative flow is interrupted in the basic flow by using the Decision and Merge nodes.The Decision and Merge nodes are created after the Call Behavior Action. The Call Behavior Action in the Use Case scenario represents the basic flow step of this alternative flow. In other words, in an Activity diagram, the elements of the alternative flow are created after the basic flow step this alternative flow belongs to.The Decision node name corresponds to the name of the alternative condition.The Call Behavior Action is created for each alternative flow step.All these elements are connected with the Control Flow relationships.The [else] Guard property is defined for the Control Flow created from the Decision node to the Merge node. |  |
| : exception type, exceptional flow step | The Structured Activity Node with the Input Pin is created.The Call Behavior Action for which the exceptional flow was created is linked with the Input Pin using the Exception Handler relationship.The Class element is created under the Activity. The Class corresponds to the exception type in the Use Case scenario, that is, the Class name corresponds to the exception type name in the Use Case scenario.The Class is assigned to the Input Pin as a type property.In the Structured Activity Node the Call Behavior Action is created for each Exceptional Flow step.The Call Behavior Actions inside the Structured Activity Node are connected with the Control Flow relationships.In the Structured Activity Node the Initial Node is created before the first Call Behavior Action, and the Final Node is created after the last Call Behavior Action.In the Structured Activity Node, the Initial Node is connected with the first Call Behavior Action by using the Control Flow relationship and the last Call Behavior Action is connected with the Final Node by using the Control Flow relationship. |  |

879969664

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967647 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00002: Abstraction

- page_id: `243967647`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967647/Abstraction
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:44:15.786+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Dependency
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

AnAbstractionis a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency' space=''] relationship that relates two elements or sets of elements that represent the same concept at different levels of abstraction or from different viewpoints.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

An Abstraction can be specified by changing its property values in the Abstraction[Specification window](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Specification+window). Each property is described in the description area in the Specification window.

You may see between which elements Abstraction is drawn in the Abstraction dialog, Source and Target boxes.

Set the Abstraction symbol properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MT'].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating Abstraction relationship

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=9919452 space=MED version=4 -->
## PAGE 00003: Accept Change Structural Feature Event Action

- page_id: `9919452`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/9919452/Accept+Change+Structural+Feature+Event+Action
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:46:27.499+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > SysML v1 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Accept Change Structural Feature Event Action is an action that waits for the occurrence of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Change Structural Feature Event' space=''] (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Directed Feature' space='']). The actions have exactly two output pins. The first output pin holds the values of the structural feature just after the values changed, while the second pin holds the values just before the values changed. The action only accepts events for structural features on the blocks owning the behavior containing the Action, or on the behavior itself, if the behavior is not owned by a Block.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Accept Event Action' space='MT']

#### PANEL: Related diagrams

Related diagrams

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Activity Diagram' space='MT']

The actions have exactly two output pins. The first output pin holds the values of the structural feature just after the values changed, while the second pin holds the values just before the values changed. The action only accepts events for structural features on the blocks owning the behavior containing the Action, or on the behavior itself, if the behavior is not owned by a Block.</p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="173ae66c-4bd7-448c-acbb-6872cb097815"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Accept Event Action" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cce34faa-2f55-4678-b24f-7f27f024c038"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related diagrams</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="SysML Activity Diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967464 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00004: Accept Event Action

- page_id: `243967464`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967464/Accept+Event+Action
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:32.715+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Action
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

907000387

907000389

907036862

**On this page**

****

907000388

The Accept Event Action is an Action that waits for the occurrence of an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Event' space='']that meets specified conditions. If an Accept Event Action has no incoming edges, then the Action starts when the containing activity or Structured Node starts, whichever most immediately contains the Action. In addition, an Accept Event Action with no incoming edges remains enabled after it accepts an Event. It does not terminate after accepting an Event and outputting a value, but continues to wait for other Events. An Accept Event Action that has no incoming edges and is contained by a Structured Node is terminated when its container is terminated.

| Notation | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Accept Event Action | The Cancel Order Request Accept Event Action is connected to Cancel Order with the Exception Handler relationship. |
| Rotated Accept Event Action | The symbol of Accept Event Action is rotated. |
| Accept Time Event Action | The Time Event is connected to the Call Behavior Action. |

Assigning signals

To assign a Signal using a drag-and-drop operation

To assign a Signal to an Accept Event Action, at least one Signal element should exist in the project.

1. Select the Signal in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MT'] .
2. Drag the Signal to the Accept Event Action as shown in the following picture. [ATTACHMENT filename='accept_event_action_drag_signal.png']

To assign a Signal using the Accept Event Action’s shortcut menu

1. Right-click the Accept Event Action to open the shortcut menu.
2. On the shortcut menu, click Signal. Click to select the Signal that you wish to assign to the element, or click the Create Signal button.

Once the Signal is assigned to the Accept Event Action, a Signal Event and a Trigger for this Action are created automatically. If you change the Signal, the Signal Event and the Trigger will change accordingly to a new Signal. The following figure shows the Signal Event and the Trigger positions in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MT'].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Using Time Event Shape

To draw the Accept Event Action with the Time Event Shape

- In the Activity diagram panel, select the Time Event button, and then click the diagram pane.

To change the position of the Accept Event Action name

1. Select the Accept Event Action shape on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='accept_event_action_selected.png']
2. Click the **Rotate State** button [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the upper-right side of the shape. The name of the Accept Event Action will appear on the opposite side of the shape. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can specify for an Accept Event Action, whether there is a single Output Pin for the Event or multiple Output Pins for the attributes of the Event.

##### Specifying the Is Unmarshall property

To specify the **Is Unmarshall** property

1. Select the Accept Event Action, and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'] .
2. Select or clear the Is Unmarshall check box. If the value is set to:

- 
  - false , then there is a single Output Pin for the Event, and a real-world instance of the Signal is placed on this Output Pin.
  - true , then there are multiple Output Pins for the attributes of the Event, and feature values of the Signal instance are placed on the corresponding Output Pins.

907000386

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Elements multiple selection' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca4452bd-ef92-45f9-b3d3-e3e59c12db62"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">907000387</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="151b6883-5297-4fc1-8d06-3fce8100226e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">907000389</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="40526df2-bc86-4ec9-91e4-3bf1fff7327c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">907036862</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><strong><br /></strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1eba450-976f-4713-a38b-1e6e8e6fc8a5" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6847d0c8-c6f1-4c05-8400-aaad0a55e295"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">907000388</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>The Accept Event Action is an Action that waits for the occurrence of an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Event" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Event ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>that meets specified conditions. If an Accept Event Action has no incoming edges, then the Action starts when the containing activity or Structured Node starts, whichever most immediately contains the Action. In addition, an Accept Event Action with no incoming edges remains enabled after it accepts an Event. It does not terminate after accepting an Event and outputting a value, but continues to wait for other Events. An Accept Event Action that has no incoming edges and is contained by a Structured Node is terminated when its container is terminated.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Notation</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Accept Event Action</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="accept_event_action.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>The<em> Cancel Order Request</em> Accept Event Action is connected to <em>Cancel Order</em> with the Exception Handler relationship.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Rotated Accept Event Action</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="accept_event_action_sample2.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>The symbol of Accept Event Action is rotated.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Accept Time Event Action</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="accept_event_action_sample3.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>The Time Event is connected to the Call Behavior Action.</p></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);font-size: 16.0px;font-weight: bold;line-height: 1.5;">Assigning signals</span></p><p>To assign a Signal using a drag-and-drop operation</p><hr /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c496d81b-c9cb-4c80-bd18-a7fbf5277b4a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To assign a Signal to an Accept Event Action, at least one Signal element should exist in the project.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><ol><li>Select the Signal in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Drag the Signal to the Accept Event Action as shown in the following picture.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="Signal dragging to Accept Event Action" ac:alt="Signal dragging to Accept Event Action"><ri:attachment ri:filename="accept_event_action_drag_signal.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To assign a Signal using the Accept Event Action’s shortcut menu</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Accept Event Action to open the shortcut menu.</li><li>On the shortcut menu, click Signal. Click to select the Signal that you wish to assign to the element, or click the <strong>Create Signal</strong> button.</li></ol><p>Once the Signal is assigned to the Accept Event Action, a Signal Event and a Trigger for this Action are created automatically. If you change the Signal, the Signal Event and the Trigger will change accordingly to a new Signal. The following figure shows the Signal Event and the Trigger positions in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Signal Event and Trigger in the Containment tree" ac:alt="Signal Event and Trigger in the Containment tree"><ri:attachment ri:filename="containment_tree_new_trigger_signal_event.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Using Time Event Shape</h3><p>To draw the Accept Event Action with the Time Event Shape</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Activity diagram panel, select the <strong>Time Event</strong> button, and then click the diagram pane.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To change the position of the Accept Event Action name</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Accept Event Action shape on the diagram pane.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="accept_event_action_selected.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><p>Click the <strong>Rotate State</strong> button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="rotate.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the upper-right side of the shape. The name of the Accept Event Action will appear on the opposite side of the shape.<br /><br /></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="accept_event_action_rotated.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ol><p>You can specify for an Accept Event Action, whether there is a single Output Pin for the Event or multiple Output Pins for the attributes of the Event.</p><h3>Specifying the Is Unmarshall property</h3><p>To specify the <strong>Is Unmarshall</strong> property</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Accept Event Action, and open its <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Select or clear the <strong>Is Unmarshall</strong> check box. If the value is set to:</li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><em>false</em>, then there is a single Output Pin for the Event, and a real-world instance of the Signal is placed on this Output Pin.</li><li><em>true</em>, then there are multiple Output Pins for the attributes of the Event, and feature values of the Signal instance are placed on the corresponding Output Pins.</li></ul></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dfd4cd6a-3026-4101-b63f-800de6248a68"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">907000386</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Elements multiple selection" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=228985872 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00006: Achieves

- page_id: `228985872`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228985872/Achieves
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:29:33.887+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A dependency relationship that exists between an Actual State (e.g., observed/measured during testing) of an element that attempts to achieve a desired effect and an Achiever.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

Dependency

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">A dependency relationship that exists between an Actual State  (e.g., observed/measured during testing) of an element that attempts to achieve a desired effect and an Achiever.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong><span class="fontstyle0">Architecture Framework</span></strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Dependency</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967463 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00007: Action

- page_id: `243967463`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967463/Action
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:32.678+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

906958764

906958767

906958766

An Action is a named element that is the fundamental unit of an executable functionality. The execution of an Action represents some transformations or processing in the modeled system, be it a computer system or otherwise. An Action execution represents the run-time [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space='']of executing an Action within a specific Behavior execution. As the Action is an abstract [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''], all Action executions will be the executions of a specific kind of Action. When the Action will be executed and what its actual inputs will be is determined by the concrete Action and the Behaviors in which it is used.

To remove an action from a diagram by keeping incoming and outgoing control flows connected, do one of the following

- On a diagram, select the Action and press Delete. Then, in the opened message, click Yes .
- In the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button. Then, in the opened message, click Yes .

- Flows can be joined only if the removed Action has one incoming and one outgoing Control Flow.
- You can select more than one Action to remove only if the selected part has one incoming and one outgoing Control Flow.
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='MT']**Environment Options** dialog , in the Diagram options group, under the Editing category, you can select one of the Join Control Flows option values: Ask to join control flows , Yes, or No .

An Action can be specified by changing its property values in the Action [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']. Each property is described in the description area on this window.

906958763

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Actions' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='MT']

As the Action is an abstract <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link>, all Action executions will be the executions of a specific kind of Action. When the Action will be executed and what its actual inputs will be is determined by the concrete Action and the Behaviors in which it is used.</p><p><br /></p><p>To remove an action from a diagram by keeping incoming and outgoing control flows connected, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>On a diagram, select the Action and press Delete. Then, in the opened message, click <strong>Yes</strong>.</li><li>In the diagram toolbar, click the <ac:image ac:alt="gt_delete.png"><ri:url ri:value="https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/9918725/gt_delete.png?version=1&amp;modificationDate=1444133483319&amp;api=v2" /></ac:image>Delete button. Then, in the opened message, click <strong>Yes</strong>.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a26999b-3209-4341-838c-866cd0689dc7"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Flows can be joined only if the removed Action has one incoming and one outgoing Control Flow.</li><li>You can select more than one Action to remove only if the selected part has one incoming and one outgoing Control Flow.</li><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, in the <strong>Diagram</strong> options group, under the <strong>Editing</strong> category, you can select one of the <strong>Join Control Flows</strong> option values: <em>Ask to join control flows</em>, <em>Yes,</em> or <em>No</em>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>An Action can be specified by changing its property values in the Action <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967809 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00008: Activators and predecessors

- page_id: `243967809`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967809/Activators+and+predecessors
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:41.833+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Message > Messages in Communication diagrams
- labels: ['change-activator']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

372605732

372605734

372605733

INLINE

The predecessor denotes the set of Messages. The completion of these Messages enables the execution of the current Message. The predecessor means that the execution of a Message is not enabled until all the communications with sequence numbers appearing in the list have occurred. Therefore, the list of predecessors represents a synchronization of threads. The Message corresponding to the numerically preceding sequence number is an implicit predecessor and does not need to be explicitly listed.

All of the sequence numbers with the same prefix form a sequence. The numerical predecessor is the one in which the final term is one less. That is, number 6.4 is the predecessor of 6.5, where the number “6” is an activator (see the example in the following figure).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To show predecessors beside the Message number

Make sure the **Use Advanced Numbering** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Messages in Communication diagrams' space=''].

- From the Message shortcut menu, select Show Predecessors .
- In the Message [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MT']**Symbol Properties** dialog , set Show Predecessors to true .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To change an activator number of Messages

Make sure the **Use Advanced Numbering** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Messages in Communication diagrams' space=''].

1. From the Message shortcut menu, select Activator .
2. Select the activator you want to assign to the selected Message. Numbering of the selected Message and subsequent Messages decreases by one level. The first level number is the number of an activator Message (see the following figure). [IMAGE alt='' src=''] If you change the activator number to a predecessor Message, this number will change for subsequent predecessors.

372605731

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Lifeline' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='']

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d6f12b6d-67dc-43b8-8bc2-bc28e5a05c37"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">372605732</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f0e6c9ed-021e-4ff4-8c84-91071150d7d3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">372605734</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a928da5-26c6-4eb9-a37b-de592881ccfd"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">372605733</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="351fbf21-37f5-4a9c-8f7a-64aadcbbaee6"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The predecessor denotes the set of Messages. The completion of these Messages enables the execution of the current Message. The predecessor means that the execution of a Message is not enabled until all the communications with sequence numbers appearing in the list have occurred. Therefore, the list of predecessors represents a synchronization of threads. The Message corresponding to the numerically preceding sequence number is an implicit predecessor and does not need to be explicitly listed.</p><p>All of the sequence numbers with the same prefix form a sequence. The numerical predecessor is the one in which the final term is one less. That is, number 6.4 is the predecessor of 6.5, where the number “6” is an activator (see the example in the following figure).</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Message numbering with activators and predecessors" ac:alt="Message numbering with activators and predecessors"><ri:attachment ri:filename="predecessor_activator.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To show predecessors beside the Message number</p><hr /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dcb980bb-89d9-4d45-a82c-ac16fd8be796"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Make sure the <strong>Use Advanced Numbering</strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Messages in Communication diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[is selected]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li>From the Message shortcut menu, select <strong>Show Predecessors</strong>.</li><li>In the Message <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /><ac:link-body><strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, set <strong>Show Predecessors</strong> to <em>true</em>.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="Predecessors displayed beside Message number" ac:alt="Predecessors displayed beside Message number"><ri:attachment ri:filename="predecessors_of_message.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To change an activator number of Messages</p><hr /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8610b86f-04dd-499f-bcac-0eecb649cfd7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Make sure the <strong>Use Advanced Numbering</strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Messages in Communication diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[is selected]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ol><li>From the Message shortcut menu, select <strong>Activator</strong>.</li><li><p>Select the activator you want to assign to the selected Message. Numbering of the selected Message and subsequent Messages decreases by one level. The first level number is the number of an activator Message (see the following figure).</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Changing activator" ac:alt="Changing activator"><ri:attachment ri:filename="changing_activator.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c7abd52f-267e-4030-a6ce-9b8d4dfe3c73"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you change the activator number to a predecessor Message, this number will change for subsequent predecessors.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4ff96047-a639-4562-9c59-f097cda5d3c0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">372605731</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Lifeline" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lifelines]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interaction Use" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Interaction]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operation" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Message" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967762 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00009: Active Validation Rules for Links

- page_id: `243967762`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967762/Active+Validation+Rules+for+Links
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:40.894+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Link
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Active Validation instantly checks the accuracy, completeness, and correctness of a model, displays errors in the model, and suggests solutions.

There are two active validation rules for links:

- [Instance Specification type mismatch](#ActiveValidationRulesforLinks-InstanceSpecificationtypemismatch)
- [Missing slots in links](#ActiveValidationRulesforLinks-Missingslotsinlinks)

###### Instance Specification type mismatch

The *Instance Specification type mismatch* validation rule allows for updating slot values when referenced association end types are incompatible with connected instance specification types.

###### Missing slots in links

The *Missing slots on links* validation rule is more useful than migrating projects from the modeling tool 17.0.2 version or earlier. The validation rule detects links that have related associations but do not have slots created in them. For each incorrect link, the warning is displayed. To resolve the warning, there is the **Create slots for this link** resolution action. After choosing that action, slots are created in the link.

The validation rule is available only if the **Automatically Create Slots in Link** option is set to *true* in the Environment Options dialog.

#### PANEL: On this Page

On this Page

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Link' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Links' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Object diagram' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Active Validation instantly checks the accuracy, completeness, and correctness of a model, displays errors in the model, and suggests solutions. </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">There are two active validation rules for links: </span></p><ul><li><span class="toc-item-body"><a href="#ActiveValidationRulesforLinks-InstanceSpecificationtypemismatch" class="toc-link">Instance Specification type mismatch</a></span></li><li><span class="toc-item-body"><a href="#ActiveValidationRulesforLinks-Missingslotsinlinks" class="toc-link">Missing slots in links</a></span></li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br /></span></p><h4><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Instance Specification type mismatch</span></h4><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The <em>Instance Specification type mismatch</em> validation rule allows for updating slot values when referenced association end types are incompatible with connected instance specification types. </span></p><h4><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Missing slots in links </span></h4><p>The <em>Missing slots on links</em> validation rule is more useful than migrating projects from the modeling tool 17.0.2 version or earlier. The validation rule detects links that have related associations but do not have slots created in them. For each incorrect link, the warning is displayed. To resolve the warning, there is the <strong>Create slots for this link</strong> resolution action. After choosing that action, slots are created in the link.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="317c1fd0-1cec-4975-8085-1bdd1042810a"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The validation rule is available only if the <strong>Automatically Create Slots in Link</strong> option is set to <em>true</em> in the Environment Options dialog.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e13e61bc-d2f0-4b94-97f4-1eb81bfd9471"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">On this Page</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8fd8a45d-df41-4f9c-8787-45423aa74b33" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0f82cb6f-54ce-4959-b04d-82eb40eb6663"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Link" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Links" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Object diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967490 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00010: Activity

- page_id: `243967490`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967490/Activity
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:44:14.401+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

907240486

907240489

907240488

An Activityis a parameterized [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space='']represented as a coordinated flow of[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Action' space=''].An Activity specifies the coordination of executions of subordinate behaviors, using a control and data flow model. The subordinate behaviors coordinated by these models may be initiated because other behaviors in the model finish executing, objects and data become available, or events occur external to the flow. The flow of execution is modeled as activity nodes connected by activity edges.

An Activity element is created in the Activity diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

An Activity can be specified by changing its property values in the Activity[Specification window](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Specification+window). Each property is described in the description area on this window.

General specification pane properties

| Property Name | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Read Only | If true, this activity must not make any changes to variables outside the activity or to objects. (This is an assertion, not an executable property. It may be used by an execution engine to optimize model execution. If the assertion is violated by the action, then the model is not formed correctly.) The default is false (an activity may make non-local changes). |
| Single Execution | If true, all invocations of the activity are handled by the same execution. |
| Node | Nodes coordinated by the activity. |

In theActivity[Specification window](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Specification+window), choose [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Variables' space=''] from the property group list.

907240485

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Action' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Variables' space='']

## PAGE 00011: Activity Final Node

- page_id: `243967498`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967498/Activity+Final+Node
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:44:11.001+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Activity Final Node (symbol a circle surrounding a smaller solid circle) is a final node that stops all flows in an activity.

An Activity Final Node can be specified by changing its property values in the Activity Final Node[Specification window](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Specification+window). Each property is described in the description area on this window.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">An Activity Final Node (<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">symbol a circle surrounding a smaller solid circle)</span> is a final node that stops all flows in an activity.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">An Activity Final Node can be specified by changing its property values in the Activity Final Node </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Specification+window">Specification window</a><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. Each property is described in the description area on this window.</span><br /></span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></p>
````

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An abstract element that represents a behavior or process (i.e. a Function or Operational Activity) that can be performed by a Performer.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Activity

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967828 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00013: Activity Parameter Node

- page_id: `243967828`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967828/Activity+Parameter+Node
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:44:10.704+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Node
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

836520319

836520323

836624898

**On this page**

43

836520322

##### Description

Activity Parameter Nodes are object nodes at the beginning and end of the flows that are used to accept inputs and provide outputs from activities.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Creating an Activity Parameter Node

To create an Activity Parameter Node

1. In the Activity diagram palette, click the arrow next to the Object Node button to expand the list, then select Activity Parameter Node .
2. Click the Activity diagram frame. The Activity Parameter Node is created, and its shortcut menu opens.
3. Select Edit New Parameter to specify the parameter. The Parameter dialog opens.
4. Specify the parameter, and click Close.

The parameter for the Activity Parameter Node is created automatically. 
The parameter name, type, and multiplicity are synchronized with the pin or Object Node from which/to which the Object Flow was created. The direction of the parameter is set according to the Object Flow direction, otherwise you can set it manually by simply right-clicking the activity parameter and selecting the preferred direction.

When creating an Activity Parameter Node on a diagram frame, click on the diagram frame to open the menu. In the menu, the following Activity Parameter Nodes are listed:

- 
  - parameters that are not displayed on the diagram frame.
  - parameters that are owned by the same activity as the activity diagram.
  - parameters that have assigned parameters that are also owned by the same activity.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If a parameter with an *inout* direction is selected for the Activity Parameter Node creation, then two Activity Parameter Nodes are created on the diagram frame.

You can also create an Activity Parameter Node by drawing an Object Flow directly to or from the Activity diagram frame.

##### Specifying Activity Parameter Node type

Once the Activity Parameter Node is created, you can specify its type. Use one of the following ways:

- Using the **Specify Type** button.
- 

buttonTo specify Activity Parameter Node type by using the **Specify Type** button

1. Select the Activity Parameter Node shape.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='specify_type.png'] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulator toolbar' space='MT'] .
3. Do one of the following: - Select an existing element from the Select Type menu. - Create a new type: a. Type a name. b. Press Enter. c. In the Create <type name> As dialog, select the element kind. The type is specified for an Activity Parameter Node.

windowTo specify Activity Parameter Node type by using the Specification window

1. Right-click the Activity Parameter Node and select the Specification command.
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'] , select the Type property value cell.
3. Do one of the following: - Select an existing element from the list. - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button_in_specification_window.png'] and in the Select Element dialog do the following: - Select an existing element from the Tree or List tab. - create a new element by switching on the Creation Mode and clicking the Create button.
4. Click Close . The type is specified for an Activity Parameter Node.

##### Setting the direction of an Activity Parameter Node

To set the direction of an Activity Parameter Node

1. Right-click the Activity Parameter Node on the diagram and select Specification .
2. In the Parameter property group, click to expand the Direction options drop-down menu.
3. Select the preferred direction and click Close .

##### Displaying Activity Parameter Nodes

You can display Activity Parameter Nodes on the frame of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT'] when you are:

- .
- .

newTo display Activity Parameter Nodes when creating a new Activity diagram

1. When creating a new Activity diagram for an Activity element that contains Activity Parameter Nodes, the Select Parameters dialog will open automatically. [ATTACHMENT filename='select_parameters_dialog_when_creating_new_activity_diagram.png']
2. In the opened**Select Parameters** dialog, select the parameters that you want to display, and click **OK**. 
Selected Activity Parameter Nodes are displayed on the frame of the Activity diagram. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] If you want all Activity Parameter Nodes to be displayed automatically when creating a new Activity diagram, go to **Options** > **Project**on the main menu, and set the **Display All Activity Parameter Nodes in Diagrams** option to *true.***

already_createdTo display Activity Parameter Nodes on the frame in a pre-existing Activity diagram

1. Select the Activity diagram pane.
2. Open the Select Parameters dialog by performing one of the following steps: - From the shortcut menu, select Display > Display Parameter Nodes . - On the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select Display Parameter Nodes. [ATTACHMENT filename='display_parameter_nodes_command.png']
3. In the dialog, select the parameters you want to display, and click **OK**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The selected Activity Parameter Nodes are displayed on the frame of the Activity diagram. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Displaying or hiding the direction of an Activity Parameter Node

To display or hide the direction of an Activity Parameter Node

1. Right-click the Activity Parameter Node on the diagram, and select Symbol Properties .
2. In the Symbol Properties dialog, click to select or clear the Show Direction check box to display or hide the direction.
3. Click Close .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- You can see the direction of the parameter displayed in the Containment tree before choosing the parameter name.
- If an Activity Parameter Node is associated with the parameter of an inout direction, then the direction to the Activity Parameter Node is set according to the connected flows. If an outgoing flow is connected to the newly created Activity Parameter Node, then this Activity Parameter Node is set as an input node, and the in parameter direction is used.

836520317

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Activity diagram** sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download *[ATTACHMENT filename='activity diagram.mdzip'].*
- Find in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\diagrams\activity diagram.mdzip.

<strong>Specification</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Parameter</strong> property group, click to expand the <strong>Direction</strong> options drop-down menu.</li><li>Select the preferred direction and click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Displaying Activity Parameter Nodes</h3><p>You can display Activity Parameter Nodes on the frame of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link> when you are:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="new"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating a new Activity diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="already_created"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Modifying a pre-existing Activity diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ecaa510e-e61f-4ecd-a290-03c160939962"><ac:parameter ac:name="">new</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To display Activity Parameter Nodes when creating a new Activity diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>When creating a new Activity diagram for an Activity element that contains Activity Parameter Nodes, the <strong>Select Parameters</strong> dialog will open automatically.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Automatically opened the Select Parameters dialog when creating a new Cool Room Activity diagram" ac:alt="Automatically opened the Select Parameters dialog when creating a new Cool Room Activity diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_parameters_dialog_when_creating_new_activity_diagram.png" /></ac:image></li><li><p>In the opened<strong> Select Parameters</strong> dialog, select the parameters that you want to display, and click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />Selected Activity Parameter Nodes are displayed on the frame of the Activity diagram.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Displayed Activity Parameter Nodes on the frame of the Cool Room Activity diagram" ac:alt="Displayed Activity Parameter Nodes on the frame of the Cool Room Activity diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="parameter_nodes-displayed_on_frame_after_creating_new_activity_diagram.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dbdb3dc4-ec3d-4573-bfc9-0c8c4f04f7e9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want all Activity Parameter Nodes to be displayed automatically when creating a new Activity diagram, go to <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project </strong>on the main menu, and set the <strong>Display All Activity Parameter Nodes in Diagrams</strong> option to <em>true.</em><em><br /></em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea579e1a-d860-43bd-ad58-b3e82b2ed0c8"><ac:parameter ac:name="">already_created</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To display Activity Parameter Nodes on the frame in a pre-existing Activity diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Activity diagram pane.</li><li>Open the <strong>Select Parameters</strong> dialog by performing one of the following steps:<br /> - From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Parameter Nodes</strong>.<br /> - On the diagram toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image></span></span> and select <strong>Display Parameter Nodes.<br /></strong><br /><ac:image ac:title="The Display Parameter Nodes command in the Collect Computer Activity diagram" ac:alt="The Display Parameter Nodes command in the Collect Computer Activity diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_parameter_nodes_command.png" /></ac:image></li><li><p>In the dialog, select the parameters you want to display, and click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:title="The Select Parameters dialog of the Collect Computer Activity diagram" ac:alt="The Select Parameters dialog of the Collect Computer Activity diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_parameters_dialog.png" /></ac:image><br />The selected Activity Parameter Nodes are displayed on the frame of the Activity diagram.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Displayed Activity Parameter Nodes on the frame of the Collect Computer Activity diagram" ac:alt="Displayed Activity Parameter Nodes on the frame of the Collect Computer Activity diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="parameter_nodes-displayed_on_frame.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ol><h3>Displaying or hiding the direction of an Activity Parameter Node</h3><p>To display or hide the direction of an Activity Parameter Node</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Activity Parameter Node on the diagram, and select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog, click to select or clear the <strong>Show Direction</strong> check box to display or hide the direction.</li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:title="Example of the parameter direction displayed on Activity Parameter Node " ac:alt="Example of the parameter direction displayed on Activity Parameter Node " ac:height="70" ac:width="145"><ri:attachment ri:filename="activity-parameter-node-direction.png" /></ac:image><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper confluence-embedded-manual-size"> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b6789eb8-2f33-43c5-9a69-aa41b4f188a9"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can see the direction of the parameter displayed in the <strong>Containment</strong> tree before choosing the parameter name.</li><li>If an Activity Parameter Node is associated with the parameter of an <em>inout</em> direction, then the direction to the Activity Parameter Node is set according to the connected flows. If an outgoing flow is connected to the newly created Activity Parameter Node, then this Activity Parameter Node is set as an input node, and the <em>in</em> parameter direction is used.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e8c27d1-9fc6-4666-ba85-f7c2e75effbf"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">836520317</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="three_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /><ac:link-body><span>Activity</span> diagram</ac:link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Node" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Expansion Region and Expansion Nodes" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Loop, Sequence, and Conditional Nodes" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Structured Activity Node" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures of this page is the <strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Activity diagram</span></strong> sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Download<span class="confluence-link"><em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span> <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="activity diagram.mdzip"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.<br class="confluence-link" /></span></span></em></span></li><li>Find in modeling tool <em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">samples\diagrams</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">\<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">activity diagram</span>.mdzip.</span></span></em></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967499 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00015: Actor

- page_id: `243967499`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967499/Actor
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:33.418+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

907312959

907312961

907312960

Actors represent roles played by human users, external hardware, and other subjects. An Actor does not necessarily represent a specific physical entity but merely a particular “role” of some entities that is relevant to an action performed by the Actor. Typically, such actions are called [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='']. An Actor is represented as a “stick man” figure with the name below the figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can format the actor symbol properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MT'].

An Actor can be specified by changing its property values in the Actor [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']. Each property is described in the description area on this window.

Actors can be represented in other diagrams, such as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MT'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT'].

##### Actor usage in other diagrams

The actor can be used in other diagrams in various ways. Most common cases are as follows:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Lifeline' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Swimlanes' space=''] .

lifelineTo create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Lifeline' space='']for an Actor in a Sequence diagram

1. Open the Sequence diagram wherein you want to create a lifeline.
2. Select an Actor in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MT'] .
3. Drag and drop the selected Actor on the Sequence diagram pane. The Lifeline is created. In the following figure, see an example wherein two Lifelines are depicted.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

swimlaneTo create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Swimlanes' space=''] header for an Actor in an Activity diagram

1. Open an Activity diagram.
2. Create a Swimlane.
3. Select an Actor in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MT'] .
4. Drag and drop the Actor on the Swimlane header in the Activity diagram. The Actor is assigned to the header of the Swimlane. See the example in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can see the usage of Actors in the example of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MT'] in *<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\diagrams\use case diagram.mdzip*.

907312958

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Typically, such actions are called <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Use Case" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Use Cases]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. An Actor is represented as a “stick man” figure with the name below the figure.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Example of Actor connected to Use Case" ac:alt="Example of Actor connected to Use Case"><ri:attachment ri:filename="actor.png" /></ac:image></p><p>You can format the actor symbol properties in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol Properties dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>An Actor can be specified by changing its property values in the Actor <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. Most common cases are as follows:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="lifeline"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Actors can be depicted as ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Lifeline" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lifelines ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <ac:link ac:anchor="lifeline"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[in a Sequence diagram.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="swimlane"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Actors can be depicted as ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Swimlanes" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Swimlane ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <ac:link ac:anchor="swimlane"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[headers in an Activity diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul><p><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="690b59b3-cd57-4518-88c9-32929d2f549b"><ac:parameter ac:name="">lifeline</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To create a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Lifeline" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lifeline ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>for an Actor in a Sequence diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Sequence diagram wherein you want to create a lifeline.</li><li>Select an Actor in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link>.</li><li><p>Drag and drop the selected Actor on the Sequence diagram pane. The Lifeline is created. In the following figure, see an example wherein two Lifelines are depicted.</p></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:title="Lifelines of particular Actor types in Sequence diagram" ac:alt="Lifelines of particular Actor types in Sequence diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="actor_in_sequence.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d10d97b-3203-48bb-acbc-9d71c1ac607b"><ac:parameter ac:name="">swimlane</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To create a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Swimlanes" /></ac:link> header for an Actor in an Activity diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open an Activity diagram.</li><li>Create a Swimlane.</li><li>Select an Actor in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Drag and drop the Actor on the Swimlane header in the Activity diagram. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=228985881 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00021: Actual Location

- page_id: `228985881`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228985881/Actual+Location
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:29:37.222+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Actual Location is an Actual State that describes a physical location, for example using text to provide an address, Geo-coordinates, etc.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

Instance Specification

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>Actual Location is an Actual State that describes a physical location, for example using text to provide an address, Geo-coordinates, etc.</p><p><strong><span class="fontstyle0">Architecture Framework</span></strong></p><p>UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</p><p><strong>Extension</strong></p><p>Instance Specification</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=228985884 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00024: Actual Milestone Kind

- page_id: `228985884`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228985884/Actual+Milestone+Kind
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:29:37.817+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Enumeration of the possible kinds of Actual Measurement. Its enumeration literals are:

- InService - Indicates that the Actual Project Milestone associated with the Actual Milestone Kind is when the configuration goes into service.
- Deployed - Indicates that the Actual Project Milestone associated with the Actual Milestone Kind is a configuration deployment milestone.
- NoLongerUsed - Indicates that the Actual Project Milestone associated with the Actual Milestone Kind is when the deployed configuration is no longer used.
- OutOfService - Indicates that the Actual Project Milestone associated with the Actual Milestone Kind is when the in service configuration goes out of service.
- Other - Indicates that the Actual Project Milestone associated with the Actual Milestone Kind is not one of the standard Actual Milestone Kinds.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGBoldBulletKeepNextParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Enumeration of the possible kinds of Actual Measurement. Its enumeration literals are:</p><ul><li>InService - Indicates that the Actual Project Milestone associated with the Actual Milestone Kind is when the configuration goes into service.</li><li>Deployed - Indicates that the Actual Project Milestone associated with the Actual Milestone Kind is a configuration deployment milestone.</li><li>NoLongerUsed - Indicates that the Actual Project Milestone associated with the Actual Milestone Kind is when the deployed configuration is no longer used.</li><li>OutOfService - Indicates that the Actual Project Milestone associated with the Actual Milestone Kind is when the in service configuration goes out of service.</li><li>Other - Indicates that the Actual Project Milestone associated with the Actual Milestone Kind is not one of the standard Actual Milestone Kinds.</li></ul><p><strong>Architecture</strong> <strong>Framework</strong></p><p>UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=228985888 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00032: Actual Outcome

- page_id: `228985888`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228985888/Actual+Outcome
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:29:40.865+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Something that happens or is produced as the final consequence or product and is related to one of the goals for the business or enterprise. The Outcome is a special kind of effect, one that is usually at the end of a chain of effects, i.e. an "end effect".

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

Instance Specification

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Something that happens or is produced as the final consequence or product and is related to one of the goals for the business or enterprise. The Outcome is a special kind of effect, one that is usually at the end of a chain of effects, i.e. an &quot;end effect&quot;.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extensions</strong></p><p>Instance Specification</p>
````

## PAGE 00040: Actual Resource

- page_id: `228985896`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228985896/Actual+Resource
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:29:45.138+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Role in an Organization, where the role carries the authority to undertake a function - though the Actual Organizational Resource given the role has the responsibility.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Instance Specification

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>Role in an Organization, where the role carries the authority to undertake a function - though the Actual Organizational Resource given the role has the responsibility.</p><p><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p>UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</p><p><strong>Extension</strong></p><p>UML Instance Specification </p><p><br /></p>
````

An Actuator is a special external system that influences the environment of the system under development.

Example

- Heater assembly.
- Central locking system of a car.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968067 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00050: Adding a basic flow step from other use cases

- page_id: `243968067`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243968067/Adding+a+basic+flow+step+from+other+use+cases
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:49.234+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Use Case > Use Case Scenario sketch
- labels: ['included-use-case', 'extending-use-case']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

879372979

879372981

879372980

Make sure the selected Use Case has included or extending use cases.

To add a basic flow step from other use cases

1. Open the Use Case Specification window and click the Use Case Scenario Sketch property group.
2. Select the basic flow step where you want to add steps from another Use Case.
3. Click the Add steps from other use cases button [ATTACHMENT filename='add_steps_from_included_use_cases.png'] . The shortcut menu opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='add_new_steps_from_other_use_case.png']
4. Do one of the following:

- 
  - Click Add New Steps From Included Use Case or press ALT+I to add steps from the included Use Case.
  - Click Add New Steps From Extending Use Case or press ALT+E to add steps from the extending Use Case.

Depending on your choice, the **Select Included Use Case**or **Select Extending Use Case**dialog opens.

5. In the opened dialog, select the use case(s) from which you want to add steps to the selected step of the basic flow. Click **OK**after you have selected a Use Case.

- Steps from included use cases are added to the basic flow as substeps of the newly created basic flow step.
- Steps from extending use cases with no extension points use cases are added to the basic flow as substeps of the selected basic flow step.
- Steps from extending use cases with extension points are added as steps of an alternative flow to the selected basic flow step. The extension point is considered the alternative condition.

See the examples in the following figures.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Steps added from included or extending use cases are read-only. You are not allowed to modify them in the current scenario.

You can modify added steps only in use cases from which these steps are added.

879372978

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The shortcut menu opens.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_new_steps_from_other_use_case.png" /></ac:image></li><li><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Do one of the following:</span></li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Click </span><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Add New Steps From Included Use Case </strong><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">or press ALT+I to add steps from the included Use Case.</span></li><li><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Click </span><strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Add New Steps From Extending Use Case </strong><span style="line-height: 1.4285715;">or press ALT+E to add steps from the extending Use Case.</span></li></ul></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Depending on your choice, the <strong>Select Included Use Case </strong>or <strong>Select Extending Use Case </strong>dialog opens.</p><p>    5.   In the opened dialog, select the use case(s) from which you want to add steps to the selected step of the basic flow. Click <strong>OK </strong>after you have selected a Use Case.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bf14501a-708f-4964-b3e9-e09e1e1e2e0d"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Steps from included use cases are added to the basic flow as substeps of the newly created basic flow step.</li><li>Steps from extending use cases with no extension points use cases are added to the basic flow as substeps of the selected basic flow step.</li><li>Steps from extending use cases with extension points are added as steps of an alternative flow to the selected basic flow step. The extension point is considered the alternative condition.</li></ul><p> See the examples in the following figures.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="included_extended_use_cases.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4d92a3c0-278e-4a39-a740-8f305ca2b987"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Steps added from included or extending use cases are read-only. You are not allowed to modify them in the current scenario.</p><p>You can modify added steps only in use cases from which these steps are added.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cc89b5b3-7c3d-45f7-b1de-7e90a5a23abf"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">879372978</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Transition" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
