<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967670 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00054: Alternative Fragment

- page_id: `243967670`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967670/Alternative+Fragment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:38.672+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Fragment
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Our modeling tool represents twelve kinds of fragments: Alternatives, Loop, Option, Parallel, Break, Negative, Critical Region, Consider, Ignore, Weak Sequencing, Strict Sequencing, and Assertion.

The alternative fragment models *if...then...else* constructions.

To draw an Alternative Fragment

- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MT'] pallet, select the Alternatives element to draw.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Fragment' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Combined Fragment' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Our modeling tool represents twelve kinds of fragments: Alternatives, Loop, Option, Parallel, Break, Negative, Critical Region, Consider, Ignore, Weak Sequencing, Strict Sequencing, and Assertion.</p><p>The alternative fragment models <em>if...then...else</em> constructions. </p><p><br /></p><p>To draw an Alternative Fragment</p><hr /><ul><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link> pallet, select the Alternatives element to draw. </li></ul><p><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-21 at 5.54.15 PM.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></p><p><span><br /></span></p><p><span><br /></span></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff1aef2b-4429-4784-bb8f-aa49ccc1bf16"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Fragment" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Combined Fragment" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967503 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00055: Anchor

- page_id: `243967503`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967503/Anchor
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:33.504+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

907355913

907355917

907355915

Use an Anchor to relate an element to a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Note' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Comment' space='']. By default, a newly created Anchor links an element with a note.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of an Anchor relating an Action to a Note.

You can format the anchor symbol properties in the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MT']** dialog.

To draw an anchor from a symbol

1. On the diagram's pane, select a symbol: class, signal, data type, etc.
2. In the smart manipulator toolbar appearing on the side of the symbol, click on the Anchor button.
3. If you drag the shadowed Anchor anywhere on the diagram pane, a new note appears anchored to the symbol.
4. Or you can click the note or comment to which you want to anchor the element .
5. The Anchor is created for the symbol.

To draw an anchor from the diagram pane

1. Click on the diagram's p ane to create a new anchored note.
2. Drag it anywhere on the diagram pane and see a new anchored note appear.
3. Now, a new note is anchored to your diagram pane.

To change an anchor path style

1. On the diagram pane, select an anchor and open its shortcut menu.
2. Select **Symbol Properties**.
3. Select the Path Style property, then choose one of the following path styles: Rectilinear , Oblique , or Bezier . [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-21 at 6.34.24 PM.png']

907355912

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=228985909 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00057: Architectural Description

- page_id: `228985909`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228985909/Architectural+Description
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:29:48.598+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An Architecture Description is a work product used to express the Architecture of some System Of Interest.

It provides executive-level summary information about the architecture description in a consistent form to allow quick reference and comparison between architecture descriptions -- It includes assumptions, constraints, and limitations that may affect high-level decisions relating to an architecture-based work program.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Package

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">An Architecture Description is a work product used to express the Architecture of some System Of Interest.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">It provides executive-level summary information about the architecture description in a consistent form to allow quick reference and comparison between architecture descriptions -- It includes assumptions, constraints, and limitations that may affect high-level decisions relating to an architecture-based work program. </p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong><span class="fontstyle0">Architecture Framework</span></strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UML Package</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967508 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00061: Artifact

- page_id: `243967508`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967508/Artifact
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:33.599+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

907414243

907414245

907414244

An Artifactrepresents a physical piece of information that is used or produced by a software development process. Examples of artifacts include models, source files, scripts, and binary executable files. An artifact may constitute the implementation of a deployable component.

To describe how an artifact is created, the model draws a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manifestations' space=''] relationship, between the artifact and the elements used in its construction. All artifact manifestations are listed in the Artifact [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'], Manifestations pane. In this pane you may add new manifestations.

To be used, an artifact must be deployed, meaning, it must be placed in a location where it may be stored and used. The target of the deployment may be a node, such as an execution environment or a device, a property, or an instance specification. The relationship between the location and the artifact is defined by the deployment relationship.

907414242

Examples of artifacts include models, source files, scripts, and binary executable files. An artifact may constitute the implementation of a deployable component. </span></p><p>To describe how an artifact is created, the model draws a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Manifestations" /></ac:link> relationship, between the artifact and the elements used in its construction.  All artifact manifestations are listed in the <span class="hcp1">Artifact <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></span>, <span class="hcp1">Manifestations</span> pane. In this pane you may add new manifestations.</p><p>To be used, an artifact must be deployed, meaning, it must be placed in a location where it may be stored and used. The target of the deployment may be a node, such as an execution environment or a device, a property, or an instance specification. The relationship between the location and the artifact is defined by the <a>deployment</a> relationship.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="44de9bea-20bd-4578-9956-35688578bd88"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">907414242</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Editing property values" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Editing Property Values]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Manifestations" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /></ac:link></li><li class="hcp3"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Component diagram" /></ac:link></li><li class="hcp3"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Deployment diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967531 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00064: Assigning Behavior diagrams automatically

- page_id: `243967531`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967531/Assigning+Behavior+diagrams+automatically
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:44:11.771+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Behavior
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When assigning a behavior of an Activity, Interaction, Protocol State machine, or State Machine type for a state or transition, the corresponding behavior diagram is created automatically:

- an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT'] for an Activity behavior
- a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MT'] for Interaction behavior
- a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol State Machine diagram' space='MT'] for Protocol State Machine behavior
- a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MT'] for State Machine behavior

Double-click the assigned behavior on a state shape to open the diagram. You can also open the behavior diagram from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Browser' space='MT'].

To disable an automatic diagram creating

1. On the **Options** menu, click **Environment**. The Environment Options dialog opens.
2. In General options, Editing category, set the Create behavior diagram when the behavior type is selected for transition effect or state activities value to false (the default value is true ).

You can specify behavior properties in the Specification window. In the same window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window.

#### TIP: More information

More information

For more information about the Specification window usage, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'].

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assigning Behaviors' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']

#### PANEL: Related diagrams

Related diagrams

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol State Machine diagram' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">When assigning a behavior of an Activity, Interaction, Protocol State machine, or State Machine type for a state or transition, the corresponding behavior diagram is created automatically:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link> for an Activity behavior</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link> for Interaction behavior</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Protocol State Machine diagram" /></ac:link> for Protocol State Machine behavior</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /></ac:link> for State Machine behavior</span></li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Double-click the assigned behavior on a state shape to open the diagram. You can also open the behavior diagram from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Browser" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Browser]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To disable an automatic diagram creating</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">On the <strong>Options</strong> menu, click <strong>Environment</strong>. The Environment Options dialog opens.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">In </span><strong style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">General</strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> options, </span><strong style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Editing</strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> category, set the </span><strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Create behavior diagram when the behavior type is selected for transition effect or state activities</span></strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> value to </span><em style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">false</em><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> (the default value is </span><em style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">true</em><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">).</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">You can specify behavior properties in the Specification window. In the same window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967981 space=MED version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00065: Assigning behavior to state

- page_id: `243967981`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967981/Assigning+behavior+to+state
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:47.109+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > State Machine > State
- labels: ['state-behavior']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can define a behavior to be executed correspondingly to the listed events while being in the State whenever the State is entered and exited. 
 
 To assign a behavior to a State by dragging

1. In the Model Browser, select a behavior type element (Activity, Function Behavior, Interaction, Opaque Behavior, State Machine, or Protocol State Machine) or a Signal element.
2. Drag it to the selected State on the diagram. The list of activities appears. [ATTACHMENT filename='worddav9d9404b7bf87a7312cc856184577c1b0.png']

- Click a required activity. 
The behavior is assigned to the selected State's activity. - If you drag a behavior type element (Activity, Function Behavior, Interaction, Opaque Behavior, State Machine, or Protocol State Machine), the Activity diagram under the Activity element with a Call Behavior Action element are created automatically.- If you drag a Signal element, the Activity diagram under the Activity element with a Send Signal Action element are created automatically.

To assign a behavior to a State by using the transition's Specification window

1. Open the State's Specification window.
2. In the **Entry,****Do****Activity**, or **Exit** category, click the **Behavior****Type** property value cell. The list of available behavior types opens. Select the behavior type from the list. If you do not see the **Behavior Type** property, click the **+** button near the **Entry**, **Do****Activity**, or **Exit** category to expand its content.
3. According to the selected behavior type, the **Behavior Element** value is defined automatically. Additional properties **Name** and **Owned Diagram** appears. Specify these properties if it is needed.
4. Click the **Close** button after you have defined desired properties. [ATTACHMENT filename='worddav4a14ed756a0d12b3f922001d3f22deb4.png']

If a behavior of an Activity, Interaction, Protocol State Machine, or State Machine type is assigned to an Entry, Exit, or Do activity, a diagram for that behavior is created automatically.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MT']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968033 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00066: Assigning Behavior Type

- page_id: `243968033`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243968033/Assigning+Behavior+Type
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:48.498+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Transition
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A behavior type is a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space='']that is performed when the transition fires.

To assign a behavior type, do one of the following

- In the Containment tree, select a behavior type element and drag it to the selected transition on the diagram.
- In the transition’s [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'] > **Effect** category, click the **Behavior Type** property value cell and select the behavior from the list. According to the selected behavior, additional corresponding properties will appear in the **Effect** category. Specify desired properties.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- To quickly trace the effect of the transition, from the transition shortcut menu, select Go To and then the effect element.
- If a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space=''] of an activity, interaction, protocol state machine, or state machine type is assigned to a transition, a diagram for that behavior is created automatically.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='']

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MT']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967528 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00067: Assigning Behaviors

- page_id: `243967528`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967528/Assigning+Behaviors
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:34.530+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Behavior
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See references on how to assign the behavior for the following elements:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Action' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space='']

See the following procedures, on how to assign the behavior for the rest of the elements,

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''],
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Component' space=''],
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space=''],
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Node' space=''],
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actor' space=''],
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space=''],
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaboration' space=''],
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction' space=''],
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MT'],
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT'],
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opaque Behavior' space=''], and
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space=''].

To create and assign a behavior

There are two ways to create and assign behaviors, so do one of the following:

- Create a behavior in the element's Specification Window.
  1. Open the element's Specification Window and click the **Behaviors** property group. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  2. Click the **Create** button and then from the menu, select the behavior type. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  3. In the behavior's Specification Window, specify the behavior and click **Close**.
- Right-click the element in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MT'] , on its shortcut menu, click Create Diagram and then click to create Activity , Sequence , State Machine or other behavior diagram.

#### TIP: To create an operation for the behavior

To create an operation for the behavior

1. In the element's Specification Window, click Behaviors property group.
2. Click the behavior, then click the Create Operation button.

If the Specification property is defined already, then the**Create Operation**button is unavailable.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assigning Behavior diagrams automatically' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']

#### PANEL: Related diagrams

Related diagrams

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol State Machine diagram' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968030 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00068: Assigning Event Type

- page_id: `243968030`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243968030/Assigning+Event+Type
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:44:11.909+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Transition
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1769354984

1769354987

1769354986

You need to assign an event to the transition in order to specify an occurrence of an event which can trigger a state transition.

You can assign an event type to a transition in the following ways:

- Using the transition’s [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'] .
- By typing an appropriate command straight on the selected transition path.

To assign an event type in the Transition Specification window

1. Open the transition’s Specification window.
2. In the **Trigger** category, select the **Event Type** property value cell. The list of available event types opens.
3. Select the event type from the list. According to the selected event type, Event Element, and Trigger property values are defined and additional corresponding properties appears in the Trigger category (see the following figure).
4. Click the **Close** button after you have defined desired properties.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To assign an event type straight on a transition path

1. Select a transition path on the diagram pane.
2. Type the command of the appropriate event type. The event type is assigned to the transition. In the following figure, see an example of the signal event type assignment.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

See the list with the event type and its command syntax in the following table.

| Name | Command syntax |
| --- | --- |
| Any Receive Event | all |
| Call Event | <operation name ()>NOTE: Before assigning operation straight on the transition path the Call Event together with this operation should be already created in the same State Machine as the transition. |
| Change Event | when (<expression>) |
| Signal Event | <signal name> |
| Time Event | after (<time>) - for an event occurring at a relative timeat (<time>) - for an event occurring at an absolute time |

The event type assigning by typing the command straight on the diagram pane is also available for a transition to self in the State Machine diagram, as well as for a protocol transition and protocol transition to self in the Protocol State Machine diagram.

1769354980

**Related pages**

- Assigning Behavior Type
- Model Elements
- Transition
- State Machine diagram

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967777 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00069: Assigning Operations to Messages

- page_id: `243967777`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967777/Assigning+Operations+to+Messages
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:41.190+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Message
- labels: ['signature', 'create-operation']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

372250885

372250887

372250886

INLINE

You can assign [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space='']only to Call (synchronous and asynchronous) Messages. Only one Operation can be assigned to a Message.

To assign an Operation to a Message

- Assign an Operation to a Message in the Message [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']: 
 
1. Draw a call Message between two [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Lifeline' space='']or select an existing Message on a diagram pane. 
2. Open the Message Specification window. 
3. Click the **Signature (operation)** property value cell and then select an Operation in the drop-down list. If the **Signature (operation)**drop-down list is empty, check if the Lifeline the current Message is drawn to has a type assigned, and if this type has a specified Operation.In the **Signature (operation)**drop-down list, both Operations and signals of your project are listed. Make sure you have selected an Operation. If you assign a signal to a call Message, the Message will automatically be converted to a send Message.

- Type an Operation name right on the Message on the diagram pane.
- On the diagram, right-click the Message and on the shortcut menu, click Operation . Then click to select the Operation that you want to apply on the element, or click the Create Operation button.

If an Operation you are assigning to a Message has default parameter values, these default parameter values are assigned to the Message arguments. However, if the types of the Operation parameter and the Message argument do not match, the default parameter value is assigned to the Message argument.

Names of operation parameters are displayed in message signature by default. However, you can change this behavior by opening the **Symbol Properties** dialog (right-click a Message and select **Symbol Properties**) and setting the **Show Attribute/Parameter Names** property to **true** to display them or **false** to hide them.

To create a new Operation for a Message

You can create a new Operation only if the Lifeline the Message is drawn to has a type assigned.

1. Draw a Call Message between two Lifelines or select an existing Message on a diagram pane.
2. Do one of the following:

- 
  - Click the Create New Operation According to This Message button (a small green circle at the end of the Message name) and define the Operation property values in the Operation Specification window.
  - On the Message shortcut menu, click Operation and then click the Create Operation button. Select the owner and click OK . In the Operation Specification window, define the Operation and click Close .
  - Type an Operation name right on a Message on the diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

372250883

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967785 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00070: Assigning Signals to Messages

- page_id: `243967785`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967785/Assigning+Signals+to+Messages
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:41.378+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Message
- labels: ['signature']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

372369698

372369700

372369699

INLINE

You can assign a Signal only to Send or Call (synchronous or asynchronous) Messages. Only one Signal can be assigned to a Message.

If a Signal is assigned to a Call Message, the Message is automatically converted to a Send Message.

To assign a Signal to a Message by dragging

1. Select a Signal in the Containment tree.
2. Drag the selected Signal to a desired Message.

To assign a Signal to a Message by typing Message name

- Click the Message to type the Message name on the diagram. A Signal is automatically created with the same name as Message name.

To assign a Signal to a Message from the Message Specification window

1. Draw a Send Message between two Lifelines or select an existing one on a diagram pane.
2. Open the Message Specification window.
3. Click the Signature (signal) property value cell and then select a Signal in the drop-down list.

Names of signal attributes are displayed in the message signature by default. However, you can change this behavior by opening the **Symbol Properties** dialog (right-click a Message and select **Symbol Properties**) and setting the **Show Attribute/Parameter Names** property to **true** to display them or **false** to hide them.

372369697

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967511 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00071: Association

- page_id: `243967511`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967511/Association
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:33.750+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

973915845

INLINE

100.00002%

973915847

INLINE

973915846

INLINE

In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MT'], an Association represents the semantic relationship between two or more classifiers which specifies connections between their instances. An Association relationship is the most general of all relationships and the most semantically weak.

In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MT'], an Association represents the participation of an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actor' space='']in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space=''], for example, when instances of an Actor and instances of a Use Case communicate with each other. This is the only relationship between Actors and Use Cases. Sometimes an association relationship is called communication Association.

An Association is drawn as a solid path connecting two classifier symbols.

The communication path is a subclass of an Association. It specifies the relationship between nodes by defining the number of nodes that may be connected (multiplicity), and the nature of the connection, via the name of the path or a stereotype.

907535765

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="09b3cb0d-988a-4c8d-9c7f-af5310c424b9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">973915845</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d9cca555-8ee8-45ae-9929-437275e38eb9"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">100.00002%</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="id">973915847</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7fdf100b-a456-4db2-ac2d-0d33b219e525"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">973915846</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Class diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, an Association represents the semantic relationship between two or more classifiers which specifies connections between their instances. An Association relationship is the most general of all relationships and the most semantically weak.</p><p>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Use Case diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, an Association represents the participation of an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Actor ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>in a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Use Case" /></ac:link>, for example, when instances of an Actor and instances of a Use Case communicate with each other. This is the only relationship between Actors and Use Cases. Sometimes an association relationship is called communication Association.</p><p>An Association is drawn as a solid path connecting two classifier symbols.</p><p>The communication path is a subclass of an Association. It specifies the relationship between nodes by defining the number of nodes that may be connected (multiplicity), and the nature of the connection, via the name of the path or a stereotype.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8951d3cb-4742-4c12-a730-a0e8de046cf3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">907535765</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="029c4436-0cb7-4f63-8ae4-e5a9c4c8facb" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Class diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Use Case" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=23467613 space=MED version=10 -->
## PAGE 00072: Association Block

- page_id: `23467613`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/23467613/Association+Block
- version_number: 10
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:46:26.357+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > SysML v1 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Association Block can own properties and connectors, like any other [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='']. Each instance of an Association Block can link together instances of the end classifiers of the association. To refer to linked objects and values of an instance of an Association Block, it is necessary for the modeler to specify which [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Participant Property' space=''] of the Association Block identify the instances being linked at which end of the association. The value of a participant property on an instance (link) of the Association Block is the value or object at the end of the link corresponding to this end of the association.[How to create Association Block >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Defining+Blocks+in+Block+Definition+Diagram#DefiningBlocksinBlockDefinitionDiagram-CreatingAssociationBlock)

The Association Block can be the type of multiple other Connectors to reuse the same internal structure for all the Connectors. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Association Block as Connector type' space='MT']>]]>

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An Association Block can own properties and connectors, like any other <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Block" /></ac:link>. Each instance of an Association Block can link together instances of the end classifiers of the association. To refer to linked objects and values of an instance of an Association Block, it is necessary for the modeler to specify which <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Participant Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Participant Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the Association Block identify the instances being linked at which end of the association. The value of a participant property on an instance (link) of the Association Block is the value or object at the end of the link corresponding to this end of the association.<a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Defining+Blocks+in+Block+Definition+Diagram#DefiningBlocksinBlockDefinitionDiagram-CreatingAssociationBlock"> How to create Association Block &gt;&gt;</a></p><p>The Association Block can be the type of multiple other Connectors to reuse the same internal structure for all the Connectors. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Using Association Block as Connector type" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to use the Association Block as Connector type >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967520 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00074: Association Class

- page_id: `243967520`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967520/Association+Class
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:34.133+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Association
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: N-ary Association button

491530473

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: N-ary Association button

491530475

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: N-ary Association button

491530474

An Association Class is a model element with both [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='']and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''] properties. You can look at an Association Class as an Association that also has Class properties, or as a Class that also has Association properties. Additionally, it connects a set of classifiers and defines a set of features belonging only to the relationship and not to any of the classifiers.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of an association class.

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MT'] in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'].

To draw an association class

1. Draw two Classes.
2. Click the **Association Class** button on the diagram palette. Association Class buttonIf you do not see the **Association Class** button in the diagram palette, click the black arrow next to the **Association** button to see the list of associations. Select **Association Class** from the list.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. On the diagram pane, click the first Class shape (path source).
4. Drag the path to the second Class (path destination) and drop it there.

If you need to model a relationship among several classes, you should use the N-ary Association. The N-ary association is drawn as a big diamond with all associations attached to its points. Every involved Class can have a role name and multiplicity.

To draw an N-ary association class

1. Draw several Classes on the diagram pane.
2. Draw the N-ary Association connector shape. N-ary Association buttonIf you do not see the **N-ary Association** button in the diagram palette, click the black arrow next to the **Association** button to see the list of Associations. Select **N-ary Association** from that list. See the figure above.
3. Connect all classes to the N-ary Association using an Association relationship.

491530472

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MT']
