<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967515 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00075: Association End

- page_id: `243967515`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967515/Association+End
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:33.983+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Association
- labels: ['association-end-type', 'qualifier', 'aggregation', 'association-navigability', 'dot-notation']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

907695017

907695020

907719011

**On this page**

**3**

907695019

Association Ends are represented by properties each of which is connected to the type of the End. When a property is an Association End, the value or values are related to the instance or instances at the other End(s) of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space=''].

An Association End is the connection between lines depicting an Association and the shape.

The following properties of the association end can be specified: name, association end type, visibility, multiplicity, qualifier, aggregation kind.

##### Association end properties

###### Name

The Association End has other name - role. A role indicates a role played by the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''] in terms of an Association. The role name is placed at the Association End near the Class playing that role. The role name at the implementation level maps to the reference name of the opposite Class. Roles can have visibility (public, package, protected, and private).

###### Association End type

Changing the Association End type, changes the target of the Association or in other words the classifier to which the Association is connected.

###### Qualifier

A qualifier is an attribute or a list of attributes whose values serve to partition the set of instances associated with an instance across an Association. Qualifiers are attributes of an Association. It is represented as a small rectangle attached to the End of an Association path between the final path segment and the symbol of the classifier that it connects to. The qualifier rectangle is part of the Association path, not part of the classifier. The qualifier rectangle drags with the path segments. The qualifier is attached to the source end of the Association.

##### Editing the Association End properties

To change the Association End type, do one of the following

- On the diagram, select the Association End and move it to the other target. The Association is connected to the other target and the type of the Association End (that is, property) is changed.
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MT'] , select the property which represents the Association End and drag it onto the available classifier (the Association target). If the Association is represented on the diagram pane, the Association is redrawn (connected) to the changed target automatically.
- In the property (that is, the Association End) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'] , change the Type property value.

To define the Association End visibility

1. Open the Association End Specification window.
2. From the Visibility list, select one of the visibility type.

The Association End multiplicity describes how many entities are participating at each Association End:

- 0 – zero and only zero.
- 1 – one and only one.
- 0..1 – zero or one.
- 0..* – from zero to any positive integer.
- 1..* – from one to any positive integer.
- * – any positive integer.

To place multiplicity values in Association path ends

- Open the shortcut menu of a selected Association End and click a desired multiplicity.
- Open Association’s shortcut menu, point to one of the desired Association Ends (Role of <class name> ), and then click the desired multiplicity value.
- Open the Association Specification window and, from the Multiplicity list, select or type the multiplicity value for the desired Association End.
- Perform the following steps:

1. 
  1. Open the Association End Specification window.
  2. In the Multiplicity property value cell, type or select from the list a multiplicity value.

- Edit directly on the diagram pane:

1. 
  1. Select multiplicity area and press F2 to switch it to edit mode.
  2. Press Ctrl + Spacebar or Ctrl + Backspace to see available suggestions.

Choose one of the suggestions or type the multiplicity value.

To define an Association End name

- Open the shortcut menu of a selected Association End and click Edit Name . The Association End is marked for editing. Type or edit the name directly on the diagram pane.
- Open the Association’s shortcut menu, point to one of a desired Association End ( Role of *<class name>* ), and then click Edit Name . The Association End is marked for editing. Type or edit the name directly on the diagram pane.
- Perform the following steps:

1. 
  1. Open the selected Association End Specification window.
  2. Type the Association End name in the Name property value cell.

##### Defining Association End qualifiers

A qualifier is an attribute or a list of attributes whose values serve to partition the set of instances associated with an instance across an Association. Qualifiers are attributes of an Association. It is represented as a small rectangle attached to the end of an Association path between the final path segment and the symbol of the classifier that it connects to. The qualifier rectangle is part of the Association path, not part of the classifier. The qualifier rectangle drags with the path segments. The qualifier is attached to the source end of the Association.

To add, edit, or remove a qualifier to/from an Association End

1. Open the Association End Specification window.
2. In the property group list, click the Qualifiers group and do one of the following:
  - To add a new qualifier, click the Create button. In the opened qualifier Specification window, define qualifier properties and click Back to return to the Association End Specification window or click Close to exit the Specification window.
  - To edit a qualifier, select a qualifier and edit its properties in the Association End Specification window or click the Open Specification button [IMAGE alt='' src=''] at the end of a qualifier row to edit qualifier properties in the qualifier Specification window. Existing qualifiers are also displayed in the property group list under the **Qualifiers** group. Click the selected qualifier to open its Specification window, and define qualifier properties there.
  - To remove a qualifier, select a qualifier and click Delete .
3. After you have finished working with qualifiers, click Close to exit the Specification window.

##### Defining Association End aggregation

The shared or composite aggregation kind can be assigned to the Association End. A composite aggregation is represented as a filled diamond. A shared aggregation is represented as a hollow diamond.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To assign an aggregation kind to the Association End

- Open Association’s shortcut menu, point to one of the desired Association Ends ( Role of *<class name>* ), and then select one of the following:
  - None . No aggregation is assigned to the selected Association End.
  - Shared .
  - Composite .
- On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MT'] , click the Composition or Aggregation button and draw an appropriate path on the diagram.
- Right click the Association path end and select Shared or Composite command from the shortcut menu.
- Perform the following steps:

1. 
  1. Open the selected Association or Association End Specification window.
  2. For the selected Association End, click **Aggregation** property value cell to select an aggregation kind (**None**, **Shared**, or **Composite**). By default, the **Aggregation** property is in the **All** properties display mode.

##### Showing Association End properties

If two classes are linked with an Association path, both Classes have an attribute of an opposite Class type. This property can be displayed on the Class shape as well as an Association link.

To show Association Ends as attributes on linked Class shapes

1. From the Class shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties or press Alt+Enter. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MT']**Symbol Properties** dialog opens.
2. In the Attribute category, click the Show Association End as Attributes property value cell.
3. Click one of the following display modes:

- 
  - All . Properties and Association paths will be displayed on the Class shape.
  - Without Association Symbol . If an Association symbol is deleted, the property will be displayed on the Class shape.
  - Do Not Show . Neither property, nor Association path will be displayed on the Class shape.

An Association End is defined as a property. It has attribute properties defined in the Specification window.

To show Association Ends as Ports on the shape

1. Select the shape on which you want to display Association Ends as Ports and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MT']**Symbol Properties** dialog :
  - Right-click a shape to open the shortcut menu, and click Symbol Properties .
  - Press Alt+Enter.
2. In the Symbol Properties dialog, at the right-top corner, click to expand the menu and select the All mode.
3. Under the **Ports** category, click the **Show Association Ends as Ports** property cell and select *All*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Converting Association End properties

To convert an Association role to an attribute

1. On a diagram pane, select an Association with a role.
2. From its shortcut menu, select the Refactor > Convert To > Attribute(s) .

The Association is converted to the attribute.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can specify Association End properties in the Association End Specification window. In the same window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window.

To convert Association Ends to Ports

1. Right- click an Association End(s).
2. On the shortcut menu, click Refactor > Convert To > Port .

##### Association navigability

The Association navigability indicates whether it is possible to traverse an Association within an expression of a classifier to obtain the object or a set of objects associated with instances. The navigability is shown as an arrow that can be attached to the end of the path to indicate that the navigation is supported toward the classifier attached to the arrow.

By default, an Association is navigable on both sides and its navigability is not visible.

A role indicates the role played by the Class in terms of an Association. The role name is placed at the Association End, near the Class playing that role. The role name at the implementation level maps to the reference name of the opposite Class. Roles can have visibility (public, package, protected, and private).

To change the Association navigability, do one of the following

- Open the Association End Specification window, turn on the Expert property display mode, and do one of the following:
  - Set the Navigable property value to true to mark the Association End as navigable.
  - Set the Navigable property value to false to mark the Association End as not navigable.
- From the Association shortcut menu, select Role of *<class name>* and then do one of the following:
  - Select Navigable to mark the Association End as navigable.
  - Click to clear Navigable to mark the Association End as not navigable.
- Open the Association End shortcut menu and do one of the following:
  - Select Navigable to mark the Association End as navigable.
  - Click to clear Navigable to mark the Association End as not navigable.

To display the Association navigability

- From both Association Ends shortcut menu, select Show Navigability .

In the following figure, the Association is navigable on both sides and its navigability is visible.

##### Advancing actions: navigable owned Association Ends

The navigability describes the need for an object to access another object by navigating across the link. The Association End can be owned by a classifier or an Association. The Association End owned by a classifier can be decorated with the dot. The absence of the dot signifies the ownership by the Association.

The dot notation is not enabled by default. Please pay attention to this before making decisions about the Association End ownership just from the model representation on a diagram.

To enable a dot notation

1. On the Options menu, click Project . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='MT']**Project Options** dialog opens.
2. In the General options list, select General and set the Enable Dot Notation for Associations value to true . [ATTACHMENT filename='dot_notation_on_association_end.png']

According to the UML 2.4.1 specification, the Association Ends owned by Classes and Associations is navigable. This improved functionality allows a proper management of the *navigableOwnedEnd* property for Associations:

- The ownership of an Association End can be changed manually.
- The navigability of Association Ends owned by the Associations can be changed keeping the ownership.

To change the ownership of an Association End, do one of the following

- On the Association End shortcut menu, click Owned By and then select the desired owner.
- In the Association End Specification window, click the Owned By property value cell and then select the desired owner from the list.
- From the Association shortcut menu, select the role of the desired Association End, click Owned By and then select the desired owner.
- In the Association Specification window, click the Owned By property value cell of the role with the desired Association End and then select the desired owner from the list.

907695015

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="199eb445-2a67-4d19-ba33-9128b3263d8c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">907695017</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="92b66b6c-c48f-4cf6-b1a6-77e6e5417c7b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">907695020</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="170395d4-2fed-40c9-80bc-7fd8b3dfba4d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">907719011</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c8c27635-47e9-4c6d-aad2-0da08dc1a4e9"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ee977768-041a-4e18-a58c-52dd1bde52c0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">907695019</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>Association Ends are represented by properties each of which is connected to the type of the End. When a property is an Association End, the value or values are related to the instance or instances at the other End(s) of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Association" /></ac:link>.</p><p>An Association End is the connection between lines depicting an Association and the shape.</p><p>The following properties of the association end can be specified: name, association end type, visibility, multiplicity, qualifier, aggregation kind.</p><h3>Association end properties</h3><h4>Name</h4><p><span>The Association End has other name - role. A role indicates a role played by the</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link> <span>in terms of an Association. The role name is placed at the Association End near the Class playing that role. The role name at the implementation level maps to the reference name of the opposite Class. Roles can have visibility (public, package, protected, and private).</span> <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span></p><h4>Association End type </h4><p>Changing the Association End type, changes the target of the Association or in other words the classifier to which the Association is connected.<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span></p><h4>Qualifier </h4><p>A qualifier is an attribute or a list of attributes whose values serve to partition the set of instances associated with an instance across an Association. Qualifiers are attributes of an Association. It is represented as a small rectangle attached to the End of an Association path between the final path segment and the symbol of the classifier that it connects to. The qualifier rectangle is part of the Association path, not part of the classifier. The qualifier rectangle drags with the path segments. The qualifier is attached to the source end of the Association.</p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Editing the Association End properties</span></h3><p><br /></p><p>To change the Association End type, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>On the diagram, select the Association End and move it to the other target. The Association is connected to the other target and the type of the Association End (that is, property) is changed.</li><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, select the property which represents the Association End and drag it onto the available classifier (the Association target). If the Association is represented on the diagram pane, the Association is redrawn (connected) to the changed target automatically.</li><li>In the property (that is, the Association End) <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, change the <strong>Type</strong> property value.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To define the Association End visibility</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Association End Specification window.</li><li>From the <strong>Visibility</strong> list, select one of the visibility type.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The Association End multiplicity describes how many entities are participating at each Association End:</p><ul><li>0 – zero and only zero.</li><li>1 – one and only one.</li><li>0..1 – zero or one.</li><li>0..* – from zero to any positive integer.</li><li>1..* – from one to any positive integer.</li><li>* – any positive integer.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To place multiplicity values in Association path ends</p><hr /><ul><li>Open the shortcut menu of a selected Association End and click a desired multiplicity.</li><li>Open Association’s shortcut menu, point to one of the desired Association Ends (Role of <em>&lt;class name&gt;</em>), and then click the desired multiplicity value.</li><li>Open the Association Specification window and, from the <strong>Multiplicity</strong> list, select or type the multiplicity value for the desired Association End.</li><li>Perform the following steps:</li></ul><ol><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ol><li>Open the Association End Specification window.</li><li>In the <strong>Multiplicity</strong> property value cell, type or select from the list a multiplicity value.</li></ol></li></ol><ul><li>Edit directly on the diagram pane:</li></ul><ol><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ol><li>Select multiplicity area and press F2 to switch it to edit mode.</li><li>Press Ctrl + Spacebar or Ctrl + Backspace to see available suggestions.</li></ol></li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Choose one of the suggestions or type the multiplicity value.</p><p><br /></p><p>To define an Association End name</p><hr /><ul><li>Open the shortcut menu of a selected Association End and click <strong>Edit Name</strong>. The Association End is marked for editing. Type or edit the name directly on the diagram pane.</li><li>Open the Association’s shortcut menu, point to one of a desired Association End (<strong>Role of <em>&lt;class name&gt;</em> </strong>), and then click <strong>Edit Name</strong>. The Association End is marked for editing. Type or edit the name directly on the diagram pane.</li><li>Perform the following steps:</li></ul><ol><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ol><li>Open the selected Association End Specification window.</li><li>Type the Association End name in the <strong>Name</strong> property value cell.</li></ol></li></ol><h3 style="text-align: left;">Defining Association End qualifiers</h3><p style="text-align: left;">A qualifier is an attribute or a list of attributes whose values serve to partition the set of instances associated with an instance across an Association. Qualifiers are attributes of an Association. It is represented as a small rectangle attached to the end of an Association path between the final path segment and the symbol of the classifier that it connects to. The qualifier rectangle is part of the Association path, not part of the classifier. The qualifier rectangle drags with the path segments. The qualifier is attached to the source end of the Association.</p><p style="text-align: left;"><br /></p><p>To add, edit, or remove a qualifier to/from an Association End</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Association End Specification window.</li><li>In the property group list, click the <strong>Qualifiers</strong> group and do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>To add a new qualifier, click the <strong>Create</strong> button. In the opened qualifier Specification window, define qualifier properties and click <strong>Back</strong> to return to the Association End Specification window or click <strong>Close</strong> to exit the Specification window.</li><li><p>To edit a qualifier, select a qualifier and edit its properties in the Association End Specification window or click the Open Specification button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="open_specification_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> at the end of a qualifier row to edit qualifier properties in the qualifier Specification window.</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e93528d3-23f4-43e4-bf8a-c2549ee31bc0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Existing qualifiers are also displayed in the property group list under the <strong>Qualifiers</strong> group. Click the selected qualifier to open its Specification window, and define qualifier properties there.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>To remove a qualifier, select a qualifier and click <strong>Delete</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>After you have finished working with qualifiers, click <strong>Close</strong> to exit the Specification window.</li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Defining Association End aggregation</span></h3><p>The shared or composite aggregation kind can be assigned to the Association End. A composite aggregation is represented as a filled diamond. A shared aggregation is represented as a hollow diamond.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Representation of composite and shared aggregations" ac:alt="Representation of composite and shared aggregations"><ri:attachment ri:filename="composite_and_shared_aggregation.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To assign an aggregation kind to the Association End</p><hr /><ul><li>Open Association’s shortcut menu, point to one of the desired Association Ends (<strong>Role of <em>&lt;class name&gt;</em> </strong>), and then select one of the following:<ul><li><strong>None</strong>. No aggregation is assigned to the selected Association End.</li><li><strong>Shared</strong>.</li><li><strong>Composite</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>On the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram palette]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, click the <strong>Composition</strong> or <strong>Aggregation</strong> button and draw an appropriate path on the diagram.</li><li>Right click the Association path end and select <strong>Shared</strong> or <strong>Composite</strong> command from the shortcut menu.</li><li>Perform the following steps:</li></ul><ol><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ol><li>Open the selected Association or Association End Specification window.</li><li><p>For the selected Association End, click <strong>Aggregation</strong> property value cell to select an aggregation kind (<strong>None</strong>, <strong>Shared</strong>, or <strong>Composite</strong>).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="99f9e968-b9d2-4145-befb-9f8a039f3803"><ac:rich-text-body><p>By default, the <strong>Aggregation</strong> property is in the <strong>All</strong> properties display mode.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li></ol><h3>Showing Association End properties</h3><p>If two classes are linked with an Association path, both Classes have an attribute of an opposite Class type. This property can be displayed on the Class shape as well as an Association link.</p><p><br />To show Association Ends as attributes on linked Class shapes</p><hr /><ol><li>From the Class shortcut menu, select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> or press Alt+Enter. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Attribute</strong> category, click the <strong>Show Association End as Attributes</strong> property value cell.</li><li>Click one of the following display modes:</li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><strong>All</strong>. Properties and Association paths will be displayed on the Class shape.</li><li><strong>Without Association Symbol</strong>. If an Association symbol is deleted, the property will be displayed on the Class shape.</li><li><strong>Do Not Show</strong>. Neither property, nor Association path will be displayed on the Class shape.</li></ul></li></ul><p>An Association End is defined as a property. It has attribute properties defined in the Specification window.</p><p><br /></p><p>To show Association Ends as Ports on the shape</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the shape on which you want to display Association Ends as Ports and open its <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>:<br /><ul><li>Right-click a shape to open the shortcut menu, and click <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>.</li><li>Press Alt+Enter.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog, at the right-top corner, click to expand the menu and select the <strong>All</strong> mode.</li><li><p>Under the <strong>Ports</strong> category, click the <strong>Show Association Ends as Ports</strong> property cell and select <em>All</em>.</p></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:title="Example of displaying association end as port on shape" ac:alt="Example of displaying association end as port on shape"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_association_ends_as_ports.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Converting Association End properties</h3><p>To convert an Association role to an attribute</p><hr /><ol><li>On a diagram pane, select an Association with a role.</li><li>From its shortcut menu, select the <strong>Refactor</strong> &gt; <strong>Convert To</strong> &gt; <strong>Attribute(s)</strong>.</li></ol><p>The Association is converted to the attribute.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Example of association role (on the left side) conversion to attribute (on the right side)" ac:alt="Example of association role (on the left side) conversion to attribute (on the right side)"><ri:attachment ri:filename="converting_association.png" /></ac:image></p><p>You can specify Association End properties in the Association End Specification window. In the same window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window.</p><p><br /></p><p>To convert Association Ends to Ports</p><hr /><ol><li>Right- click an Association End(s).</li><li>On the shortcut menu, click <strong>Refactor </strong>&gt; <strong>Convert To</strong> &gt; <strong>Port</strong>.</li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Association navigability</span></h3><p>The Association navigability indicates whether it is possible to traverse an Association within an expression of a classifier to obtain the object or a set of objects associated with instances. The navigability is shown as an arrow that can be attached to the end of the path to indicate that the navigation is supported toward the classifier attached to the arrow.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="22f67761-feb4-4b13-96ec-4ed37b04c97c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>By default, an Association is navigable on both sides and its navigability is not visible.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>A role indicates the role played by the Class in terms of an Association. The role name is placed at the Association End, near the Class playing that role. The role name at the implementation level maps to the reference name of the opposite Class. Roles can have visibility (public, package, protected, and private).</p><p><br />To change the Association navigability, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Open the Association End Specification window, turn on the Expert property display mode, and do one of the following:<ul><li>Set the <strong>Navigable</strong> property value to <em>true</em> to mark the Association End as navigable.</li><li>Set the <strong>Navigable</strong> property value to <em>false</em> to mark the Association End as not navigable.</li></ul></li><li>From the Association shortcut menu, select <strong>Role of <em>&lt;class name&gt;</em> </strong> and then do one of the following:<ul><li>Select <strong>Navigable</strong> to mark the Association End as navigable.</li><li>Click to clear <strong>Navigable</strong> to mark the Association End as not navigable.</li></ul></li><li>Open the Association End shortcut menu and do one of the following:<ul><li>Select <strong>Navigable</strong> to mark the Association End as navigable.</li><li>Click to clear <strong>Navigable</strong> to mark the Association End as not navigable.</li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="181ec65e-7216-4b58-a116-fba25484f862">To display the Association navigability</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ul><li>From both Association Ends shortcut menu, select <strong>Show Navigability</strong>.</li></ul><p>In the following figure, the Association is navigable on both sides and its navigability is visible.</p><h3>Advancing actions: navigable owned Association Ends</h3><p>The navigability describes the need for an object to access another object by navigating across the link. The Association End can be owned by a classifier or an Association. The Association End owned by a classifier can be decorated with the dot. The absence of the dot signifies the ownership by the Association.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2ed26ed9-91e3-43c2-9ab1-7bfc7f5db2f4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The dot notation is not enabled by default. Please pay attention to this before making decisions about the Association End ownership just from the model representation on a diagram.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To enable a dot notation</p><hr /><ol><li>On the <strong>Options</strong> menu, click <strong>Project</strong>. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>In the <strong>General</strong> options list, select <strong>General </strong>and set the <strong>Enable Dot Notation for Associations</strong> value to <em>true</em>.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Example of dot notation" ac:alt="Example of dot notation"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dot_notation_on_association_end.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p>According to the UML 2.4.1 specification, the Association Ends owned by Classes and Associations is navigable. This improved functionality allows a proper management of the <em>navigableOwnedEnd</em> property for Associations:</p><ul><li>The ownership of an Association End can be changed manually.</li><li>The navigability of Association Ends owned by the Associations can be changed keeping the ownership.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To change the ownership of an Association End, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>On the Association End shortcut menu, click <strong>Owned By</strong> and then select the desired owner.</li><li>In the Association End Specification window, click the <strong>Owned By</strong> property value cell and then select the desired owner from the list.</li><li>From the Association shortcut menu, select the role of the desired Association End, click <strong>Owned By</strong> and then select the desired owner.</li><li>In the Association Specification window, click the <strong>Owned By</strong> property value cell of the role with the desired Association End and then select the desired owner from the list.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1945caa1-81f7-4505-9cdd-a583015268d3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">907695015</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Class diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Use Case" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967523 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00076: Association in Use Case diagrams

- page_id: `243967523`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967523/Association+in+Use+Case+diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:34.244+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Association
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Association shows the participation of an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actor' space='']in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space=''], for example, instances where an Actor and a Use Case communicate with each other. This is the only relationship between Actors and Use Cases. The association relationships are also known as communication associations.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An Association shows the participation of an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Actor ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>in a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Use Case" /></ac:link>, for example, instances where an Actor and a Use Case communicate with each other. This is the only relationship between Actors and Use Cases. The association relationships are also known as communication associations.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="association_in_use_case_diagrams.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967544 space=MED version=3 -->
## PAGE 00077: Attribute

- page_id: `243967544`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967544/Attribute
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:44:12.185+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Class
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Logitics

227180465

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Logitics

227180477

227292795

**On this Page**

- [New Attribute](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Attribute#Attribute-NewAttribute)
- [Attribute Name](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Attribute#Attribute-AttributeName)
- [Attribute Type](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Attribute#Attribute-AttributeType)
- [Additional Information About Attribute Type](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Attribute#Attribute-AdditionalInformationAboutAttributeType)
- [Attribute Visibility](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Attribute#Attribute-AttributeVisibility)
- [Attribute Multiplicity](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Attribute#Attribute-AttributeMultiplicity)
- [Attribute Changeability](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Attribute#Attribute-AttributeChangeability)
- [Convert Attribute To Association](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Attribute#Attribute-ConvertAttributeToAssociation)
- [Convert Attribute To Port](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Attribute#Attribute-ConvertAttributeToPort)

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Logitics

227180467

An attribute is a named property of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''] that describes a range of values that can be held by the instances of that Class. You can specify attribute properties in the attribute's Specification window. Specifying attribute properties include changing the name, defining the type, converting the attribute to a port, etc. In the same window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window at the bottom of the Specification window.

#### TIP: More information

More information

- For more information about the Specification window usage, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'].
- For more information about specifying property values, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MT'] .

###### Adding New Attribute

New AttributeTo create a new attribute, do one of the following:

- In the Containment tree, from the class shortcut menu, select Create Element > Property .
- In the class Specification window > Attributes , click the Create button.
- On the diagram pane, select the class and press Ctrl+Alt+A .
- On the diagram pane, select the class and click **Create Property**.
- On the diagram pane, select the class and click **Create Element** > **Property**.

###### Attribute Name

The attribute's name must be unique in the Class scope. Nevertheless, do one of the following instructions to change the attribute's name.

#### WARNING: Warning

Warning

Illegal characters for attribute names are **( ) [ ] { } ! @ # % ; ', ; : ' " ?**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of changing the attribute name from 'country' to 'Country'

Attribute NameTo change an attribute name

- Click the Attribute in the selected Class on the diagram pane or in the Browser tree. Type a new name. [ATTACHMENT filename='Country Renaming.png']
- Click on the Class block, double-click on the Attribute, and on the Attribute Specification window, change the name and click Close . [ATTACHMENT filename='Name Property country.png']

###### Attribute Type

#### NOTE: Attribute Type

Attribute Type

The attribute's type can be any of these: Class, Interface, or Primitive Class, such as *int* or *double*. You are allowed to define the attribute's type.

Attribute TypeTo define the type of an attribute

- Do one of the following:
  - Open the Specification window of an Attribute in your Class block, by right-clicking on the Attribute, and click on the cell of the Type property value. Then click the [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-08 at 10.40.42 PM.png'] button. In the opened window, select the type. Click OK .
  - On the diagram pane, right-click the Attribute, and then point to Type . In the opened list, select the type or point to New to create a new type.
  - On the Class shape, click the Attribute, and then on the smart manipulator toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-08 at 10.46.42 PM.png'] button. In the opened list, select the desired type.
  - On the Class shape, click the Attribute, and then click the Attribute the second time. After the Attribute name, type a colon ":" and then type in the name of the new attribute type. Click Enter or click anywhere on the diagram. The type is now created and assigned.

#### TIP: Colon

Colon

Type the colon ":" , press **Ctrl+Space** or **Ctrl+Backspace**, and then in the open list, select the **type**.

- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment Tree' space=''] , select an element and drag it to the Attribute on the diagram.

###### Additional Information About Attribute Type

AdditionalTo add additional information about the type of an attribute

1. Open the Attribute Specification window, by right-clicking on the Attribute.
2. In the top right corner of the window, select Expert in the Properties drop-down menu.
3. In the Type Modifier list, select a particular sign in the:
  1. & - one class has a reference to other model elements.
  2. * - one class has a pointer to other model elements.
  3. [] - one class has an array of other model elements.

###### Attribute Visibility

| Visibility Name | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Public '+' | An attribute can be accessed by any other elements. |
| Package '~' | An attribute can be accessed by elements from the same package. |
| Protected '#' | An attribute can be accessed from the inside of the selected class and classes derived from that class. |
| Private '-' | An attribute can be accessed only from inside of that class. |

#### INFO: Visibility

Visibility

The attribute visibility is shown at the attribute signature.

Attribute VisibilityTo set the attribute visibility

Do one of the following:

- Type '+', '~', '-', or '#' visibility marks just before an Attribute name directly on a diagram.
- Open the Attribute Specification window. From the Visibility drop-down list, select the desired item: public, package, protected, or private.

Attribute Scope

ScopeTo set an attribute scope

1. Open the Attribute Specification window.
2. In the top right corner of the window, select Expert in the Properties drop-down menu.
3. Select the Is Static check box to true .

###### Attribute Multiplicity

MultiplicityTo set the attribute multiplicity

1. Open the Attribute Specification window.
2. In the top right corner of the window, select Expert in the Properties drop-down menu.
3. Select or set the multiplicity value for the Multiplicity field.

###### Attribute Changeability

The attribute changeability controls the access by operations on the class on the opposite end.

| Name | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Is Read Only | When false - no restrictions on modifications.When true - the value can not be altered after the object is instantiated and its values initialized. No additional values can be added to a set. |

ChangeabilityTo set the attribute changeability

1. Open the Attribute Specification window.
2. In the top right corner of the window, select Expert in the Properties drop-down menu.
3. Set the Is Read Only check box to true .

###### Convert Attribute To Association

#### NOTE: Converting an Attribute

Converting an Attribute

Keep in mind a few things about an attribute when converting it to an association:

- the attribute has to have a type specified, and
- the attribute is not a member end of some association.

AssociationTo convert an attribute to an association

1. On a diagram pane, select a Class with an Attribute, or select an Attribute itself.
2. From the Class's or Attribute's shortcut menu, select the Refactor command, then Convert To , and Association .
3. On the diagram pane, the Attribute is represented as an association with a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Role' space='MT']**role** .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### TIP: Logitics

Logitics

You can move attributes back to the class by dragging and dropping them on the class shape.

###### Convert Attribute To Port

PortTo convert to a port

1. Right-click an Attribute or multiple Attributes.
2. On their shortcut menu, click Refactor > Convert To > Port .

227180461

**Related Pages**

- UML elements
- Class
- Class diagram
- Editing Property Values
- Specification window

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e728a9d7-e5d1-4a95-82c7-3ce48cf87500"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">227180465</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e3120b6a-5cfd-45ea-a328-6555baf1d2de"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">227180477</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9cc89824-14fa-47f2-8fdd-4d1bb49144fa"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">227292795</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this Page</strong></p><ul><li><span class="toc-item-body"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Attribute#Attribute-NewAttribute" class="toc-link">New Attribute</a></span></li><li><span class="toc-item-body"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Attribute#Attribute-AttributeName" class="toc-link">Attribute Name</a></span></li><li><span class="toc-item-body"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Attribute#Attribute-AttributeType" class="toc-link">Attribute Type</a></span></li><li><span class="toc-item-body"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Attribute#Attribute-AdditionalInformationAboutAttributeType" class="toc-link">Additional Information About Attribute Type</a></span></li><li><span class="toc-item-body"><a class="toc-link" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Attribute#Attribute-AttributeVisibility">Attribute Visibility</a></span></li><li><span class="toc-item-body"><a class="toc-link" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Attribute#Attribute-AttributeMultiplicity">Attribute Multiplicity</a></span></li><li><span class="toc-item-body"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Attribute#Attribute-AttributeChangeability" class="toc-link">Attribute Changeability </a></span></li><li><span class="toc-item-body"><a class="toc-link" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Attribute#Attribute-ConvertAttributeToAssociation">Convert Attribute To Association </a></span></li><li><span class="toc-item-body"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Attribute#Attribute-ConvertAttributeToPort" class="toc-link">Convert Attribute To Port</a></span></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="abd37a84-b219-4d12-a5fd-6279f703d5e9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">227180467</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>An attribute is a named property of a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link> that describes a range of values that can be held by the instances of that Class. You can specify attribute properties in the attribute's Specification window. Specifying attribute properties include changing the name, defining the type, converting the attribute to a port, etc. In the same window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window at the bottom of the Specification window.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b34b21a3-c6d6-4b97-aca6-15736f4ae9a8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">More information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For more information about the Specification window usage, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</span></li><li>For more information about specifying property values, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Editing property values" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Editing Property Values]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Adding New Attribute</h4><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="91667eb7-1c84-455d-a21f-8035f6e62ebe"><ac:parameter ac:name="">New Attribute</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To create a new attribute, do one of the following:</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Containment tree, from the class shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Element</strong> &gt; <strong>Property</strong>. </li><li>In the class Specification window &gt; <strong>Attributes</strong>, click the <strong>Create</strong> button. </li><li>On the diagram pane, select the class and press <strong>Ctrl+Alt+A</strong>. </li><li><span>On the diagram pane, select the class and click <strong>Create Property</strong>. </span></li><li><span>On the diagram pane, select the class and click <strong>Create Element</strong> &gt; <strong>Property</strong>. </span><br /><br /></li></ul><h4>Attribute Name</h4><p>The attribute's name must be unique in the Class scope. Nevertheless, do one of the following instructions to change the attribute's <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3eec21b7-d111-4e10-a3a4-76600c400aeb">name</ac:inline-comment-marker>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="47579500-6ed4-4832-9068-d4ae19aa712e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Illegal characters for attribute names are <strong>( ) [ ] { } ! @ # % ; ', ; : ' &quot; ?</strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="country to Country.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of changing the attribute name from 'country' to 'Country' </h6><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="87c3e2af-5f82-418c-9146-7aca171c1a4e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Attribute Name</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To change an attribute name</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the Attribute in the selected Class on the diagram pane or in the <strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="42409b89-8b90-4a38-9458-ba92efc57bbb">Browser</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="42409b89-8b90-4a38-9458-ba92efc57bbb"> tree.</ac:inline-comment-marker> Type a new name.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Country Renaming.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Click on the Class block, double-click on the Attribute, and on the <strong>Attribute </strong>Specification window, change the name and click <strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e04ee6ad-da75-43d1-8729-4cf7d9dfb4d9">Close</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Name Property country.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><p><br /></p><h4>Attribute Type</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2fe2cd07-c1eb-45aa-adf2-f24b99845aa9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Attribute Type</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The attribute's type can be any of these: Class, Interface, or Primitive Class, such as <em>int</em> or <em>double</em>. You are allowed to define the attribute's type.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f8de3cde-7d4d-4a83-9d7d-18382e16b968"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Attribute Type</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To define the type of an attribute</p><hr /><ul><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Open the Specification window of an Attribute in your Class block, by right-clicking on the Attribute, and click on the cell of the <strong>Type</strong> property value. Then click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-08 at 10.40.42 PM.png" /></ac:image> button. In the opened window, select the type. Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>On the diagram pane, right-click the Attribute, and then point to <strong>Type</strong>. In the opened list, select the type or point to <strong>New</strong> to create a new type.</li><li>On the Class shape, click the Attribute, and then on the smart manipulator toolbar, click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-08 at 10.46.42 PM.png" /></ac:image> button. In the opened list, select the desired type.</li><li>On the Class shape, click the Attribute, and then click the Attribute the second time. After the Attribute name, type a colon &quot;:&quot; and then type in the name of the new attribute type. Click <strong>Enter </strong>or click anywhere on the diagram. The type is now created and assigned.</li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05831661-3126-49e0-87ac-636861007ae9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Colon</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Type the colon &quot;:&quot; , press <strong>Ctrl+Space</strong> or <strong>Ctrl+Backspace</strong>, and then in the open list, select the <strong>type</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li class="auto-cursor-target">In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment Tree" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, select an element and drag it to the Attribute on the diagram.</li></ul><p><br /></p><h4>Additional Information About Attribute Type</h4><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="abaa5d7a-11e8-4501-adb5-c790c832d075"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Additional</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add additional information about the type of an attribute</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Attribute</strong> Specification window, by right-clicking on the Attribute. </li><li>In the top right corner of the window, select <strong>Expert</strong> in the <strong>Properties</strong> drop-down menu.</li><li>In the <strong>Type Modifier</strong> list, select a particular sign in the:<ol><li>&amp; - one class has a reference to other model elements.</li><li>* - one class has a pointer to other model elements.</li><li>[] - one class has an array of other model elements.</li></ol></li></ol><p><br /></p><h4>Attribute Visibility</h4><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 100.0%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 14.344385%;" /><col style="width: 85.696686%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Visibility Name</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Public '+'</strong></td><td>An attribute can be accessed by any other elements.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Package '~'</strong></td><td>An attribute can be accessed by elements from the same package.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Protected '#'</strong></td><td>An attribute can be accessed from the inside of the selected class and classes derived from that class.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Private '-'</strong></td><td>An attribute can be accessed only from inside of that class.</td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2a7f8f46-9e86-49c4-9b5c-df80ad95289a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Visibility</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The attribute visibility is shown at the attribute signature.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8f40674c-1c36-4dbe-b149-9ca84b1e3ea0"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Attribute Visibility</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To set the attribute visibility</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Type '+', '~', '-', or '#' visibility marks just before an Attribute name directly on a diagram.</li><li>Open the Attribute Specification window. From the <strong>Visibility</strong> drop-down list, select the desired item: public, package, protected, or private.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p><span style="font-weight: bold;">Attribute Scope</span></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="32ad5469-542d-40f7-aaf9-067d91cf0e52"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Scope</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To set an attribute scope</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Attribute Specification window.</li><li>In the top right corner of the window, select <strong>Expert</strong> in the <strong>Properties</strong> drop-down menu.</li><li>Select the <strong>Is Static</strong> check box to <em>true</em>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><h4>Attribute Multiplicity</h4><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="20920863-6e0b-4ec7-bb50-4e792eb0306a"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Multiplicity </ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To set the attribute multiplicity</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Attribute Specification window.</li><li>In the top right corner of the window, select <strong>Expert</strong> in the <strong>Properties</strong> drop-down menu.</li><li>Select or set the multiplicity value for the <strong>Multiplicity</strong> field.</li></ol><p><br /></p><h4>Attribute Changeability </h4><p>The attribute changeability controls the access by operations on the class on the opposite end.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Name</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Is Read Only</strong></td><td><p>When <em>false</em> - no restrictions on modifications.</p><p>When <em>true</em> - the value can not be altered after the object is instantiated and its values initialized. No additional values can be added to a set.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0380cf81-bd94-4c71-b146-ab19f222a2f0"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Changeability </ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To set the attribute changeability </p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Attribute Specification window.</li><li>In the top right corner of the window, select <strong>Expert</strong> in the <strong>Properties</strong> drop-down menu.</li><li>Set the <strong>Is Read Only</strong> check box to <em>true</em>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><h4>Convert Attribute To Association </h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2655b20d-27e2-4495-a139-92cb3e94b7ad"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Converting an Attribute</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Keep in mind a few things about an attribute when converting it to an association:</p><ul><li>the attribute has to have a <strong>type</strong> specified, and</li><li>the attribute is <strong>not</strong> a member end of some association.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b73cc0b2-ab93-455b-8808-1ffc51048707"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Association </ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To convert an attribute to an association</p><hr /><ol><li>On a diagram pane, select a Class with an Attribute, or select an Attribute itself.</li><li>From the Class's or Attribute's shortcut menu, select the <strong>Refactor</strong> command, then <strong>Convert To</strong>, and <strong>Association</strong>.</li><li>On the diagram pane, the Attribute is represented as an <strong>association</strong> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0dbbfdc4-0c54-4a85-807e-4a3a03ef5955">with a</ac:inline-comment-marker> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Role" /><ac:link-body><strong>role</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Combined Association Role.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4dce3c28-a549-477e-84fd-67327ec2b3c0"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Logitics</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can move attributes back to the class by dragging and dropping them on the class shape.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4>Convert Attribute To Port</h4><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e4d53c02-44d2-4957-a51d-a44c293f34fc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Port</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To convert to a <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="37dee737-7ee2-44a0-9260-77527fc2ac36">port</ac:inline-comment-marker> </p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click an Attribute or multiple Attributes.</li><li>On their shortcut menu, click <strong>Refactor </strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e4c0c441-a688-4526-9306-ba4df07f00dd">&gt;</ac:inline-comment-marker> <strong>Convert To</strong> &gt; <strong>Port</strong>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="514e902d-8951-45a2-aed2-0fb7c3bde736"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">227180461</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/UML+elements">UML elements</a></li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Class">Class</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Class+diagram">Class diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Editing+property+values">Editing Property Values</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Specification+window">Specification window</a></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967526 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00078: Behavior

- page_id: `243967526`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967526/Behavior
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:34.483+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: ['classifier-behavior', 'owned-behavior', 'behavior-type']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Behavior describes how the states of objects change over time.

Behavior types are as follows:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function Behavior' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opaque Behavior' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol State Machine diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MT']

Behaviors are used:

- To [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assigning behavior to state' space=''] of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space=''] .
- To [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assigning Behavior Type' space=''] of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space=''] .

The Behaviors are displayed in the compartments of the following elements:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Component' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Node' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actor' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaboration' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opaque Behavior' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='']

Behaviors are divided into two groups:

- classifier Behaviors and
- owned Behaviors.

Keep in mind that the **classifier** Behavior group can have only **one** Behavior.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Working With Behavior

Working With Behavior

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']

#### PANEL: Related Diagrams

Related Diagrams

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol State Machine diagram' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>A Behavior describes how the states of objects change over time.</p><p>Behavior types are as follows:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Activity" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Function Behavior" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interaction" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Opaque Behavior" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Protocol State Machine diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Protocol State Machine]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[State Machine]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p>Behaviors are used:</p><ul><li>To <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Assigning behavior to state" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specify an Entry, Do, and Exit activities]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="State" /></ac:link>.</li><li>To <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Assigning Behavior Type" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specify an Effect]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Transition" /></ac:link>.</li></ul><p>The Behaviors are displayed in the compartments of the following elements:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Component" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Stereotype" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Node" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actor" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Use Case" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Collaboration" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interaction" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[State Machine]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Activity]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Opaque Behavior" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Association" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Association Class]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p>Behaviors are divided into two groups:</p><ul><li>classifier Behaviors and</li><li>owned Behaviors.</li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0ba3c93-f61c-4d02-9001-17d89100ab97"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Keep in mind that the <strong>classifier</strong> Behavior group can have only <strong>one</strong> Behavior.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Two groups of Behavior compartment" ac:alt="Two groups of Behavior compartment"><ri:attachment ri:filename="behavior_compartment.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=9919461 space=MED version=8 -->
````
## PAGE 00079: Binding Connector

- page_id: `9919461`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/9919461/Binding+Connector
- version_number: 8
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:46:29.296+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > SysML v1 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

711187185

711187197

711187186

A Binding connector is the only connector that allows to bind [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Parameter' space=''] to the properties of other Block. It is a connector which specifies that the properties at both ends of the connector have equal values. If the properties at both ends of a binding connector are typed by Data Types or Value Types, it means that the instances of the properties at both ends must hold equal values, recursively through any nested properties within the connected properties. If the properties at both ends of a binding connector are typed by Blocks, it means that the instances of the properties must refer to the same block instance. As with any connector owned by a SysML Block, each end of a binding connector may be nested within a multi-level path of properties accessible from the owning Block. The NestedConnectorEnd stereotype is used to represent such nested ends, just as for nested ends of other SysML connectors.

The two ends of a binding connector must have either the same type or types that are compatible so that equality of their values can be defined.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

711187183

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Parametric Diagram' space='MT']
- [Creating Binding Connector](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Creating+Binding+Connector)

It is a connector which specifies that the properties at both ends of the connector have equal values. If the properties at both ends of a binding connector are typed by Data Types or Value Types, it means that the instances of the properties at both ends must hold equal values, recursively through any nested properties within the connected properties. If the properties at both ends of a binding connector are typed by Blocks, it means that the instances of the properties must refer to the same block instance. As with any connector owned by a SysML Block, each end of a binding connector may be nested within a multi-level path of properties accessible from the owning Block. The NestedConnectorEnd stereotype is used to represent such nested ends, just as for nested ends of other SysML connectors.</p><p>The two ends of a binding connector must have either the same type or types that are compatible so that equality of their values can be defined.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Binding Connector" ac:alt="Binding Connector"><ri:attachment ri:filename="binding_connector.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc5995b5-c75f-4a85-947c-5f31267419ec"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">711187183</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="SysML Parametric Diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><a class="current" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Creating+Binding+Connector">Creating Binding Connector</a></p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=9919416 space=MED version=15 -->
## PAGE 00080: Block

- page_id: `9919416`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/9919416/Block
- version_number: 15
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:46:26.945+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > SysML v1 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Visibility representation

297287617

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Visibility representation

297287619

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Visibility representation

297287618

Blocks provide a general purpose capability to describe the architecture of a system and represent the system hierarchy in terms of systems and subsystems. Blocks describe not only the connectivity relationships within/between a system and its subsystems, but also quantitative values, as well as other information about that system (for example, documentation).

You can use SysML blocks throughout all phases of system specification and design, and apply them to many different kinds of systems. These include modeling either the logical or physical decomposition of a system, and the specification of software, hardware, or human elements.

A Block is a modular unit that describes the structure of a system or an element. It may include both structural and behavioral features (such as properties and operations) that represent the state of the system and behavior that the system may exhibit. Some of these properties may hold parts of a system that can also be described by blocks. A block may include a structure of connectors between its properties to indicate how its parts or other properties relate to one another.

Any reusable form of description that may be applied to a system or a set of system characteristics can be described by a block. These reusable descriptions, for example, may be applied to purely conceptual aspects of a system design, such as relationships that hold between parts or properties of a system. Parts (properties) in these systems can interact by many different means, such as software operations, discrete state transitions, flows of inputs and outputs, or continuous interactions. Connectors owned by SysML blocks can be used to define relationships between parts or other properties of the same containing block.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Visibility representation

Visibility representation

In SysML, properties and operations of the Block are public. Visibility representation literals, like +, #, ~, -, are not displayed in the Containment tree or in the element symbol on a diagram.

297287616

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Block Definition Diagram' space='MT']

Blocks describe not only the connectivity relationships within/between a system and its subsystems, but also quantitative values, as well as other information about that system (for example, documentation).</p><p>You can use SysML blocks throughout all phases of system specification and design, and apply them to many different kinds of systems. These include modeling either the logical or physical decomposition of a system, and the specification of software, hardware, or human elements.</p><p>A Block is a modular unit that describes the structure of a system or an element. It may include both structural and behavioral features (such as properties and operations) that represent the state of the system and behavior that the system may exhibit. Some of these properties may hold parts of a system that can also be described by blocks. A block may include a structure of connectors between its properties to indicate how its parts or other properties relate to one another.</p><p>Any reusable form of description that may be applied to a system or a set of system characteristics can be described by a block. These reusable descriptions, for example, may be applied to purely conceptual aspects of a system design, such as relationships that hold between parts or properties of a system. Parts (properties) in these systems can interact by many different means, such as software operations, discrete state transitions, flows of inputs and outputs, or continuous interactions. Connectors owned by SysML blocks can be used to define relationships between parts or other properties of the same containing block.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Block" ac:alt="Block"><ri:attachment ri:filename="block.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="45e244e1-7a14-47d4-88f2-ec499c585171"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Visibility representation</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In SysML, properties and operations of the Block are public. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=9919404 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00081: Bound Reference

- page_id: `9919404`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/9919404/Bound+Reference
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:46:23.844+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > SysML v1 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Properties with Bound Reference applied and upper multiplicity greater than one are ordered, with values ordered according to when they are reached in navigating the binding path (and how they are ordered within their blocks), and non-unique, to support paths that lead to or pass through the same object.

#### PANEL: Related diagrams

Related diagrams

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=9919458 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00082: Boundary System

- page_id: `9919458`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/9919458/Boundary+System
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:46:25.321+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > SysML v1 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Boundary System is a special external system that serves as medium between another system and the system under development without having its own interests in the communication.

#### TIP: Example

Example

- Bus system.
- Communication system.

#### PANEL: Related diagrams

Related diagrams

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Use Case Diagram' space='MT']
