<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967472 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00084: Call Behavior Action

- page_id: `243967472`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967472/Call+Behavior+Action
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:32.833+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Action
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

673708054

673708057

673747585

**On this page**

43

673708056

##### Description

The Call Behavior Action invokes a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space=''] directly rather than invoking a behavioral feature that, in turn, causes the Behavior. The argument values of the Action are available to the execution of the invoked Behavior. The execution of the Call Behavior Action waits until the execution of the invoked Behavior is completed and a result is returned on its Output Pin. In particular, the invoked Behavior can be an Activity.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Assigning a Behavior to the Call Behavior Action

To assign a Behavior to the Call Behavior Action, do one of the following

- On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT'] [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MT'] , click [ATTACHMENT filename='automatic_behavior_creation.png'] . An Activity will be created [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT'] .
- Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'] of the Call Behavior Action, and click the cell of the **Behavior**property value. Then click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and, in the opened dialog, select the Behavior. Click **OK** when you are finished.
- On the Activity diagram, right-click the Call Behavior Action shape. On the shortcut menu, click **Behavior,** and then click to select a Behavior that you wish to assign on the element.
- On the Activity diagram, click a Call Behavior Action shape, and then, on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. In the opened list, select the Behavior.
- On the Activity diagram, click a Call Behavior Action shape, and then press Ctrl+T. In the opened list, select the Behavior.

- On the diagram, click a Call Behavior Action shape, then click the name area. Type the “:” colon, and then type the existing Behavior name. Press Enter, or click anywhere on the diagram. The Behavior is created and assigned.

Type the “:” colon, then press Ctrl+Space or Ctrl+Backspace and then, in the opened list, select the Behavior.

- Drag a Behavior from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='MT'] onto the Call Behavior Action shape on the diagram. The Behavior is assigned to the Call Behavior Action.

- Double click the Call Behavior Action with the assigned Behavior - the Behavior [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'] opens, or, if the assigned Behavior is a diagram, the diagram opens in the same diagram tab.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying rake icon' space='MT'] [ATTACHMENT filename='rake.png'] is displayed on the shape if the Call Behavior Action refers to another Activity diagram. The rake icon is not shown by default, therefore, you need to set the Show Rake Icon property value to true in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MT']**Symbol Properties** dialog .
- A parameter is created automatically for each newly created Pin on the Call Behavior Action. The parameter’s name, type, direction, and multiplicity are synchronized with the Pin name, type, direction, and multiplicity. Note that a parameter for a pin is created automatically if:
  - the Call Behavior Action has the Behavior assigned.
  - the Behavior is editable.
  - the Behavior has only one usage, and it is not used in another project (in server projects).

##### Creating a new diagram for the Call Behavior Action

To create a new diagram for the Call Behavior Action, do one of the following

- On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT'] , click the Call Behavior Action shape, and then, on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='activity_diagram_smart_manipulator.png'] .The new Activity diagram opens in the same diagram tab.

- The name of the new Activity diagram is the same as the name of the Call Behavior Action.
- If a Behavior was already assigned for the Call Behavior Action, a new Activity diagram under the Behavior will be created. This is valid if the Behavior is editable and the Behavior’s type is Activity.
- If Pin(s) are created on the Call Behavior Action, then the parameters for these Pins are created in a newly created activity. Pins are synchronized with the created parameters, i.e., the Pin name, type, and multiplicity are synchronized with the parameter name, type, and multiplicity.
- If parameters are created for a new Activity, then Activity parameter Nodes are created in a new Activity diagram and displayed on the Activity diagram frame.

- On the Activity diagram, right-click the Call Behavior Action shape, point to **Create Diagram,** and then point to a diagram type.

##### Changing the Name Display Mode on the Call Behavior Action symbol

To change the Name Display Mode on the Call Behavior Action symbol

- Right-click the Call Behavior Action and select the **Symbol Properties**command.
- In the****[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MT']**Symbol Properties** dialog, find the**Name Display Mode** property.
- Select one of the following value: 
 - **Show Action Name** (default only for UPDM projects) - to show action name. 
 - **Show Behavior Name**to show behavior name.**** 
 - **Show Both** - to show action and behavior names.**** 
 - **Show Both or Behavior Name** (default) - to show only the behavior name if the action name is not specified, or if the action name matches the behavior name.

- Click **Close**. 
The selected name is shown on the Call Behavior Action symbol.

673708053

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Action' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="716c849c-8552-49ae-bb85-23a7ac4450a5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">673708054</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="de079c83-f484-47f7-bbd6-1db4cb0c2233"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">673708057</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1877972e-7ac9-4809-b708-58ad6f0137f3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">673747585</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="89b24b9a-f3a6-4b51-b1c5-678e3ef55e67"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b2a4904-8d7f-4be0-b4b5-f70f9f935d99"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">673708056</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3>Description</h3><p>The Call Behavior Action invokes a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Behavior" /></ac:link> directly rather than invoking a behavioral feature that, in turn, causes the Behavior. The argument values of the Action are available to the execution of the invoked Behavior. The execution of the Call Behavior Action waits until the execution of the invoked Behavior is completed and a result is returned on its Output Pin. In particular, the invoked Behavior can be an Activity.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="The Receive Order and Fill Order Call Behavior Actions" ac:alt="The Receive Order and Fill Order Call Behavior Actions"><ri:attachment ri:filename="call_behavior_sample.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Assigning a Behavior to the Call Behavior Action</h3><p>To assign a Behavior to the Call Behavior Action, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>On the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[palette]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, click <ac:image ac:title=" Behavior Creation Mode" ac:alt=" Behavior Creation Mode"><ri:attachment ri:filename="automatic_behavior_creation.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. An Activity will be created <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[automatically on a Call Behavior Action creation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><p>Open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> of the Call Behavior Action, and click the cell of the <strong>Behavior </strong>property value. Then click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and, in the opened dialog, select the Behavior. Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are finished.</p></li><li><p>On the Activity diagram, right-click the Call Behavior Action shape. On the shortcut menu, click <strong>Behavior,</strong> and then click to select a Behavior that you wish to assign on the element.</p></li><li><p>On the Activity diagram, click a Call Behavior Action shape, and then, on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specify_type.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. In the opened list, select the Behavior.</p></li><li><p>On the Activity diagram, click a Call Behavior Action shape, and then press Ctrl+T. In the opened list, select the Behavior.</p></li></ul><ul><li>On the diagram, click a Call Behavior Action shape, then click the name area. Type the “:” colon, and then type the existing Behavior name. Press <strong>Enter,</strong> or click anywhere on the diagram. The Behavior is created and assigned.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="51ab5312-bac6-4b6e-a001-5efc39df9cea"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Type the “:” colon, then press Ctrl+Space or Ctrl+Backspace and then, in the opened list, select the Behavior.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li class="auto-cursor-target">Drag a Behavior from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> onto the Call Behavior Action shape on the diagram. The Behavior is assigned to the Call Behavior Action.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd5f7d96-42df-486c-b95d-03df2f020f93"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Double click the Call Behavior Action with the assigned Behavior - the Behavior <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> opens, or, if the assigned Behavior is a diagram, the diagram opens in the same diagram tab.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Displaying rake icon" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[The rake icon]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="rake.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="_icons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> is displayed on the shape if the Call Behavior Action refers to another Activity diagram. The rake icon is not shown by default, therefore, you need to set the <strong>Show Rake Icon </strong>property value to <em>true</em> in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>A parameter is created automatically for each newly created Pin on the Call Behavior Action. The parameter’s name, type, direction, and multiplicity are synchronized with the Pin name, type, direction, and multiplicity. <br />Note that a parameter for a pin is created automatically if:<ul><li>the Call Behavior Action has the Behavior assigned. </li><li>the Behavior is editable.</li><li>the Behavior has only one usage, and it is not used in another project (in server projects).</li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Creating a new diagram for the Call Behavior Action</h3><p>To create a new diagram for the Call Behavior Action, do one of the following</p><hr /><p><br /></p><ul><li>On the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link>, click the Call Behavior Action shape, and then, on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="activity_diagram_smart_manipulator.png" /></ac:image>.The new Activity diagram opens in the same diagram tab.<br /><br /></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2f821a10-c300-43be-ba52-3a8656134cc6"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The name of the new Activity diagram is the same as the name of the Call Behavior Action.</li><li>If a Behavior was already assigned for the Call Behavior Action, a new Activity diagram under the Behavior will be created. This is valid if the Behavior is editable and the Behavior’s type is Activity.</li><li>If Pin(s) are created on the Call Behavior Action, then the parameters for these Pins are created in a newly created activity. Pins are synchronized with the created parameters, i.e., the Pin name, type, and multiplicity are synchronized with the parameter name, type, and multiplicity.</li><li>If parameters are created for a new Activity, then Activity parameter Nodes are created in a new Activity diagram and displayed on the Activity diagram frame.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li><p>On the Activity diagram, right-click the Call Behavior Action shape, point to <strong>Create Diagram,</strong> and then point to a diagram type.</p><p><br /></p></li></ul><h3>Changing the Name Display Mode on the Call Behavior Action symbol</h3><p>To change the Name Display Mode on the Call Behavior Action symbol</p><hr /><ul><li><p>Right-click the Call Behavior Action and select the <strong>Symbol Properties </strong>command.</p></li><li><p><span class="confluence-link">In the<strong> </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></span>, find the<strong> Name Display Mode</strong> property.</p></li><li><p>Select one of the following value:<br /> - <strong>Show Action Name</strong> (default only for UPDM projects)  - to show action name.<br /> - <strong>Show Behavior Name </strong>to show behavior name.<strong> </strong><br /> - <strong>Show Both</strong> - to show action and behavior names.<strong> </strong><br /> - <strong>Show Both or Behavior Name</strong> (default) - to show only the behavior name if the action name is not specified, or if the action name matches the behavior name.</p></li></ul><ul><li><p>Click <strong>Close</strong>.<br />The selected name is shown on the Call Behavior Action symbol.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2cfd6768-5795-4d08-a9d2-c86fb5deb299"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">673708053</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Behavior" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Action" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967475 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00085: Call Operation Action

- page_id: `243967475`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967475/Call+Operation+Action
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:32.914+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Action
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

907090850

907090852

907090851

The Call Operation Action transmits an operation call request to the target object, where it can cause the invocation of an associated [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space=''].

You can display an Action name and/or name of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space=''] on the Call Operation Action shape. For example, if you have two Call Operation Action elements calling the same Operation, you can specify their names to distinguish the Actions from each other.

| Notation | Description |
| --- | --- |
| The Call Operation Action with the getOrder operation | In this example, the Call Operation Action has been assigned for the getOrder operation whose type is the OrdersDB class. |
| The Call Operation Action named Get supplementary order | In this example, the Call Operation Action is named Get supplementary order. It has been assigned for getOrder operation whose type is the OrdersDB class. |
| The Call Operation Action with the hidden classified name | The classified name of an operation is hidden from the Call Operation Action shape. |

To assign an Operation for the Call Operation Action, do one of the following

- On the diagram, right-click the Call Operation Action, and, on the shortcut menu, click Select Operation . In the opened [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='MT']**Select Operation** dialog , select an Operation you need to assign to the element, or click the Creation Mode button to create a new Operation. Click OK when you are finished.
- In the Call Operation Action [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'] , click to select the Operation property specification cell, and click button [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] . In the opened [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='MT']**Select Operation** dialog , select the Operation you need to assign to the element, or click the Creation Mode button to create a new Operation. Click OK then you are finished.

The modeling tool allows you to display the Operation name and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='']of the Operation on the Call Operation Action shape.

When the Operation is assigned to the Call Operation Action, there are two name/operation display options available:

| Description | Example |
| --- | --- |
| If the Call Operation Action is not named, or the name of the Call Operation Action is the same as the assigned Operation name, the name of the Class is displayed under the Operation name. |  |
| If the Call Operation Action name differs from the assigned Operation name, then <class of the operation>::<operation name> is displayed under the Call Operation Action name. |  |

To show / hide the Operation name and Class of the Operation from the Call Operation Action shape

- Open the Call Operation Action [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MT']**Symbol Properties** dialog , and set the Show Qualified Name for Operation property value to true / false.

907090849

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Action' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=228985915 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00086: Capability

- page_id: `228985915`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228985915/Capability
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:29:53.873+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An enterprise's ability to Achieve a desired effect realized through a combination of ways and means (e.g., CapabilityConfigurations) along with specified measures.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

Class

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="14a5b431-d758-483a-8615-5d19c8a3ac55">Description</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">An enterprise's ability to Achieve a desired effect realized through a combination of ways and means (e.g., CapabilityConfigurations) along with specified measures.</span></p><p><strong><span class="fontstyle0"><span class="fontstyle0">Architecture Framework</span></span></strong></p><p>UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</p><p><strong><span class="fontstyle0"><span class="fontstyle2">Extension</span></span></strong></p><p><span class="fontstyle0"><span class="fontstyle2">Class</span></span></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=228985918 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00087: Capability Configuration

- page_id: `228985918`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228985918/Capability+Configuration
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:29:54.776+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A composite structure representing the physical and human resources (and their interactions) in an enterprise, assembled to meet a capability.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Class

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=228985919 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00088: Capability Kind

- page_id: `228985919`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228985919/Capability+Kind
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:29:55.748+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Enumeration of the possible kinds of Capability.

The following are enumeration literals for Capability Kind:

- Operational
- Other
- Personnel
- Resource
- Security
- Service
- Strategic

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

Enumeration

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

````

Property of a Capability typed by another Capability, enabling whole-part relationships and structures.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Property

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=228985921 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00090: Capable Element

- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228985921/Capable+Element

- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

**Description**

An abstract element that represents a structural element that can perform behaviors (i.e. Operational Activity).

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

Element

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=228985922 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00091: Challenge

- page_id: `228985922`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228985922/Challenge
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:29:59.029+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An existing or potential difficulty, circumstance, or obstacle which will require effort and determination from an enterprise to overcome in achieving its goals.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

Class

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">An existing or potential difficulty, circumstance, or obstacle which will require effort and determination from an enterprise to overcome in achieving its goals.</p><p><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p>UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</p><p><strong>Extensions</strong></p><p>Class</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=9919477 space=MED version=4 -->
## PAGE 00093: Change Structural Feature Event

- page_id: `9919477`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/9919477/Change+Structural+Feature+Event
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:46:25.907+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > SysML v1 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Change Structural Feature Event is an event which is used to model changes in values of structural features.

#### PANEL: Related page

Related page

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Event' space='MT']

#### PANEL: Related diagrams

Related diagrams

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Activity Diagram' space='MT']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967983 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00094: Changing state to composite/submachine/orthogonal state

- page_id: `243967983`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967983/Changing+state+to+composite+submachine+orthogonal+state
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:47.199+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > State Machine > State
- labels: ['changing-states']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can change your current State to a simple State, composite State, orthogonal State, and submachine State. 
 
 #simpletocompositeTo change a State to a composite State

- Drag a vertex on a simple State symbol. A region is created and the simple State is converted to a composite State.

To change a State to a orthogonal State

- Add two regions to the State. Read more about [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space=''].

To change a State to a submachine State

1. On the diagram, right-click the State, then on the shortcut menu, click **Submachine**.
2. In the dialog, click to select a submachine that you wish to apply on the State or click the **Create** button, to create the submachine.

To change a State to a submachine State, the State must contain no regions.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MT']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967969 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00095: Choice

- page_id: `243967969`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967969/Choice
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:46.785+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > State Machine > Pseudo States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The choice vertices, when reached, result in the dynamic evaluation of the guards or the triggers of its outgoing transitions. This realizes a dynamic conditional branch. It allows splitting of transitions into multiple outgoing paths such that the decision on which path to take may be a function of the results of prior actions performed in the same run-to-completion step.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pseudo States' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

This realizes a dynamic conditional branch. It allows splitting of transitions into multiple outgoing paths such that the decision on which path to take may be a function of the results of prior actions performed in the same run-to-completion step.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36e20852-22dd-420b-8d4b-52b136b22981"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f01b84d9-d9d1-4049-8315-15a6a05d850d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="7e19d119-2f65-4f2a-b944-60c34f7c4ba8"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Pseudo States" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967532 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00096: Class

- page_id: `243967532`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967532/Class
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:34.635+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1408904160

1408904163

1408904162

A Class is drawn as a solid-outline rectangle with three compartments separated by horizontal lines. The top name compartment holds the Class name and other general properties of the Class (including [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='']); the middle list compartment holds a list of properties; the bottom list compartment holds a list of operations. The property and operation compartments are optional, and you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying and suppressing compartments on shapes' space='MT'] them.

A Class is a descriptor for a set of objects with similar structures, behaviors, and relationships. The model is concerned with describing the intention of the Class, which are the rules that define it. The run-time execution provides its extension that are its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space=''].

A Class represents a concept within the system being modeled. It has a data structure, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space=''], and relationships to other elements. The name of a Class has a scope within the package in which it is declared, and the name must be unique, among Class names, within its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space=''].

Classes are declared in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MT'] and are used in most of other diagrams. UML provides a graphical notation for declaring and using these Classes as well as a textual notation for referencing Classes within the descriptions of other model elements.

1408904159

The model is concerned with describing the intention of the Class, which are the rules that define it. The run-time execution provides its extension that are its <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Instance Specification" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[instances]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </p><p>A Class represents a concept within the system being modeled. The name of a Class has a scope within the package in which it is declared, and the name must be unique, among Class names, within its <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Package" /></ac:link>.</p><p>Classes are declared in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Class diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and are used in most of other diagrams. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967573 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00097: Collaboration

- page_id: `243967573`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967573/Collaboration
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:44:15.009+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: More Information

1357439945

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: More Information

1357439958

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: More Information

1357439947

A Collaboration describes the structure of elements that implement a certain behavior. In the Collaboration, the elements are the properties connected with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space=''].

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Example of a collaboration

In this example, you can see the*Loan* Collaboration. The Collaboration contains two properties - *CatcherInTheRye* and *Adele*. These are the properties (attributes) and the*LibraryItem*and*AudioRecord* arethe types of each property. The *CatcherInTheRye*and *Adele* are connected with the connector relationship.

#### TIP: More Information

More Information

For more information on how to assign a behavior to a Collaboration Use, read [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assigning Behavior diagrams automatically' space=''].

To create the property and connector in the collaboration

1. Create a Collaboration symbol [IMAGE alt='' src='']in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MT'], you may name it *Loan*, for example.
2. On the C lass diagram , select the Collaboration, right-click on it to open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Element Shortcut Menu' space='MT'] . Select the **Symbol Properties** command.
3. In the **Symbol Properties** dialog, c lick to clear the **Suppress Structure** check box. Click **Close**.
4. On the diagram pane, or in the**Model Browser**,select the elementand drag-and-drop it to the Collaboration symbol on the diagram pane.
5. The new property is created. The type of the property is the element that was dragged and dropped.
6. To create aconnectorbetween properties, expand the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space='MT']Composite Structurediagrampallet and click the button.
7. Draw the connector relationship betweenproperties.
8. Now you have the structure represented in the Collaboration.

##### Collaboration properties

You can format collaboration symbol properties in the **Symbol Properties** dialog.

#### TIP: More Information

More Information

For more information about symbol representation properties, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MT'].

You can specify collaboration properties in the collaboration Specification window. In the same window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window.

#### TIP: More Information

More Information

For more information about the Specification window usage, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'].

For more information about specifying property values, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MT'].

1357439941

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967576 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00098: Collaboration Use

- page_id: `243967576`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967576/Collaboration+Use
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:44:15.460+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Collaboration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Specification Window and Property Values

1353557643

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Specification Window and Property Values

1353557654

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Specification Window and Property Values

1353557644

A Collaboration Use represents the implementation of a pattern described by a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaboration' space=''] in a specific situation. In this context, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency' space=''] means that the roles in the Collaboration are bound by concrete elements. A Collaboration can be used many times in different Collaboration Uses.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of multiple Collaboration Uses along with Parts

#### TIP: Model Tip

Model Tip

These instructions will work best if you have a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space='MT'] created already.

To assign a Collaboration to a Collaboration Use

Before assigning a Collaboration to a Collaboration Use, you **must** have a Collaboration created in your model.

#### INFO: Collaboration

Collaboration

Read [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaboration' space=''] on how to create a Collaboration in your model.

After creating a Collaboration, do one of the following:

- On the Composite Structure diagram palette, click the Collaboration Use button [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-12 at 3.24.55 PM.png'] , and then in the opened dialog, select an existing Collaboration or click the Creation Mode button [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-12 at 3.35.47 PM.png'] to create a new Collaboration. Click OK when you are done.
- On the Composite Structure diagram pane, create a Collaboration Use, then click the Collaboration Use shape, and then on the smart manipulator toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-13 at 6.14.09 PM.png'] symbol. Select a Collaboration. Press Enter or click anywhere on the diagram pane. The Collaboration is now created and assigned.
- On the Composite Structure diagram pane, create a Collaboration Use, then click the Collaboration Use shape, and then press Ctrl+T . Type the name of the new Collaboration and press Enter , or click anywhere on the diagram pane. The Collaboration is now created and assigned.
- On the Composite Structure diagram pane, create a Collaboration Use, then click the Collaboration Use shape, after that, click the name area. Type the “:” colon and then type the Collaboration name. Press Enter , or click anywhere on the diagram pane. The Collaboration is now created and assigned.
- In the Containment tree , select the Collaboration and drag and drop it on the diagram pane. A Collaboration Use with the assigned Collaboration is created.
- On the Composite Structure diagram pane, open the Specification window of the Collaboration Use and click the cell of the Type property value. In the opened dialog, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-12 at 2.59.14 PM.png'] , and select the Collaboration. Click OK when you are done.

##### Collaboration use properties

You can format Collaboration Use symbol properties in the **Symbol Properties** dialog.

#### TIP: Formatting Symbols

Formatting Symbols

For more information about symbol representation properties, see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MT'].

You can specify Collaboration Use properties in the Collaboration Use's **Specification window**. In the same window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window.

#### TIP: Specification Window and Property Values

Specification Window and Property Values

For more information about the Specification window usage, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'].

For more information about specifying property values, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MT'].

1353557641

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967672 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00099: Combined Fragment

- page_id: `243967672`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967672/Combined+Fragment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:38.706+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Fragment
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

876860645

876860648

876860646

Our modeling tool represents twelve kinds of fragments: Alternatives, Loop, Option, Parallel, Break, Negative, Critical Region, Consider, Ignore, Weak Sequencing, Strict Sequencing, and Assertion.

The UML Combined Fragment element allows the expressions of interaction fragments to be defined in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MT']. The Combined Fragments provide a means to define particular conditions and subprocesses for any sections of lifelines in the Sequence diagram by specifying an area where the conditions or subprocesses apply. Using the Combined Fragment, a fragment of a Sequence diagram can be separated.

To draw a Combined Fragment

1. In the Sequence diagram toolbar, select the Alternatives element to draw.
2. From the Combined Fragment shortcut menu, select the Covered Lifelines command and in the Covered Lifelines dialog, select the lifelines to display.
3. In the Sequence diagram toolbar, in the Options group select the Parallel Combined Fragment to draw.
4. Draw the Critical Region Combined Fragment from the Sequence diagram toolbar, in the Option button group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

876860644

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alternative Fragment' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Fragment' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The Combined Fragments provide a means to define particular conditions and subprocesses for any sections of lifelines in the Sequence diagram by specifying an area where the conditions or subprocesses apply. Using the Combined Fragment, a fragment of a Sequence diagram can be separated. </p><p><br /></p><p>To draw a Combined Fragment</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Sequence diagram toolbar, select the Alternatives element to draw.</li><li>From the Combined Fragment shortcut menu, select the <strong>Covered Lifelines</strong> command and in the Covered Lifelines dialog, select the lifelines to display.</li><li>In the Sequence diagram toolbar, in the <strong>Options</strong> group select the <strong>Parallel</strong> Combined Fragment to draw. </li><li>Draw the <strong>Critical Region</strong> Combined Fragment from the Sequence diagram toolbar, in the <strong>Option</strong> button group. </li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-21 at 6.08.00 PM.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd976d2d-6bed-4b89-8be6-f75277a7b1be"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">876860644</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Alternative Fragment" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Fragment" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00100: Command

- page_id: `228985924`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228985924/Command
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:29:59.820+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A type of Resource Exchange that asserts that one Organizational Resource commands another.

Architecture Framework

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Information Flow

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">A type of Resource Exchange that asserts that one Organizational Resource commands another. </p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span class="fontstyle0">Architecture Framework</span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UML Information Flow</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967584 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00101: Comment

- page_id: `243967584`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967584/Comment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:35.809+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A **Comment** is a UML element used to specify various remarks of an element in the model. In the diagram palette, under **Common**, the Comment command appears under the Note command. You can display text in the Comment as plain or HTML text.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a comment.

You can format the Comment's symbol properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MT']**Symbol Properties** dialog.

You can specify a Comment in the Comment's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'], which also displays the description of each Comment property in the description area of the Specification window.

To create a comment in the element Specification window

1. Open the Specification window of the selected element.
2. In the panel on the left, select the Documentation/Comments property group.
3. Under the Comments pane, click Add . The Comment's Specification window opens. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Type the Comment content as the **Body** property value and click **OK**.
5. Close the Specification window.

To view the **Documentation/Comments** symbol on the diagram pane, expand the**Documentation/Comments**property group and drag the selected documentation/comment on the diagram pane.

To switch a comment to HTML or Plain Text

Do one of the following:

- On the diagram pane, select the Comment symbol. Click the button that appears on the lower-left corner of the shape:
  - The HTML button - to switch the text to the HTML text. The Plain button - to switch the text to the Plain text. [ATTACHMENT filename='comment_switch_to_html.png']

#### TIP: HTML text

HTML text

You can edit the HTML text in the comment using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='HTML editor' space='MT'].

To display an anchored element documentation in a comment

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Anchor' space=''] a Comment to a symbol on the diagram pane.
2. Select the Comment on the diagram pane. Open its shortcut menu.
3. Select the Retrieve Documentation command. The anchored symbol's documentation is displayed on the Comment’s symbol.

#### TIP: Retrieve Documentation

Retrieve Documentation

You can enable the **Retrieve Documentation** option **only** if you already have documentation in the Comment's Specification window. Read the first set of instructions on this page to see how to create the comment in the element's Specification window.

#### TIP: Edit

Edit

You can edit element documentation directly on the Comment symbol.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A <strong>Comment</strong> is a UML element used to specify various remarks of an element in the model. In the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7ada6c7a-9164-40b6-a89e-ada926cefcad">diagram palette</ac:inline-comment-marker>, under <strong>Common</strong>, the Comment command appears under the Note command. You can display text in the Comment as plain or HTML text.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="comment.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of a comment.</h6><p>You can format the Comment's symbol properties in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /><ac:link-body><strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>You can specify a Comment in the Comment's <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, which also displays the description of each Comment property in the description area of the Specification window.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a comment in the element Specification window</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Specification window of the selected element.</li><li>In the panel on the left, select the <strong>Documentation/Comments </strong>property group.</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="32666a56-71d4-4dfc-813e-e12235819c11">Under the Comments pane, click </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="32666a56-71d4-4dfc-813e-e12235819c11">Add</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="32666a56-71d4-4dfc-813e-e12235819c11">. The Comment's Specification window opens.</ac:inline-comment-marker><br /><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="32666a56-71d4-4dfc-813e-e12235819c11"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="docummentation_comments_dialog.png" /></ac:image><br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><p>Type the Comment content as the <strong>Body</strong> property value and click <strong>OK</strong>.</p></li><li>Close the Specification window.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="97427e0f-6b33-4e13-a3f2-1ece8761d2f3"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>To view the <strong>Documentation/Comments</strong> symbol on the diagram pane, expand the </span><strong>Documentation/Comments</strong><span> property group and drag the selected documentation/comment on the diagram pane. </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br />To switch a comment to HTML or Plain Text</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li>On the diagram pane, select the Comment symbol. Click the button that appears on the lower-left corner of the shape:<ul><li>The <strong>HTML</strong> button - to switch the text to the HTML text.<br />The <strong>Plain</strong> button - to switch the text to the Plain text.<br /><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="111"><ri:attachment ri:filename="comment_switch_to_html.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="471f6d9b-6129-4147-ad25-9f84cece96fa"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTML text</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can edit the HTML text in the comment using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="HTML editor" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To display an anchored element documentation in a comment</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Anchor" /></ac:link> a Comment to a symbol on the diagram pane.</li><li>Select the Comment on the diagram pane. Open its shortcut menu.</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="30d0409a-5445-4345-93e0-f4cc22f8786b">Select the </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="30d0409a-5445-4345-93e0-f4cc22f8786b">Retrieve Documentation</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="30d0409a-5445-4345-93e0-f4cc22f8786b"> command. </ac:inline-comment-marker><br />The anchored symbol's documentation is displayed on the Comment’s symbol.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="80451aa7-c458-4c7e-8de8-9e8483d6d683"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Retrieve Documentation</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can enable the <strong>Retrieve Documentation</strong> option <strong>only</strong> if you already have documentation in the Comment's Specification window. Read the first set of instructions on this page to see how to create the comment in the element's Specification window.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fefe8eed-17e3-48d0-b388-18215fa615dc"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Edit</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can edit element documentation directly on the Comment symbol.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967525 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00102: Communication Path

- page_id: `243967525`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967525/Communication+Path
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:34.382+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Association
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Communication path is a type of association between nodes in a deployment diagram that shows how the nodes exchange messages and signals.

When you specify communication paths, you can define the number of nodes that can be connected at each end. You can also use labels to identify the type of protocol or network that is used in the communication.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A Communication path is a type of association between nodes in a deployment diagram that shows how the nodes exchange messages and signals.</p><p class="p">When you specify communication paths, you can define the number of nodes that can be connected at each end. You can also use labels to identify the type of protocol or network that is used in the communication.</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=228985925 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00103: Compares To

- page_id: `228985925`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228985925/Compares+To
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:30:00.428+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An abstraction relationship relating the effect that is achieved with the originally expected Desired Effect. Providing a means of comparison, between the expectation of the desirer and the actual result.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

Abstraction

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">An abstraction relationship relating the effect that is achieved with the originally expected Desired Effect. Providing a means of comparison, between the expectation of the desirer and the actual result.</p><p><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p>UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</p><p><strong>Extensions</strong></p><p>Abstraction</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967591 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00107: Component

- page_id: `243967591`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967591/Component
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:35.980+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Component represents all kinds of elements that make up the system. A Component can always be considered as an autonomous unit within a system or subsystem. It is a module having the following features:

- A Component is a replaceable and independent part of the system performing a specific action.
- A Component acts in a context of a well-defined architecture.
- Components interact among each other using [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface' space=''] .

Components provides compartments for listing its provided and required Interfaces, realizations, and artifacts.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

A Component can be specified by changing its property values in the Component [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']. Each property is described in the description area on this window.

To show/hide the interfaces, realizations, and artifacts on the Component’s shape, do one of the following

- On the diagram, right-click the Component’s shape and then from the shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties . In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MT']**Symbol Properties** dialog , set the Suppress Interfaces , Suppress Realizations , or Suppress Artifacts property values to true or false .
- On the diagram, click the Component’s shape and then click the Compartments button [ATTACHMENT filename='compartments.png'] . Then in the menu, click to select or clear the Interfaces , Realizations , or Artifacts check box.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Component diagram' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>A Component represents all kinds of elements that make up the system. A Component can always be considered as an autonomous unit within a system or subsystem. It is a module having the following features:</p><ul><li>A Component is a replaceable and independent part of the system performing a specific action.</li><li>A Component acts in a context of a well-defined architecture.</li><li>Components interact among each other using <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Interfaces]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul><p>Components provides compartments for listing its provided and required Interfaces, realizations, and artifacts.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="An example of a Component" ac:alt="An example of a Component"><ri:attachment ri:filename="component_listed_properties.png" /></ac:image></p><p>A Component can be specified by changing its property values in the Component <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. Each property is described in the description area on this window.</p><p> </p><p>To show/hide the interfaces, realizations, and artifacts on the Component’s shape, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>On the diagram, right-click the Component’s shape and then from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>. In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, set the <strong>Suppress Interfaces</strong>, <strong>Suppress Realizations</strong>, or <strong>Suppress Artifacts</strong> property values to <em>true</em> or <em> false</em>.</li><li>On the diagram, click the Component’s shape and then click the Compartments button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="compartments.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. Then in the menu, click to select or clear the <strong>Interfaces</strong>, <strong>Realizations</strong>, or <strong>Artifacts</strong> check box.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b2025c5-70c1-47b6-9a1b-4fb242548002"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Class diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Component diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967593 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00108: Component realization

- page_id: `243967593`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967593/Component+realization
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:36.078+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Component Realization concept is specified in the Components package to (optionally) define the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying the classifier' space='MT']. The Classifier defines the contract offered by a component in terms of its provided and required interfaces. The component forms an abstraction from these various Classifiers.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">A Component Realization concept is specified in the Components package to (optionally) define the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specifying the classifier" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Classifier]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The Classifier defines the contract offered by a component in terms of its provided and required interfaces. The component forms an abstraction from these various Classifiers.</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249579621 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00113: Conflicts With

- page_id: `249579621`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/249579621/Conflicts+With
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-29T10:38:05.740+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An annotation that indicates UAF elements acting against or in opposition to each other. This means that there are elements in the model that disagree about something important or are incompatible with each other in some way.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

Comment

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;">An annotation that indicates UAF elements acting against or in opposition to each other. This means that there are elements in the model that disagree about something important or are incompatible with each other in some way.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;"><strong><span class="fontstyle0">Architecture Framework</span></strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;">UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;">Comment</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=9919470 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00114: Conform

- page_id: `9919470`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/9919470/Conform
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:46:29.926+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > SysML v1 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Conform relationship is a dependency between a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='View' space=''] and a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Viewpoint' space='']. The View conforms to the rules and conventions specified in the Viewpoint.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related diagram

Related diagram

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Views and Viewpoints Diagram' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>A Conform relationship is a dependency between a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="View" /></ac:link> and a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Viewpoint" /></ac:link>. The View conforms to the rules and conventions specified in the Viewpoint.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Conform" ac:title="Conform"><ri:attachment ri:filename="conform.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="945c9ebe-05c7-4a1c-b7c9-5fdadbc65f5c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related diagram</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Views and Viewpoints Diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=62893639 space=MED version=16 -->
## PAGE 00115: Conjugated Interface Block

- page_id: `62893639`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/62893639/Conjugated+Interface+Block
- version_number: 16
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:46:27.950+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > SysML v1 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

709525036

709525038

709525037

The Conjugated Interface Block (~Interface Block) is a specialization of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface Block' space=''] that has the same features as its original Interface Block except that its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Directed Feature' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Property' space=''] are reversed (conjugated). As shown in the figure below, the*I/O*Interface Block is conjugated to ~I/O which has the same*Control and Status*Flow Properties but with opposite directions.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

709525034

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Block Definition Diagram' space='MT']
- [Defining interfaces using ports](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Defining+interfaces+using+ports)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967984 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00116: Connection Point Reference

- page_id: `243967984`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967984/Connection+Point+Reference
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:47.243+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > State Machine > State
- labels: ['connection-point-reference']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Connection Point Reference represents an entry to or an exit from the submachine State. It can be used as the source or target of a transition.

The following procedures show you how to work with Connection Point Reference:

##### Drawing the Connection Point Reference

To draw the Connection Point Reference on the submachine State

1. On the State diagram palette, click the **Connection Point Reference** button.
2. Click the shape of the submachine State. The **Select Entry/Exit Point** dialog opens.
3. Select *entry point* to define the entry into the submachine State, or *exit point* to define the exit from the submachine State. The Connection Point Reference is drawn on the submachine State with a defined entry or exit point.

##### Checking the assigned entry/exit point to Connection Point Reference

To see the assigned entry/exit point

- Open the **Connection Point Reference** dialog. The **Entry** or **Exit** properties display the defined entries.

##### Assigning the entry/exit points to the Connection Point Reference

To assign the entry/exit points to the Connection Point Reference

- Right-click the Connection Point Reference shape, and, on the shortcut menu ,click **Select****Entry/Exit Point**.

In the **Select** **Entry/Exit** **Point** dialog, only the entry and exit points that are created at the same State Machine as the submachine State are listed. If there are no entry/exit points at the same State machine, the **Select Entry/Exit Point** dialog is not opened when drawing the Connection Point Reference.

##### Displaying the Connection Point References

To display the Connection Point References on the submachine State shape

1. Select the submachine State shape of the Connection Point References you want to display.
2. Open the **Select****Points**dialog by performing one of the following steps: **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** ****
  - From the shortcut menu, select **Display** > **Display Points**.
  - On the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select **Display Points**.
3. In the dialog, select the Connection Point References that you want to display, and click **OK**. [ATTACHMENT filename='select_points_dialog.png'] Selected Connection Point References are displayed on the submachine State shape. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MT']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967594 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00117: Connector

- page_id: `243967594`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967594/Connector
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:44:16.062+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1896492679

1896492681

1896492680

A Connector specifies a link that enables communication between two or more instances. This link can be an instance of an association, or it can represent the possibility of the instances being able to communicate because their identities are known by virtue of being passed on as parameters, held in variables or slots, or because the communicating instances are the same instance.

The link can be realized by something as simple as a pointer or by something as complex as a network connection. In contrast to the associations, which specify the links between any instance of the associated classifiers, the connectors specify the links between instances playing the connected parts only.

Each connector can be attached to two or more connectable elements, each representing a set of instances.

You can specify connector and connector end properties in the connector and connector end Specification window. In the same window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'].

##### Drawing a connector end

A Connector has at least two ends, each representing the participation of instances of the classifiers typing the connectable elements attached to the end. The set of Connector ends is ordered. The connector end information can be displayed on the diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Selecting a Port automatically when drawing a Connector

To select a Port automatically when drawing a Connector from a Port to a Part

1. Select a Port on the diagram pane.
2. On the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select Part shape. The **Select Port** menu appears. The menu appears if a new Port can be created on the Part or the Part has hidden Ports.
3. Select one of the following command:

- None - if you want to connect connector straight to the part.
- On a port in the list. Hidden ports of the part are listed. The selected port will be represented on the diagram and the connector will be connected to it.
- New Port - to create a new port with the same name, type and multiplicity as the port from which the connector is drawn.
- New Nested Port - to create a new nested port. Note that the command exists only if the connector is drawn from the nested port.

You can draw a connector to a Port too and the menu appears. As a result, the nested Port is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Hiding Connector type

You can hide the type name of the represented Connector on the diagram pane.

To hide the type name of Connector

1. Select a single or any number of Connectors on the diagram pane.
2. Right-click to open the shortcut menu.
3. From the shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties.
4. In the Symbol Properties dialog, set the Show Type option value to false. The name of the Connector type is hidden and no longer visible in the Connector label.

1896492678

**On this page**

- [Drawing a connector end](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Connector#Connector-Drawingaconnectorend)
- [Selecting a Port automatically when drawing a Connector](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Connector#Connector-SelectingaPortautomaticallywhendrawingaConnector)
- [Hiding Connector type](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Connector#Connector-HidingConnectortype)

**Related pages**

- Editing property values
- [Setting an Information Flow](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Information+Flow)
- Reversing Information Flow direction
- Model Elements

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

visible in the Connector label.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967598 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00118: Constraint

- page_id: `243967598`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967598/Constraint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:36.331+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Constraint represents additional semantic information attached to the constrained elements. It is an assertion that indicates a restriction that must be satisfied by a correct design of the system. The constrained elements are those elements required to evaluate the constraint specification. In addition, the context of the Constraint can be accessed, and can be used as the namespace for interpreting names used in the specification.

A Constraint is represented as a linguistic, enclosed in braces ({}), statement in some formal (OCL, C++, and other), or a natural language. There are 14 standard constraints in UML such as association, global, and parameter. You can also define your own constraints.

You can create the following constraints:

- Time Constraint. It specifies the combination of min and max timing interval values.
- Duration Constraint. It defines a value specification that indicates the temporal distance between two time instants.
- Constraint. It represents an additional semantic information attached to the constrained elements.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Link' space=''] . It is a graphical representation of a constraint element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Constraints for validation

Constraints for validation

Constraints can also be used to define [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space='MT'] for your model.

Related pages

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>A Constraint represents additional semantic information attached to the constrained elements. It is an assertion that indicates a restriction that must be satisfied by a correct design of the system. The constrained elements are those elements required to evaluate the constraint specification. In addition, the context of the Constraint can be accessed, and can be used as the namespace for interpreting names used in the specification.</p><p>A Constraint is represented as a linguistic, enclosed in braces ({}), statement in some formal (OCL, C++, and other), or a natural language. There are 14 standard constraints in UML such as association, global, and parameter. You can also define your own constraints.</p><p>You can create the following constraints:</p><ul><li>Time Constraint. It specifies the combination of min and max timing interval values.</li><li>Duration Constraint. It defines a value specification that indicates the temporal distance between two time instants.</li><li>Constraint. It represents an additional semantic information attached to the constrained elements.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Constraint Link" /></ac:link>. It is a graphical representation of a constraint element.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="Example of constraints" ac:alt="Example of constraints"><ri:attachment ri:filename="constraint.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="674cfbc4-2acb-4d26-b399-51d2b29159d2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Constraints for validation</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Constraints can also be used to define <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Validation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[validation rules]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for your model.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="74b4a27a-cb36-49b8-adcd-10b988f3aeae"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="98022f1e-958f-4e1d-a1a4-76752611ebfe" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b2025c5-70c1-47b6-9a1b-4fb242548002"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Class diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Use Case" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro> <p class="auto-cursor-target"> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````
