<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=9919485 space=MED version=10 -->
## PAGE 00119: Constraint Block

- page_id: `9919485`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/9919485/Constraint+Block
- version_number: 10
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:46:30.730+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > SysML v1 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Visibility representation

299826927

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Visibility representation

299826930

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Visibility representation

299826929

Constraint blocks provide a mechanism for integrating engineering analysis, such as performance and reliability models, with other SysML models. You can use Constraint blocks to specify a network of constraints that represent mathematical expressions, such as {tp=whlpwr-(Cd*v)-(Cf*tw*v)}, that constrain the physical properties of a system.

Such constraints can also be used to identify critical performance parameters and their relationships to other parameters that can be tracked throughout the system life cycle. A constraint block includes constraints (such as {tp=whlpwr-(Cd*v)-(Cf*tw*v)}) and their parameters (such as Cd, Cf, and i). Constraint blocks define generic forms of constraints that can be used in multiple contexts.

Reusable constraint definitions can be specified on Block Definition Diagrams and packaged into general-purpose or domain-specific model libraries.These constraints can be arbitrarily complex mathematical or logical expressions. The constraints can be nested to enable a constraint to be defined in terms of more basic constraints such as primitive mathematical operators.

In general, you should define constraints in constraint blocks in a Block Definition Diagram first, and then use a Parametric Diagram to bind constraint parameters to properties.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Visibility representation

Visibility representation

In SysML, properties and operations of the Block are public. Visibility representation literals, like +, #, ~, -, are not displayed in the Containment tree or in the element symbol on a diagram.

299826926

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Block Definition Diagram' space='MT']

****

A constraint block includes constraints (such as {tp=whlpwr-(Cd*v)-(Cf*tw*v)}) and their parameters (such as Cd, Cf, and i). <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967601 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00120: Constraint Link

- page_id: `243967601`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967601/Constraint+Link
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:36.531+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Constraint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Constraint Link is a graphical representation of a constraint element. The Constraint Link is shown on the diagram pane as a dashed arrow from one shape to another. You can assign a particular constraint to the Constraint Link. The constraint assigned to the Constraint Link is labeled in braces: {} . See an example of the constraint representation in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

On the Constraint Link, you can display the constraint direction arrow. The direction of the arrow represents a relevant information within the constraint. The client (the tail of the arrow) is mapped to the first position and the supplier (arrowhead) is mapped to the second position in the constraint.

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MT']the constraint symbol properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MT'].

After applying the constraint to the Constraint Link, you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MT']the constraint in the Constraint specification window. In the same window, you can find the description of all constraint properties. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'].

To draw a Constraint Link

1. In the Class, Use Case, Sequence, or Activity [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MT'] , under the Common category, expand the Constraint command and select the Constraint Link.
2. Draw a Constraint Link between desired shapes.

To add a constraint expression to the Constraint Link

- From the Constraint Link shortcut menu, choose the Select Constraint and do one of the following:
  - Select Create Constraint to create a new constraint.
  - Choose a constraint from the list.

To display a Constraint Link direction arrow

1. Select the Constraint Link and from the shortcut menu, select **Show Arrow**. 
The direction of the Constraint Link will be displayed. See an example in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Actions' space='']

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>A Constraint Link is a graphical representation of a constraint element. The Constraint Link is shown on the diagram pane as a dashed arrow from one shape to another. You can assign a particular constraint to the Constraint Link. The constraint assigned to the Constraint Link is labeled in braces: {} . See an example of the constraint representation in the following figure.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Constraint Link between relationships" ac:alt="Constraint Link between relationships"><ri:attachment ri:filename="constraint_link.png" /></ac:image></p><p>On the Constraint Link, you can display the constraint direction arrow. The direction of the arrow represents a relevant information within the constraint. The client (the tail of the arrow) is mapped to the first position and the supplier (arrowhead) is mapped to the second position in the constraint.</p><p>You can <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[format ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>the constraint symbol properties in the  <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /></ac:link>.</p><p>After applying the constraint to the Constraint Link, you can <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Editing property values" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specify ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>the constraint in the Constraint specification window. In the same window, you can find the description of all constraint properties. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</p><p> </p><p>To draw a Constraint Link</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Class, Use Case, Sequence, or Activity <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram palette]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, under the Common category, expand the <strong>Constraint</strong> command and select the Constraint Link.</li><li>Draw a Constraint Link between desired shapes.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To add a constraint expression to the Constraint Link</p><hr /><ul><li>From the Constraint Link shortcut menu, choose the <strong>Select Constraint</strong> and do one of the following:<ul><li>Select <strong>Create Constraint</strong> to create a new constraint. </li><li>Choose a constraint from the list.</li></ul></li></ul><p> </p><p>To display a Constraint Link direction arrow</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Select the Constraint Link and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Show Arrow</strong>. <br />The direction of the Constraint Link will be displayed. See an example in the following figure.</p></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:title="Example of constraint path with direction arrow" ac:alt="Example of constraint path with direction arrow"><ri:attachment ri:filename="constraint_path_arrow.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b2025c5-70c1-47b6-9a1b-4fb242548002"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Class diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Use Case" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Actions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Applying duration constraint on the Action]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

A**Constraint Parameter** is the formal term for a variable that appears in a constraint expression. It is displayed as a small square attached to the boundary on the inside of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=9919483 space=MED version=14 -->
## PAGE 00122: Constraint Property

- page_id: `9919483`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/9919483/Constraint+Property
- version_number: 14
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:46:30.535+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > SysML v1 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1344959267

1344959270

1344959269

A Constraint Property is a property that specifies the constraints of other properties in its containing Block. Every Constraint Property has composite AggregationKind and is typed by a Constraint Block. Constraint Properties are displayed in the **constraints** compartment on a Block shape.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Constraint Property shape containing information of its name, type and expression.

To empower the constraint expression to perform calculations, you have to specify what system parameters should be consumed as variables of that constraint.You can define the Constraint expression as:

- Mathematical equation. E.g. {area =3.14159 * (radius^2)}. When evaluating such equations, the simulation mechanism solves values to correspond the equation. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Mathematical equation' space='MMA']]]>
- Logical expression that contains a comparison operator: ==, <, >, <=, >=, and <>. E.g. {*val1 >= "500"}.*When evaluating such expressions with Boolean data type, the the simulation mechanism check the values to meet the expression. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logical expression' space='MMA']]]>

1344959266

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Parametric Diagram' space='MT']
- [Modeling parametric constraints](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Modeling+parametric+constraints)

Every Constraint Property has composite AggregationKind and is typed by a Constraint Block. Constraint Properties are displayed in the <strong>constraints</strong> compartment on a Block shape. </p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="constraint_property.png" /></ac:image><span style="color: rgb(94,108,132);">The Constraint Property shape containing information of its name, type and expression.</span></h6><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(9,30,66);">To empower the constraint expression to perform calculations, you have to specify what system parameters should be consumed as variables of that constraint. </span>You can define the Constraint expression as:</p><ul><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs">Mathematical equation. E.g. {area =<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span>3.14159 * (radius<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span>^<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span>2)}. When evaluating such equations, the simulation mechanism solves values to correspond the equation. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MMA" ri:content-title="Mathematical equation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about evaluating mathematical equations >]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></li><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs">Logical expression that <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">contains a comparison operator: ==, &lt;, &gt;, &lt;=, &gt;=, and &lt;&gt;.</span> E.g. {<em>val1 &gt;= &quot;500&quot;}. </em>When evaluating such expressions with Boolean data type, the the simulation mechanism check the values to meet the expression. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MMA" ri:content-title="Logical expression" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about evaluating logical expressions >]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eda376be-03d4-4747-9add-b8570a98a008"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1344959266</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="SysML Parametric Diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Modeling+parametric+constraints" class="current">Modeling parametric constraints</a></p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967605 space=MED version=3 -->
## PAGE 00123: Containment

- page_id: `243967605`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967605/Containment
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:45:31.612+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

943409377

943409379

943409378

The Containment relationship represents the containment of elements on the diagram pane. A Containment path replaces a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Nesting Requirements' space='MT']. For example, instead of drawing one package nested into another, you can draw a containment relationship from one package to another (see the figure below).This example shows that the Containment relationship is a graphical representation as well as a nesting of elements.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of package nesting (on the left) and package nesting using containment relationship (on the right)

In the example above, the Containment end with a circle plus symbol shows a containing element. The *User* package is a contained element and the*Domain*package is a containing element: the owner of the*User*package is the*Domain*package.

You can format containment symbol properties in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MT']**Symbol Properties** dialog.

943409376

- Generating reports from the Containment tree
- Customizing Environment Options
- Class Diagram
- Composite Structure Diagram

- Component Diagram
- Object Diagram
- Package Diagram
- Model Elements

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0f9f86af-b8b9-4a4a-99c2-038a6a21c270"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">943409377</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="62dcc777-4459-4da2-ad37-df8f08b74331"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">943409379</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cbcf2e95-266c-4773-8fc2-ab2f7a419bf3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">943409378</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The Containment relationship represents the containment of elements on the diagram pane. A Containment path replaces a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Nesting Requirements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Nesting representation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. For example, instead of drawing one package nested into another, you can draw a containment relationship from one package to another (see the figure below). </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">This example shows that the Containment relationship is a graphical representation as well as a nesting of elements. </span></p><h6><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="containment.png" /></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>Example of package nesting (on the left) and package nesting using containment relationship (on the right) </span></h6><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);font-size: 14.0px;">In the example above, the Containment end with a circle plus symbol shows a containing element. The</span> <em>User</em> <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);font-size: 14.0px;">package is a contained element and the </span><em>Domain</em><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);font-size: 14.0px;"> package is a containing element: the owner of the </span><em>User</em><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);font-size: 14.0px;"> package is the </span><em>Domain</em><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);font-size: 14.0px;"> package.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">You can format containment symbol properties in the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /><ac:link-body><strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">. </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="db4b8067-427d-489d-9b29-9f7f5e88e765"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">943409376</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967608 space=MED version=2 -->
````
## PAGE 00124: Containment Tree

- page_id: `243967608`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967608/Containment+Tree
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:42:26.945+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Containment
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

943711221

943711224

943711223

You can create the Containment tree when drawing more than one Containment relationship.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Example of several containment relationships (on the left) and example of containment tree (on the right)

To group containment paths into a tree

1. Draw a Containment path from each contained element to the containing element.
2. Now, do one of the following:

- Through the diagram pane.
  1. Select the Containment relationship.
  2. Click and hold the little square (the end of the Containment path) next to the Containment end with the circle plus symbol.
  3. Drag and drop the selected Containment end on the other Containment path.
  4. While dragging, the Containment path turns blue, indicating an allowable connection. Containment paths merge together. [ATTACHMENT filename='DnD_containment_path.png']

- Through the shortcut menu.
  1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Shortcut menu' space='CDH'] of the containing element, in the example above: Domain .
  2. Click Refactor > Make Sub Tree .

To ungroup a containment tree

1. Right-click the Containment tree's end with the circle plus symbol pointing to a containing element.
2. In the opened shortcut menu, click the Ungroup Tree command. [ATTACHMENT filename='ungroup_containment_tree.png']

To separate the containment path from the containment tree

- Select the containment path you want to remove from the tree. Open its shortcut menu and click the **Remove From Tree** command. The contained element is added to the containing element separately. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_containment_from_tree.png']
- Drag one Containment path to another element:
  1. On the diagram pane, select the vertical part of the particular Containment path.
  2. Click and hold the upper little square.
  3. Drag and drop end of the Containment path to the other containing element.
  4. The Containment path is removed from the Containment tree and the owner of the containing element has been changed.

943711219

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating reports from the Containment tree' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment' space='']

Containment paths merge together.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="DnD_containment_path.png" /></ac:image></li></ol></li></ul><ul><li><span>Through the shortcut menu.</span><ol><li>Open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CDH" ri:content-title="Shortcut menu" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[shortcut menu]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the containing element, in the example above: <em>Domain</em>.</li><li>Click <strong>Refactor </strong>&gt; <strong>Make Sub Tree</strong>.</li></ol></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To ungroup a containment tree</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Containment tree's end with the circle plus symbol pointing to a containing element.</li><li>In the opened shortcut menu, click the <strong>Ungroup Tree</strong> command.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ungroup_containment_tree.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To separate the containment path from the containment tree</p><hr /><ul><li><p>Select the containment path you want to remove from the tree. Open its shortcut menu and click the <strong>Remove From Tree</strong> command. The contained element is added to the containing element separately.</p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_containment_from_tree.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Drag one Containment path to another element: <ol><li>On the diagram pane, select the vertical part of the particular Containment path. </li><li>Click and hold the upper little square. </li><li>Drag and drop end of the Containment path to the other containing element. </li><li>The Containment path is removed from the Containment tree and the owner of the containing element has been changed. </li></ol></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="14a3464e-4ec1-432b-b684-38dae1c43080"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">943711219</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Generating reports from the Containment tree" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Customizing Environment Options]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=228985933 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00125: Control

- page_id: `228985933`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228985933/Control
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:30:12.087+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A type of Resource Exchange that asserts that one Physical Resource controls another Physical Resource (i.e. the driver of a vehicle controlling the vehicle speed or direction).

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Information Flow

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">A type of Resource Exchange that asserts that one Physical Resource controls another Physical Resource (i.e.  the driver of a vehicle controlling the vehicle speed or direction). </p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong><span class="fontstyle0">Architecture Framework</span></strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UML Information Flow</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967616 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00126: Control Flow

- page_id: `243967616`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967616/Control+Flow
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:44:14.291+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Control flow is an edge that starts an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Action' space=''] node after the previous one is finished.

You can specify a Control flow by changing its property values in the Control flow[Specification window](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Specification+window). Each property is described in the description area on this window.

You can format the Control flow symbol properties in the[Symbol Properties dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Formatting+symbols).

A Control flow can have guards (constraints that provide fine-grained control over the firing of the Control Flow). To specify guards, choose the Guard box in the Control Flow dialog box.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Specification of the Control Flow, Guard property

Set the Control Flow symbol properties in the Symbol Properties dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A Control flow is an edge that starts an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Action" /></ac:link> node after the previous one is finished.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can specify a Control flow by changing its property values in the Control flow </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Specification+window">Specification window</a><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. Each property is described in the description area on this window.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can format the Control flow symbol properties in the </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Formatting+symbols">Symbol Properties dialog</a><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">.</span><br /></span></p><p>A Control flow can have guards (constraints that provide fine-grained control over the firing of the Control Flow). To specify guards, choose the Guard box in the <span class="hcp1">Control</span> <span class="hcp1">Flow</span> dialog box.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Control_flow_Guard.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Specification of the Control Flow, Guard property</h6><p><span>S</span>et the Control Flow symbol properties in the <a>Symbol Properties dialog</a>.</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249005175 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00127: Copy

- page_id: `249005175`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/249005175/Copy
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:38:33.874+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > Requirement-related elements
- labels: ['copy-dependency', 'copy-relationship']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Copy relationship is a dependency between a supplier requirement (master) and a client requirement (slave), specifying that the client requirement text is a read-only copy of the supplier requirement text. In other words, by creating a Copy dependency between two Requirements, you copy the text property from the supplier to the client.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a Copy dependency between two requirements on the diagram

1. On the diagram, select a client Requirement symbol (slave), a symbol you want to be a copy of the supplier Requirement (master). The smart manipulator appears. [ATTACHMENT filename='copy_smart_manipulator.png']
2. Select the Copy command as depicted in the preceding figure and connect the client Requirement to the supplier Requirement.
3. The Copy path goes yellow because of violation of the rule Invalid Slave Requirement text meaning the text of the slave Requirement must be the same as of the master Requirement. Click the Copy path and, in the smart manipulator, select the Validation command. [ATTACHMENT filename='copy_validation.png']
4. The list of suggested solvers appears. Select **Copy text of the supplier requirement to the client requirement**to create and sustain this dependency in your model correctly. Ignoring solverYou may select other solvers but note:**Remove Copy dependency** - removes the dependency from the diagram and the entire model.**Ignore** - ignores the violation and the dependency is created and sustained in the model with the violation.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A Copy relationship is a dependency between a supplier requirement (master) and a client requirement (slave), specifying that the client requirement text is a read-only copy of the supplier requirement text. In other words, by creating a Copy dependency between two Requirements, you copy the text property from the supplier to the client.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="copy_relationship.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Copy dependency between two requirements on the diagram</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="75931300-1595-4146-b7fa-afd869d9f624">On the diagram, select a client Requirement symbol (slave), a symbol you want to be a copy of the supplier Requirement (master). The smart manipulator appears.<br /><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="239"><ri:attachment ri:filename="copy_smart_manipulator.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="63052a71-0082-4e94-9d05-2d9cb5b4e9ee">Select the Copy command as depicted in the preceding figure and connect the client Requirement to the supplier Requirement.</li><li data-uuid="d8b6e09e-ab67-4a69-bdc2-2e75e5bd3d6f">The Copy path goes yellow because of violation of the rule <strong>Invalid Slave Requirement text</strong> meaning the text of the slave Requirement must be the same as of the master Requirement. Click the Copy path and, in the smart manipulator, select the Validation command.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="copy_validation.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="50a5d655-0f12-4a57-b39f-a02dd8776308"><p class="auto-cursor-target">The list of suggested solvers appears. Select <strong>Copy text of the supplier requirement to the client requirement </strong>to create and sustain this dependency in your model correctly.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b28d5edd-ff53-418a-ab58-d7a392836b78"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Ignoring solver</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You may select other solvers but note:</p><ul><li><strong>Remove Copy dependency</strong> - removes the dependency from the diagram and the entire model.</li><li><strong>Ignore</strong> - ignores the violation and the dependency is created and sustained in the model with the violation.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968062 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00129: Creating a basic flow

- page_id: `243968062`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243968062/Creating+a+basic+flow
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:49.138+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Use Case > Use Case Scenario sketch
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

795872164

795872166

795872165

INLINE

To create a basic flow

1. Open the Use Case Specification window and click the Use Case Scenario Sketch property group.
2. In the Basic Flow area, click the Add new step button [ATTACHMENT filename='add_new_value_button.png'] or press Alt+ENTER and type a name of the basic flow step. Repeat this action for each basic flow step you need to create.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Steps of the basic flow are numbered automatically. Use the Up [IMAGE alt='' src='']and Down [IMAGE alt='' src=''] buttons to change the order of steps. In the activity diagram, steps are represented according to their order in the Use Case scenario.

795872163

Repeat this action for each basic flow step you need to create.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_basic_flow.png" /></ac:image></p><p>Steps of the basic flow are numbered automatically. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967537 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00130: Creating a Structured Class

- page_id: `243967537`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967537/Creating+a+Structured+Class
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:42:25.772+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Class
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There is a way to create a piece of the model with a single click: [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''], with a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space='MT'] inside it and hyperlink them. It can be useful for architects and system engineers.The same applies to SysML Block and IBD.

To create a structured class

1. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MT'] .
2. In the class diagram toolbar, click the arrow next to the Class symbol to reveal a list of available elements. Select the Structured Class element.
3. Click on the Class diagram. The class element with a hyperlink to a Composite Structure diagram is created.
4. Select the Class in the Browser to see its structure. To do this, choose Select in Containment Tree on the diagram pane from the Class [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Shortcut menu' space='CDH'] [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-21 at 6.19.54 PM.png']

In the Browser, you can see the created Class with the Composite Structure diagram inside it. The Class is linked with the diagram. It means that after double-clicking the diagram, or the Browser, the Composite Structure diagram will opened.

#### TIP: Hyperlinks

Hyperlinks

For more information about hyperlinks, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining hyperlinks' space='MT'].

The names of the Class and the Composite Structure diagram are synchronized. Type a name for the Class, for example, *Library Book*, and the name of the diagram is automatically changed to *Library Book* and vice versa. This is synchronization of a diagram name and its context name.

#### TIP: Diagram Name and Context Name

Diagram Name and Context Name

For more information about diagram name and its context synchronization, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram name and its context name synchronization' space='MT'].

#### PANEL: Related References

Related References

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><span>There is a way to create a piece of the model with a single click: <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link>, with a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link> inside it and hyperlink them. It can be useful for architects and system engineers. </span>The same applies to SysML Block and IBD.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a structured class</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Class diagram" /></ac:link>.</li><li>In the class diagram toolbar, click the arrow next to the Class symbol to reveal a list of available elements. Select the Structured Class element.</li><li>Click on the Class diagram. The class element with a <strong>hyperlink</strong> to a Composite Structure diagram is created.</li><li>Select the Class in the <strong>Browser</strong> to see its structure. To do this, choose <strong>Select in Containment Tree</strong> on the diagram pane from the Class<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CDH" ri:content-title="Shortcut menu" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ shortcut menu.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-21 at 6.19.54 PM.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>In the Browser, you can see the created Class with the Composite Structure diagram inside it. The Class is linked with the diagram. It means that after double-clicking the diagram, or the Browser, the Composite Structure diagram will opened.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f90c2d5-bdf5-41c2-aae9-e01f07133e28"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Hyperlinks</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about hyperlinks, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Defining hyperlinks" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Defining Hyperlinks]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The names of the Class and the Composite Structure diagram are synchronized. Type a name for the Class, for example, <em>Library Book</em>, and the name of the diagram is automatically changed to <em>Library Book</em> and vice versa. This is synchronization of a diagram name and its context name.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="db8876de-7036-4618-a01f-a1911bf3ca9b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Diagram Name and Context Name</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about diagram name and its context synchronization, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Diagram name and its context name synchronization" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Diagram Name and its Context Name Synchronization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b04b6584-a056-4c65-81bb-e606f1b7ca2c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968071 space=MED version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00131: Creating an alternative flow

- page_id: `243968071`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243968071/Creating+an+alternative+flow
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:49.335+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Use Case > Use Case Scenario sketch
- labels: ['alternative-condition', 'alternative-flow-step']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

879536389

879536391

879536390

To create an alternative flow

1. Open the Use Case Specification window and click the Use Case Scenario Sketch property group.
2. Select the basic flow step you want to create an alternative flow for.
3. In the Alternative Flow tab, click the Add new button [ATTACHMENT filename='add_new_value_button.png'] . The shortcut menu opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='adding_alternative_flow.png']
4. Do one of the following:

- 
  - Select the **Add New Alternative Condition**command or press ALT+R to add a new alternative condition. A new alternative condition is created together with the alternative flow step. Type the name of the alternative condition. The alternative condition for the basic flow is created.
  - Select the Add New Alternative Flow Step command. Press ALT+ENTER to add a new alternative flow step. If there was an alternative condition created in the alternative flow already, a new alternative flow step is created for the selected alternative condition. If in the alternative flow there was no alternative condition, a new alternative flow step is created together with a new alternative condition. Now you can type the name of the new alternative flow step.

Conditions and steps of the alternative flow are numbered automatically. Use the Up [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and Down [IMAGE alt='' src=''] buttons to change the order of conditions in the alternative flow or the order of steps in a particular condition. In the Activity diagram, steps are represented according to their order in the Use Case scenario.

879536388

The shortcut menu opens.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="adding_alternative_flow.png" /></ac:image></li><li><p>Do one of the following:</p></li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><p>Select the <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Add New Alternative Condition </strong>command or press ALT+R to add a new alternative condition. A new alternative condition is created together with the alternative flow step. Type the name of the alternative condition. The alternative condition for the basic flow is created.</p></li><li>Select the <strong>Add New Alternative Flow Step </strong>command. Press ALT+ENTER to add a new alternative flow step. If there was an alternative condition created in the alternative flow already, a new alternative flow step is created for the selected alternative condition. If in the alternative flow there was no alternative condition, a new alternative flow step is created together with a new alternative condition. Now you can type the name of the new alternative flow step.</li></ul></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Conditions and steps of the alternative flow are numbered automatically. Use the Up <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_up_selected_row_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and Down <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_down_selected_row.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> buttons to change the order of conditions in the alternative flow or the order of steps in a particular condition. In the Activity diagram, steps are represented according to their order in the Use Case scenario.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="08d48bca-7a36-46cb-a6dc-8a7cef6561fb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">879536388</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Transition" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968073 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00132: Creating an exceptional flow

- page_id: `243968073`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243968073/Creating+an+exceptional+flow
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:49.421+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Use Case > Use Case Scenario sketch
- labels: ['exception-type', 'exception-flow-step']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

879686455

879686457

879686456

INLINE

To create an exceptional flow

1. Open the Use Case Specification window and click the Use Case Scenario Sketch property group.
2. In the use case scenario, select the basic flow step you want to create an exceptional flow for.
3. In the Exceptional Flow tab, click the Add New button [ATTACHMENT filename='add_new_value_button.png'] . The shortcut menu opens. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Do one of the following:

- 
  - Select the **Add New Exception Type**command or press Alt+T. The drop down list opens. Type the name of a new exception type. The exception type is created (a new class is created). The created exception type contains an unnamed exceptional flow step.
  - Select the **Add New Exceptional Flow Step**command or press Alt+Enter. If there was an exceptional type created in the exceptional flow already, a new exceptional flow step is created for the selected exception type. If there was no exception type in the alternative flow, a new exceptional flow step is created together with a new exception type. Now you can type the name of the new exceptional flow step.

Types and steps of the exceptional flow are numbered automatically. Use the Up [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and Down [IMAGE alt='' src=''] buttons to change the order of types in the exceptional flow or the order of steps in the particular type. In the Activity diagram, steps are represented according to their order in the Use Case scenario.

879686454

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967540 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00133: Creating Class Elements

- page_id: `243967540`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967540/Creating+Class+Elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:34.895+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Class
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''] is drawn as a solid-outline rectangle with three compartments separated by horizontal lines. The top name compartment holds the class name and other general properties of the Class (including [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='']); the middle list compartment holds a list of properties; the bottom list compartment holds a list of operations. The property and operation compartments are optional, so you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying and suppressing compartments on shapes' space='MT'] them.

To create a class element

1. On the left side of your model, find the available options under the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MT'] toolbar and click on Class.
2. You will see an arrow for a drop-down menu which you can click if you wish to create a different kind of Class element.
3. In any case, either At this point, you should have a Class element in your Class diagram, as shown in figure below. [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-21 at 6.22.38 PM.png']
  1. Click on the element and then click anywhere on your diagram pane, or
  2. Drag and drop, the element you clicked on, anywhere on your diagram pane.

#### PANEL: Related References

Related References

true[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='']

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967789 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00134: Creating Signal receptions for Messages

- page_id: `243967789`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967789/Creating+Signal+receptions+for+Messages
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:41.482+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Message
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

372415134

372415137

372415136

INLINE

Assigning a Signal reception to a Message is very similar to the procedure of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assigning Operations to Messages' space=''].

These two conditions must be satisfied when creating a new Signal reception:

- At least one Signal must exist in your project.
- A possible Signal reception receiver (an activation to which the Message points) must have a type assigned.

To create a new signal reception for the message

1. Draw a send Message between the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Lifeline' space=''] or select an existing one on a diagram pane.
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assigning Signals to Messages' space=''] .
3. Do one of the following:

- 
  - Click the button in the shape of a small red circle at the end of the Message name. Define the Signal reception property values in the Signal reception [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']. The default name of the Signal reception is the name of the Message for which the Signal reception is being created. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - From the Message shortcut menu, select the **Create New Signal Reception** (the command with the small red circle icon, as shown above). Define Signal reception property values in the Signal reception [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']. The default name of the Signal reception is the name of the Message for which the Signal reception is being created.

372415133

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='']

assigned.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To create a new signal reception for the message</p><hr /><ol><li>Draw a send Message between the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Lifeline" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lifelines]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or select an existing one on a diagram pane.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Assigning Signals to Messages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Assign a Signal to the Message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Do one of the following:</li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>Click the button in the shape of a small red circle at the end of the Message name. Define the Signal reception property values in the Signal  <span>reception <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. The default name of the Signal reception is the </span><span>name of the Message for which the Signal reception is being created.</span><br /><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="smart_manipulator_signal_reception.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><p>From the Message shortcut menu, select the <strong>Create New Signal Reception</strong> (the command with the small red circle icon, as shown above). Define Signal reception property values in the Signal reception <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. The default name of the Signal reception is the name of the Message for which the Signal reception is being created.</p></li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71991a59-a78f-47e0-93c9-f2435821a19c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">372415133</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Lifeline" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lifelines]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interaction Use" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Interaction]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operation" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Message" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967920 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00135: Creating Slots on Link creation automatically

- page_id: `243967920`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967920/Creating+Slots+on+Link+creation+automatically
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:45.438+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Slot
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When creating [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Link' space=''] between two [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='']s, slot creation for an Instance is suggested automatically.

1. On the diagram draw two [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''] shapes - A and B Classes.
2. Draw an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space=''] with roles between them.
3. Draw two Instance shapes. Define Class A as a classifier of the first Instance and Class B as a classifier of the second Instance.
4. Draw a Link between Instances. The Select Association dialog will open.
5. The association which is drawn between Classes will be automatically selected in the Select Association dialog. Click OK .
6. The Create Slots dialog will open. Select Association ends. Ends of Association which is drawn between class A and Class B will be selected automatically in the Create Slots dialog. Click **OK**. Slots for Instance will be created.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">When creating <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Link" /></ac:link> between two <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Instance Specification" /></ac:link>s, slot creation for an Instance is suggested automatically. </span></p><ol><li class="kadov-p">On the diagram draw two <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link> shapes - A and B Classes.</li><li class="kadov-p">Draw an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Association" /></ac:link> with roles between them.</li><li class="kadov-p">Draw two Instance shapes. Define Class A as a classifier of the first Instance and Class B as a classifier of the second Instance.</li><li class="kadov-p">Draw a Link between Instances. The <span class="hcp1">Select Association</span> dialog will open.</li><li class="kadov-p">The association which is drawn between Classes will be automatically selected in the <span class="hcp1">Select Association</span> dialog. Click <strong><span class="hcp1">OK</span></strong>.</li><li class="p"><p>The <span class="hcp1">Create Slots</span> dialog will open. Select Association ends. Ends of Association which is drawn between class A and Class B will be selected automatically in the <span class="hcp1">Create Slots</span> dialog. Click <strong><span class="hcp1">OK</span></strong>. Slots for Instance will be created.</p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967923 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00136: Creating smart packages

- page_id: `243967923`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967923/Creating+smart+packages
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:44:13.451+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Smart Package
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A smart package can be created in one of the following ways:

- From the shortcut menu of an element . The newly created smart package is empty.
- From the search results (by saving either the search options or the search results as a smart package). The newly created smart package has either dynamic or static contents.

To create a smart package from the shortcut menu of an element

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the element. The shortcut menu opens. The element should be a namespace, such as a package, a class, or a requirement.
2. Select Create Element > [ATTACHMENT filename='smart_package_button.png'] Smart Package .
3. Type a name for the new smart package.
4. Press Enter to finish.

The new smart package is created, and you can proceed to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing contents of smart package' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart package properties' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a smart package from the search results

1. Open the **Find**dialog, define search options, and perform the search. The search results are displayed in the **Search Results**tab on the Model Browser. For more information about defining search options and analyzing the search results, refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Find dialog' space='MT'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Search Results tab' space='MT'].
2. On the toolbar of the Search Results tab, click [ATTACHMENT filename='save_as_smart_package_button.png'] and select one of the following commands: [ATTACHMENT filename='save_query_as_smart_package.png'] The element Selection dialog opens.
  - Save Query as Smart Package to create a new smart package with dynamic contents. The search options will be saved as the Query property value.
  - Save Results as Smart Package to create a new smart package with static contents. The search results will be saved as values of the Additional Elements property.
3. In the dialog, select an owner of the new smart package and click OK .
4. In the Containment tree, type a name for the new smart package.
5. Press Enter to finish.

The new smart package is created, and you can proceed to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing contents of smart package' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart package properties' space=''].

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Profiles' space='MT']

The newly created smart package is empty.</li><li><strong>From the search results </strong>(by saving either the search options or the search results as a smart package). The newly created smart package has either dynamic or static contents.</li></ul><p> </p><p>To create a smart package from the shortcut menu of an element</p><hr /><ol><li><p>In the Containment tree, right-click the element. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967852 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00137: Data Store Node

- page_id: `243967852`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967852/Data+Store+Node
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:44:10.890+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Node
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Data Store Node keeps all tokens that enter it, copying them when they are chosen to be moved downstream. Incoming tokens containing a particular object replace any tokens in the object node that contains the particular object.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a Data Store Node

1. Expand the Activity diagram palette, click the downward arrow button next to the Object Node icon and select **Data Store Node** from the shortcut menu . [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Click on the Activity diagram pane. The Data Store Node is created.
3. (Optional) To specify the Data Store Node by changing its property values, right-click its symbol and open its Specification window .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A Data Store Node keeps all tokens that enter it, copying them when they are chosen to be moved downstream. Incoming tokens containing a particular object replace any tokens in the object node that contains the particular object.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Data_store_node.png" /></ac:image></p><p>To create a Data Store Node </p><hr /><ol><li>Expand the Activity diagram palette, click the downward arrow button next to the <strong>Object Node</strong><strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span></strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">icon and select <strong>Data Store Node</strong> from the shortcut menu</span>.<br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_data_store_node.png" /></ac:image></span></li><li>Click on the Activity diagram pane. The Data Store Node<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span>is created. </li><li>(Optional) <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To specify the Data Store Node by changing its property values, right-click its symbol and open its </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Specification+window">Specification window</a><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. </span></li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967620 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00138: Data Type

- page_id: `243967620`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967620/Data+Type
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:37.001+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Data Type is a type whose values have no identity; that is, they are pure values. It is a classifier and inherits the general features of the classifier: visibility, generalizable element properties, and operations.

The modeling tool provides the following predefined data types: boolean, byte, char, date, double, float, int, Integer, Real, long, short, void, and String.

You can also create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enumeration' space='']or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Primitive' space='']Data Types.

To create a new Data Type, including Enumeration or Primitive

- From the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MT'] , select New from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''] or Create Element from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Subsystem' space=''] , or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model' space=''] shortcut menu, and then select Data Type, Enumeration, or Primitive.
- In the Class, Package, Subsystem, and Model [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'] , the Inner Elements property group, click Create and select a Data Type.

A Data Type can be specified by changing its property values in the Data Type [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']. Each property is described in the description area on this window.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967623 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00139: Decision and Merge

- page_id: `243967623`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967623/Decision+and+Merge
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:44:16.465+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: ['decision', 'merge']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

943280858

943280861

943280859

A Decision Node in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT'] is used much like a choice or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Junction' space=''] point in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MT']. Decision nodes allow you to separate the Activity Edges. Merge nodes allow you to combine the Activity Edges together.

Decision Nodes are created using guard conditions. They help protect Activity Edges that depend on guard conditions.

1. The symbol used for the Decision Node is a large diamond shape. It can have one or two incoming Activity Edges, and at least one outgoing Activity Edge
2. The symbol used for the Merge Node is a large diamond shape. It can have exactly one outgoing Activity Edge but may have multiple incoming Activity Edges.

The Decision and Merge are represented by the same symbol. Although, you can set the **Use different Fork/Join and Decision/Merge****notations** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project options' space='MT'] to draw different notations for the**Decision**and**Merge**.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Activity diagram illustrates that when an order cancellation request is made (only while filling, receiving or shipping orders) - the Cancel Order behavior is invoked.

943280856

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Overview diagram' space='MT']
- Activity diagram
- Junction
- Model Elements

Decision nodes allow you to separate the Activity Edges. Merge nodes allow you to combine the Activity Edges together. </p><p>Decision Nodes are created using guard conditions. They help protect Activity Edges that depend on guard conditions.</p><ol><li>The symbol used for the Decision Node is a large diamond shape. It can have one or two incoming Activity Edges, and at least one outgoing Activity Edge</li><li>The symbol used for the Merge Node is a large diamond shape. It can have exactly one outgoing Activity Edge but may have multiple incoming Activity Edges.</li></ol><p>The Decision and Merge are represented by the same symbol. Although, you can set the <strong>Use different Fork/Join and Decision/Merge</strong><span><strong> notations</strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[project option]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to draw different notations for the </span><strong>Decision</strong><span> and </span><strong>Merge</strong><span>.</span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="decision-merge.png" /></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">Activity diagram illustrates that when an order cancellation request is made (only while filling, receiving or shipping orders) - the Cancel Order behavior is invoked.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="874d329f-6bcc-4364-b901-b6155101dd17"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">943280856</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Interaction Overview diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Activity+diagram">Activity diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Junction">Junction</a></li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Model+elements">Model Elements</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967966 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00140: Deep History

- page_id: `243967966`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967966/Deep+History
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:46.555+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > State Machine > Pseudo States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Deep History represents the most recent active configuration of the composite state that directly contains this pseudo state; e.g. the state configuration that was active when the composite state was last exited. A composite state can have at most one deep history vertex.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pseudo States' space='']

A composite state can have at most one deep history vertex.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36e20852-22dd-420b-8d4b-52b136b22981"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f01b84d9-d9d1-4049-8315-15a6a05d850d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="7e19d119-2f65-4f2a-b944-60c34f7c4ba8"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Pseudo States" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
