<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967626 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00143: Dependency

- page_id: `243967626`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967626/Dependency
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:44:15.650+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Editing property values

745065613

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Editing property values

745065615

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Editing property values

745065614

A dependency is a relationship signifying that a [model element](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Model+elements) requires other model elements for its specification or implementation. The client element is either semantically or structurally dependent on the definition of the supplier element(s).

A dependency is shown as a dashed arrow between classes or packages. The client element (at the tail of the arrow) depends on the supplier element (at the arrowhead). You can label the arrow with an optional stereotype and an individual name.

You can also draw a dependency between a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''] and other Class elements, such as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Attribute' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of dependency relationships: «access» and «import».

Dependency, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Navigating between different levels of abstraction' space='MT'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing usages and dependencies' space='MT'] are defined in the dialog of the same structure. They differ from one another only by the corresponding Specification name. You can specify a dependency by changing its property values in the dependency [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']. Each property is described in the description area on this window.

#### TIP: Editing property values

Editing property values

For more information about specifying property values, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MT'].

There are several kinds of dependencies:

| Dependency kind | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Template binding | Represents a relationship between a templatable element and a template. A template binding specifies the substitutions of actual parameters for the formal parameters of the template.You can specify template binding dependency properties and find each property's description in the template binding dependency Specification window. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window. |
| Abstraction | Relates two elements or sets of elements that represent the same concept at different levels of abstraction or from different viewpoints. In the metamodel, an abstraction is a dependency in which there is a mapping between the supplier and the client.Define an abstraction relationship in the Abstraction Specification window. |
| Usage | A relationship in which one element requires another element (or set of elements) for its full implementation or . In the metamodel, a usage is a dependency in which the client requires the presence of the supplier.Define a usage relationship in the Usage Specification window. |
| Package merge | A directed relationship between two , indicating that the contents of the two packages are to be combined. It has a dependency relationship with the applied stereotype «merge».Define a merge relationship in the Dependency Specification window. |
| Element import | A directed relationship between an importing namespace and a packageable element. The name of the packageable element or its alias is added to the namespace of the importing namespace. It has a dependency relationship with the applied stereotype «import».Define an import relationship in the Dependency Specification window. To draw the Element Import link, select the Element Import path to draw in the Class diagram toolbar from the Abstraction group. |
| Access | Shows that elements can only be accessed from a , and it cannot be referenced. |
| Deployment | The allocation of a deployment target to an artifact or artifact instance. |
| Role Binding | A relationship in a . Role Binding is used to connect with . |
| Mount | A mount dependency represents a former location of a shared package; that is, a location where the package was mounted in a local project.A mount dependency is created:After you export a project from Teamwork Cloud; orWhen a local project is added to Teamwork Cloud.Mounted packages in the Containment tree are adorned with an icon and have their project usage name in brackets, as shown in the following figure:The purpose of establishing the mount dependency is to ensure the same containment structure during project transformation to Teamwork Cloud, to maintain the correct scope in utilities like smart packages, validation rules, tables etc. |

745065612

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f239384e-b503-4fb4-9f1e-b7211948cf8b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">745065613</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8e87a6e-081f-4e53-812d-9265a4881a1b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">745065615</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6eab5bf6-1140-4dc9-8eeb-0a1f913f5911"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">745065614</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A dependency is a relationship signifying that a <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Model+elements">model element</a> requires other model elements for its specification or implementation. The client element is either semantically or structurally dependent on the definition of the supplier element(s).</p><p>A dependency is shown as a dashed arrow between classes or packages. The client element (at the tail of the arrow) depends on the supplier element (at the arrowhead). You can label the arrow with an optional stereotype and an individual name.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="478fd602-4249-41b7-b0b9-3453d0416c17"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also draw a dependency between a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link> and other Class elements, such as <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Attribute" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[attributes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[operations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> <ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="example_of_dependencies.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of dependency relationships: «access» and «import».</h6><p>Dependency, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Navigating between different levels of abstraction" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[abstraction]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Analyzing usages and dependencies" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[usage relationships]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> are defined in the dialog of the same structure. They differ from one another only by the corresponding Specification name. You can specify a dependency by changing its property values in the dependency <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. Each property is described in the description area on this window.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be0793a2-8907-4c1a-bc47-4d1ec1f673c3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Editing property values</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For more information about specifying property values, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Editing property values" /></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>There are several kinds of dependencies:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Dependency kind</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><h4>Template binding</h4></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Represents a relationship between a templatable element and a template. A template binding specifies the substitutions of actual parameters for the formal parameters of the template.</p><p>You can specify template binding dependency properties and find each property's description in the template binding dependency Specification window. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window. </p></div></td></tr><tr><td><h4>Abstraction</h4></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Relates two elements or sets of elements that represent the same concept at different levels of abstraction or from different viewpoints. In the metamodel, an abstraction is a dependency in which there is a mapping between the supplier and the client.</p><p>Define an abstraction relationship in the Abstraction Specification window. </p></div></td></tr><tr><td><h4>Usage</h4></td><td><p>A relationship in which one element requires another element (or set of elements) for its full implementation or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[operation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. In the metamodel, a usage is a dependency in which the client requires the presence of the supplier.</p><p>Define a usage relationship in the Usage Specification window. </p></td></tr><tr><td><h4>Package merge</h4></td><td><p>A directed relationship between two <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[packages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, indicating that the contents of the two packages are to be combined. It has a dependency relationship with the applied stereotype <em>«merge»</em>.</p><p>Define a merge relationship in the Dependency Specification window. </p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><h4>Element import</h4></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>A directed relationship between an importing namespace and a packageable <span style="line-height: 1.42857;">element. The name of the packageable element or its alias is added to the namespace of the importing </span>namespace. It has a dependency relationship with the applied stereotype <em>«import»</em>.</p><p>Define an import relationship in the Dependency Specification window. </p><p>To draw the Element Import link, select the Element Import path to draw in the Class diagram toolbar from the Abstraction group. </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="element_import.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><h4>Access</h4></td><td colspan="1"><p>Shows that elements can only be accessed from a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[package]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and it cannot be referenced.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><h4>Deployment</h4></td><td colspan="1"><p>The allocation of a deployment target to an artifact or artifact instance.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><h4><span>Role Binding </span></h4></td><td colspan="1"><p>A relationship in a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link>. Role Binding is used to connect <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Parts]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Collaboration Use" /></ac:link>.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><h4>Mount</h4></div></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>A mount dependency represents a former location of a shared package; that is, a location where the package was mounted in a local project.</p><p>A mount dependency is created:</p><ul><li>After you export a project from Teamwork Cloud; or</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">When a local project is added to Teamwork Cloud.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="41e837f4-1df3-443e-b319-ad93d7c8ca3c"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Mounted packages in the Containment tree are <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a58e89da-d5aa-4ecd-aa7e-a435ec3ba003">adorned</ac:inline-comment-marker> with an icon and have their project usage name in brackets, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">as shown in the following figure:</span></span></p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Mounted_projects.png" /></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><p><span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a1597fed-3bf8-4a17-95a7-b2b374fabeb2">The purpose of establishing the mount dependency is to ensure the same containment structure during project transformation to Teamwork Cloud,</ac:inline-comment-marker></span> to maintain the correct scope in utilities like smart packages, validation rules, tables etc.</p></div></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="306dc793-e505-4955-9705-9586072be117"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">745065612</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="194a0c61-0fa3-49ba-bbd9-e99868580996" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967509 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00144: Deployment

- page_id: `243967509`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967509/Deployment
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:41:53.342+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Artifact
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

907499974

907499976

907499975

The Deployment Specification is a type of Artifact. The Deployment Specification is a general mechanism to parameterize a Deployment relationship.

To draw the Deployment Specification on the diagram pane

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Component diagram' space='MT'] (or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deployment diagram' space='MT'] ) diagram toolbar, click the Deployment Diagram button group to access the drop-down menu.
2. In the opened list, select Deployment Specification Instance and draw it on your diagram pane.

To specify the Deployment relationship

1. Create the Deployment Specification on the diagram pane.
2. Between the Deployment relationship and Deployment Specification, draw the Dependency relationship. The Dependency is drawn without an arrow. [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-23 at 5.52.38 PM.png']

907499973

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Component diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deployment diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Artifacts' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with dependencies' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249005174 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00145: Derive

- page_id: `249005174`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/249005174/Derive
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:37:53.872+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > Requirement-related elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A 'Derive' relationship is a dependency between two requirements (a derived requirement and a source requirement), where the derived requirement is generated or inferred from the source requirement.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target">A 'Derive' relationship is a dependency between two requirements (a derived requirement and a source requirement), where the derived requirement is generated or inferred from the source requirement.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249005166 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00146: Design Constraint

- page_id: `249005166`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/249005166/Design+Constraint
- version_number: 2

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967650 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00150: Device

- page_id: `243967650`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967650/Device
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:44:11.102+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Device is a physical computational resource, such as a piece of hardware (a desktop computer, processor, or server), or a human work unit (a department or team). A device is commonly composed of other devices. Composition may be modeled by placing the contained device in the node icon representing the containing device.

A Devicecan be specified by changing its property values in the Device[Specification window](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Specification+window). Each property is described in the description area on this window.

The Device DeviceSpecification windowconsists of the following specific specification panes:

- Nested Nodes : lists nodes defined (nested) within the device.
- Deployed Artifacts : an artifact or artifact instance that has been deployed to a deployment target.

You can format the Device symbol properties in the[Symbol Properties dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Formatting+symbols).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

A device is commonly composed of other devices. Composition may be modeled by placing the contained device in the node icon representing the containing device.      </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">A <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Device </span>can be specified by changing its property values in the <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Device</span> </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Specification+window">Specification window</a><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. Each property is described in the description area on this window. </span></span></p><p><span class="hcp1">The</span> <span>Device</span> <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Device</span> </span>Specification window<span class="hcp1"> consists of the following specific specification panes:</span></p><ul><li class="kadov-p"><span class="hcp2"><span>Nested</span></span> <span><span>Nodes</span></span><span class="hcp1">: lists</span><span class="hcp1"> nodes</span> <span class="kadov-p">defined (nested) within the device.</span></li><li class="kadov-p"><span class="hcp2"><span>Deployed</span></span> <span class="hcp2"><span>Artifacts</span></span><span class="hcp1">:</span> an artifact or artifact instance that has been deployed to a deployment target.</li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can format the Device symbol properties in the </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Formatting+symbols">Symbol Properties dialog</a><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">.</span></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=9919428 space=MED version=8 -->
## PAGE 00151: Directed Feature

- page_id: `9919428`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/9919428/Directed+Feature
- version_number: 8
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:46:24.287+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > SysML v1 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A directed feature is a feature which applies the «DirectedFeature» stereotype. It specifies that the feature is provided, required, or both required and provided by an owning block. Directed features can appear in compartments for the various kinds of properties and behavioral features.

#### PANEL: Related diagrams

Related diagrams

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='MT']
- [Specifying feature directions](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Specifying+feature+directions)

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

It specifies that the feature is provided, required, or both required and provided by an owning block. Directed features can appear in compartments for the various kinds of properties and behavioral features.</p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ad1d2f29-7ddc-4427-be11-2022073a6b8a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related diagrams</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="SysML Internal Block Diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Specifying+feature+directions" class="current">Specifying feature directions</a></p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=9919434 space=MED version=3 -->
## PAGE 00152: Distributed Property

- page_id: `9919434`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/9919434/Distributed+Property
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:46:24.471+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > SysML v1 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Distributed Property is a property of a Block or a Value Type, used to apply a probability distribution to the values of the property. Specific distributions can be defined by applying a subclass of the *DistributedProperty* stereotype to the property.

#### PANEL: Related diagrams

Related diagrams

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Specific distributions can be defined by applying a subclass of the <em>DistributedProperty</em> stereotype to the property.</p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ad1d2f29-7ddc-4427-be11-2022073a6b8a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related diagrams</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="SysML Internal Block Diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p> </ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249579682 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00153: Doctrine

- page_id: `249579682`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/249579682/Doctrine
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-29T15:04:26.659+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A formal expression of military knowledge and thought, accepted as being relevant at a given time, which covers the nature of conflict, the preparation of the military forces for conflict, and the method of engaging in conflict to achieve success. it is descriptive rather than prescriptive, requiring judgment in application.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

Class

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;">A formal expression of military knowledge and thought, accepted as being relevant at a given time, which covers the nature of conflict, the preparation of the military forces for conflict, and the method of engaging in conflict to achieve success. it is descriptive rather than prescriptive, requiring judgment in application.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;"><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;">UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph" style="text-align: left;">Class</p>
````

299228950

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Visibility representation

299228953

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Visibility representation

299228951

A Domain block represents an entity, a concept, a location, or a person from the real-world domain. A domain block is part of the system knowledge.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Visibility representation

Visibility representation

In SysML, properties and operations of the Block are public. Visibility representation literals, like +, #, ~, -, are not displayed in the Containment tree or in the element symbol on a diagram.

299228949

**Related pages**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968015 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00157: Editing tagged value

- page_id: `243968015`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243968015/Editing+tagged+value
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:47.900+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Tag
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a new tagged value, do one of the following

- In the element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'] , Tags property group, select an available tag definition and click the Create Value button.
- In the Containment tree, select an element and drag it to the Specification window tag value area.

To create default tag values

1. In the stereotype's Specification window, expand the Tag Definitions property group and select the tag definition.
2. Assign a value for the Default Value property.
3. Create an element and assign a stereotype for this element. The element will have tags with assigned default values.

##### Setting a default tag value to tag with empty value

Modeling tool allows for setting default tag values to a tag with an empty value. This functionality is needed when a stereotype is already assigned to an element and a new mandatory tag definition with a default value is created for the stereotype. After creating such a tag definition, the model elements that have the modified stereotype applied will have the newly created tags unset.

To set default values instead of empty values

1. In the main menu, select Tools > Set Empty Tags to Defaults . The Select Package dialog opens.
2. Select the scope of elements to which you want to set the default tag values. If you want to assign default values to all project tags, in the *All* data tree select the root package *Data* and click the **Add All** button. The root package *Data* is added to the **Selected** objects list. Click **OK**.
3. In the opened dialog, click Yes . Now, the element with stereotype tags has been assigned default values.

The following conditions are required to set the default tag values

- The element should have an assigned stereotype with a specified default value property.
- The tag definition, to which you want to assign a default value, should contain no value.

The default tag values are not set when the stereotype property (tag definition) multiplicity is equal to 0, 0..1, 0..*, *.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tag' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a new tagged value, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>In the element <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, <strong>Tags</strong> property group, select an available tag definition and click the <strong>Create Value</strong> button.</li><li>In the Containment tree, select an element and drag it to the Specification window tag value area.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To create default tag values</p><hr /><ol><li>In the stereotype's Specification window, expand the <strong>Tag Definitions</strong> property group and select the tag definition.</li><li>Assign a value for the <strong>Default Value</strong> property.</li><li>Create an element and assign a stereotype for this element. The element will have tags with assigned default values.</li></ol><h3><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="66458d2c-4a05-4c4b-a183-c28d967c3755">Setting a default tag value to tag with empty value</ac:inline-comment-marker></h3><p>Modeling tool allows for setting default tag values to a tag with an empty value. This functionality is needed when a stereotype is already assigned to an element and a new mandatory tag definition with a default value is created for the stereotype. After creating such a tag definition, the model elements that have the modified stereotype applied will have the newly created tags unset.</p><p><br /></p><p>To set default values instead of empty values</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Set Empty Tags to Defaults</strong>. The <strong>Select Package</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p>Select the scope of elements to which you want to set the default tag values.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b9a5e19f-c7a9-498a-89c3-bf18e7f08f41"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to assign default values to all project tags, in the <em>All</em> data tree select the root package <em>Data</em> and click the <strong>Add All</strong> button. The root package <em>Data</em> is added to the <strong>Selected</strong> objects list. Click <strong>OK</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>In the opened dialog, click <strong>Yes</strong>. Now, the element with stereotype tags has been assigned default values.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The following conditions are required to set the default tag values</p><ul><li>The element should have an assigned stereotype with a specified default value property.</li><li>The tag definition, to which you want to assign a default value, should contain no value.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d6cde589-7fae-4130-931b-7f8d66886565"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The default tag values are not set when the stereotype property (tag definition) multiplicity is equal to 0, 0..1, 0..*, *.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a1cb6614-da6a-4a7b-adf1-3ec18e11a18c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Stereotype" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Tag" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=228985941 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00158: Effect

- page_id: `228985941`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228985941/Effect
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:30:21.814+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A kind of phenomenon that follows and is caused by some previous phenomenon that could lead to downstream effects or to one or more desired outcomes.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

Class

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">A kind of phenomenon that follows and is caused by some previous phenomenon that could lead to downstream effects or to one or more desired outcomes.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extensions</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Class</p>
````

## PAGE 00160: Enhanced Security Control

- space_key: `MED`

- labels: []

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Activity

## PAGE 00162: Enterprise Goal

- space_key: `MED`

- version_number: 2

- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A statement about a state or condition of the enterprise to be brought about or sustained through appropriate Means. An Enterprise Goal amplifies an Enterprise Vision that is, it indicates what must be satisfied on a continuing basis to effectively attain the Enterprise Vision.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Class

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967964 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00166: Entry Point

- page_id: `243967964`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967964/Entry+Point
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:46.359+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > State Machine > Pseudo States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An entry point connection point reference as the target of a transition implies that the target of the transition is the entry point pseudo state as defined in the submachine of the submachine state. As a result, the regions of the submachine state machine are entered at the corresponding entry point pseudo states.

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']

As a result, the regions of the submachine state machine are entered at the corresponding entry point pseudo states.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36e20852-22dd-420b-8d4b-52b136b22981"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f01b84d9-d9d1-4049-8315-15a6a05d850d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="7e19d119-2f65-4f2a-b944-60c34f7c4ba8"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Pseudo States" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967621 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00167: Enumeration

- page_id: `243967621`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967621/Enumeration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:37.036+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Data Type
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Enumeration defines a kind of data type whose range is a list of predefined values called Enumeration Literals. An Enumeration can contain Operations, but they must be pure functions (this is the rule for all data type elements).

An Enumeration can be specified by changing its property values in the Enumeration [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']. Each property is described in the description area on this window.

##### Enumeration Literals

Enumeration Literal defines an element of the run-time extension of an Enumeration data type. It has no relevant substructure, therefore, it is atomic.

To create an Enumeration Literal, do one of the following

- Select the Enumeration shape, click the Create Element button [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and then select **Enumeration Literal**.
- In the Containment tree, right-click the Enumeration and select Create Element > Enumeration Literal .

##### Enumeration Attributes

To create an Attribute for the Enumeration, do one of the following

- Select the Enumeration shape, click the Create Element button [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and then select **Property**.
- In the Containment tree, right-click the Enumeration and select Create Element > Property .

As you can see, the Attribute is by default read-only.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Data Type' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Enumeration defines a kind of data type whose range is a list of predefined values called Enumeration Literals. An Enumeration can contain Operations, but they must be pure functions (this is the rule for all data type elements).</p><p>An Enumeration can be specified by changing its property values in the Enumeration <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. Each property is described in the description area on this window.</p><h3>Enumeration Literals</h3><p>Enumeration Literal defines an element of the run-time extension of an Enumeration data type. It has no relevant substructure, therefore, it is atomic.</p><p> </p><p>To create an Enumeration Literal, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li><p>Select the Enumeration shape, click the Create Element button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>, and then select <strong>Enumeration Literal</strong>.</p></li><li>In the Containment tree, right-click the Enumeration and select <strong>Create Element</strong> &gt; - page_id: `228985949`
- space_key: `MED`

A definition of the environmental factors in which something exists or functions. The definition of an Environment element can be further defined using Environment Kind.

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Data Type

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=228985950 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00169: Environment Kind

- page_id: `228985950`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228985950/Environment+Kind
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:30:32.349+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Enumeration of the possible kinds of Environment. Its enumeration literals are:

- TerrainType - Indicates that the Environment associated with EnvironmentKind captures a kind of terrain used to describe the terrain state of an environment at a particular time (e.g., muddy, frozen ground, deep snow, etc.).
- WeatherConditions - Indicates that the Environment associated with EnvironmentKind captures a kind of weather condition (e.g., Typhoon, Hurricane, Very Hot, Humid, etc.).
- LightConditions - Indicates that the Environment associated with EnvironmentKind captures a kind of light condition (e.g., broad daylight, dusk, moonlit, etc.).
- CBRNEnvironment - Indicates that the Environment associated with EnvironmentKind is of a Chemical, Biological, Radiological, or Nuclear (CBRN) kind.
- SituationType - Indicates that the Environment associated with EnvironmentKind captures a kind of situation used to describe the types and levels of threat (e.g., Corrosive, Fire, Smoke, Peaceful, etc.).

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description </strong></p><p>Enumeration of the possible kinds of Environment. Its enumeration literals are:</p><ul><li>TerrainType - Indicates that the Environment associated with EnvironmentKind captures a kind of terrain used to describe the terrain state of an environment at a particular time (e.g., muddy, frozen ground, deep snow, etc.).</li><li>WeatherConditions - Indicates that the Environment associated with EnvironmentKind captures a kind of weather condition (e.g., Typhoon, Hurricane, Very Hot, Humid, etc.). </li><li>LightConditions - Indicates that the Environment associated with EnvironmentKind captures a kind of light condition (e.g., broad daylight, dusk, moonlit, etc.). </li><li>CBRNEnvironment - Indicates that the Environment associated with EnvironmentKind is of a Chemical, Biological, Radiological, or Nuclear (CBRN) kind. </li><li>SituationType - Indicates that the Environment associated with EnvironmentKind captures a kind of situation used to describe the types and levels of threat (e.g., Corrosive, Fire, Smoke, Peaceful, etc.).</li></ul><p><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p>UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</p>
````

- space_key: `MED`

A property of an Environment that is typed by a Condition. The kinds of Condition that can be represented are Location, GeoPoliticalExtentType and Environment.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Property

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=9919455 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00171: Environmental Effect

- page_id: `9919455`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/9919455/Environmental+Effect
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:46:24.886+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > SysML v1 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Environmental Effect is an influence on the system from the environment without communicating with it directly.

#### TIP: Example

Example

- Temperature or Humidity.

#### PANEL: Related diagrams

Related diagrams

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Use Case Diagram' space='MT']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967651 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00172: Event

- page_id: `243967651`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967651/Event
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:37.797+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An event is the specification of some occurrence that may potentially trigger effects by an object, that is, an event shows what should happen to change a particular [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='']in a system. There are the following event types:

Events are important in diagrams which represent a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space='']of a system. These diagrams are listed in the following table.

| Diagram name | Event usage description |
| --- | --- |
|  | To specify an event type for the . |
|  | To specify a event type for the , or transition to self. |
|  | To specify an event type for the protocol transition, or protocol transition to self. |

When specifying an event type for a transition, you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assigning Event Type' space=''] on the diagram pane. The same assignment is valid for a transition to self, protocol transition, and protocol transition to self.

Event types, their functions, samples, and command syntax are described in the following table.

| Name | Function and Sample | Command syntax |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Any Receive Event | A trigger for an AnyReceiveEvent is triggered by the receipt of any that is not explicitly handled by any related trigger. | all |
| Call Event | A call event specifies the receipt by an object of a invoking a call of an :In this example, the call event type is specified with the create() operation. | <operation ()> |
| Change Event | A change event specifies a change in the system configuration that makes a condition true:In this example, the change event type is specified, and its Change expression property is specified as phone is busy. | when (<expression>) |
| Signal Event | A signal event represents the receipt of an asynchronous signal instance. A signal event may, for example, cause a statemachine to trigger a transition:In this example, the signal event type is specified, and its Signal property is specified as phone answered. | <signal name> |
| Time Event | A time event specifies a point in time. At the specified time, the event occurs. There are two possible types of eventoccurrences: at the relative time and at the absolute time:In this example, the relative time event is specified, and its When property is specified as 90 sec.For more information, see . | after (<time>) - an event occurrence at the relative timeat (<time>) - an event occurrence at the absolute time |

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol State Machine diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>An event is the specification of some occurrence that may potentially trigger effects by an object, that is, an event shows what should happen to change a particular <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="State" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[state ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>in a system. There are the following event types:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="053d6b32-00ff-45c6-89f3-6ad529a5e88c" /></p><p>Events are important in diagrams which represent a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Behavior" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[behavior ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>of a system. These diagrams are listed in the following table.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Diagram name</th><th>Event usage description</th></tr><tr><td><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></td><td>To specify an event type for the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Accept Event Action" /></ac:link>.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /></ac:link></td><td colspan="1">To specify a event type for the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Transition" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[transition]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, or transition to self.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Protocol State Machine diagram" /></ac:link></td><td colspan="1"><p>To specify an event type for the protocol transition, or protocol transition to self.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p>When specifying an event type for a transition, you can <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Assigning Event Type" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[type the command straight on the transition path]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the diagram pane. The same assignment is valid for a transition to self, protocol transition, and protocol transition to self.</p><p>Event types, their functions, samples, and command syntax are described in the following table.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Name</th><th>Function and Sample</th><th>Command syntax</th></tr><tr><td><h4>Any Receive Event</h4></td><td><p>A trigger for an AnyReceiveEvent is triggered by the receipt of any <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>that is not explicitly handled by any related trigger.</p></td><td><em>all</em></td></tr><tr><td><h4>Call Event</h4></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>A call event specifies the receipt by an object of a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>invoking a call of an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[operation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="event5.png" /></ac:image></p><p>In this example, the call event type is specified with the <em>create()</em> operation.</p></div></td><td><em>&lt;operation ()&gt;</em></td></tr><tr><td><h4>Change Event</h4></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>A change event specifies a change in the system configuration that makes a condition true:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="event7.png" /></ac:image></p><p>In this example, the change event type is specified, and its Change expression property is specified as <em>phone is busy</em>.</p></div></td><td><em>when (&lt;expression&gt;)</em></td></tr><tr><td><h4>Signal Event</h4></td><td><div class="content-wrapper">A signal event represents the receipt of an asynchronous signal instance. A signal event may, for example, cause a state<p>machine to trigger a transition:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="event8.png" /></ac:image></p><p>In this example, the signal event type is specified, and its Signal property is specified as <em>phone answered</em>.</p></div></td><td><em>&lt;signal name&gt;</em></td></tr><tr><td><h4>Time Event</h4></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>A time event specifies a point in time. At the specified time, the event occurs. There are two possible types of event<br />occurrences: at the relative time and at the absolute time:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="event11.png" /></ac:image></p><p>In this example, the relative time event is specified, and its When property is specified as <em>90 sec</em>.</p><p>For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying a time for a time event" /></ac:link>.</p></div></td><td><ul><li><em>after (&lt;time&gt;)</em> - an event occurrence at the relative time</li><li><em>at (&lt;time&gt;)</em> - an event occurrence at the absolute time</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3b6cf08a-97eb-41d3-bb72-a527d2b25d9e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="01b10abf-8c58-4f1a-bc27-6b5cc64511ee" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Protocol State Machine diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00174: Exception Handler

- page_id: `243967658`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967658/Exception+Handler
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:44:14.630+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

943151593

943151596

943151594

An Exception Handler element specifies a body to execute if the specified exception occurs during the execution of the protected node.

The Exception Handler may be drawn from an Output Pin to an Input Pin or from an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Action' space=''] to an Input Pin, as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Representation of an Exception Handler.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967661 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00176: Execution Environment

- page_id: `243967661`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967661/Execution+Environment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:38.170+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Execution Environment is a type of node that defines a set of services that support software. For example, an execution environment in the processor on a server might provide the operating system-level services needed to support a database application installed in that environment. Some typical stereotypes for an execution environment include *<OS>*, *<workflow engine>*, *<database system>*, and *<J2EE container>*. 
 
Specify the Execution Environment element in the Execution Environment [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'].

The Execution Environment dialog box contains the following specific panes:

- Nested Nodes : lists nodes defined (nested) in the Execution Environment .
- Deployed Artifacts : an artifact or artifact instance that has been deployed to a deployment target.

You can format the Execution Environment symbol properties in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MT'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">An Execution Environment is a type of node that defines a set of services that support software. For example, an execution environment in the processor on a server might provide the operating system-level services needed to support a database application installed in that environment. Some typical stereotypes for an execution environment include <em>&lt;OS&gt;</em>, <em>&lt;workflow engine&gt;</em>, <em>&lt;database system&gt;</em>, and <em>&lt;J2EE container&gt;</em>. </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">  </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Specify the Execution Environment element in the Execution Environment <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">.   </span></p><p><span class="hcp1">The</span> <span class="hcp2">Execution</span> <span class="hcp2">Environment</span> <span class="hcp1">dialog box contains the following specific panes:</span></p><ul><li class="kadov-p"><span class="hcp3"><span>Nested</span></span> <span class="hcp3"><span>Nodes</span></span><span class="hcp1">: lists</span><span class="hcp1"> nodes</span> <span class="hcp1">defined (nested) in the</span> Execution Environment<span class="hcp1">.</span></li><li class="kadov-p"><span class="hcp3"><span>Deployed</span></span> <span class="hcp3"><span>Artifacts</span></span><span class="hcp1">: </span>an artifact or artifact instance that has been deployed to a deployment target.</li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">You can format the Execution Environment symbol properties in the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /></ac:link><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">. </span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967965 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00178: Exit Point

- page_id: `243967965`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967965/Exit+Point
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:46.439+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > State Machine > Pseudo States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An exit point connection point reference as the source of a transition implies that the source of the transition is the exit point pseudo state as defined in the submachine of the submachine state that has the exit point connection point defined. When a region of the submachine state machine has reached the corresponding exit points, the submachine state exits at this exit point.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pseudo States' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>An exit point connection point reference as the source of a transition implies that the source of the transition is the exit point pseudo state as defined in the submachine of the submachine state that has the exit point connection point defined. When a region of the submachine state machine has reached the corresponding exit points, the submachine state exits at this exit point.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36e20852-22dd-420b-8d4b-52b136b22981"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f01b84d9-d9d1-4049-8315-15a6a05d850d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="7e19d119-2f65-4f2a-b944-60c34f7c4ba8"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Pseudo States" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967842 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00179: Expansion Region and Expansion Nodes

- page_id: `243967842`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967842/Expansion+Region+and+Expansion+Nodes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:42.599+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Node
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Expansion Region and Expansion Nodes can be drawn in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT'].

You can create those elements using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MT']diagram palette:

- The Expansion Region button. [ATTACHMENT filename='expansion_region.png']
- The Input and Output Expansion Nodes are located under the Object Node button group. [ATTACHMENT filename='expansion_nodes.png']

To display Expansion Nodes on the Expansion Region border:

1. Select the Expansion Region shape of the Expansion Nodes you want to display.
2. Perform one of the following steps: - From the shortcut menu, select Display > Display Expansion Nodes . - On the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select Display Expansion Nodes.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The Expansion Region and Expansion Nodes can be drawn in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link>.</p><p>You can create those elements using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">diagram palet</span>te</ac:link-body></ac:link>:</p><ul><li>The <strong>Expansion Region</strong> button.<br /><ac:image ac:title="The Expansion Region button" ac:alt="The Expansion Region button"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expansion_region.png" /></ac:image></li><li>The Input and Output Expansion Nodes are located under the <strong>Object Node</strong> button group.<br /><ac:image ac:title="The Input and Output Expansion Node buttons" ac:alt="The Input and Output Expansion Node buttons"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expansion_nodes.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ul><p>To display Expansion Nodes on the Expansion Region border:</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Expansion Region shape of the Expansion Nodes you want to display.</li><li>Perform one of the following steps:<br />- From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Expansion Nodes</strong>.<br />- On the diagram toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image></span> and select <strong>Display Expansion Nodes.</strong></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36e20852-22dd-420b-8d4b-52b136b22981"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=9919401 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00180: Expose

- page_id: `9919401`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/9919401/Expose
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:46:26.070+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > SysML v1 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Expose relationship relates a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='View' space=''] to one or more model elements. Each model element is an access point to initiate the query. The View and the model elements related to the View are passed to the constructor when it is invoked. The method describes how the exposed elements are navigated to extract the desired information.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related diagram

Related diagram

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Views and Viewpoints Diagram' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The Expose relationship relates a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="View" /></ac:link> to one or more model elements. Each model element is an access point to initiate the query. The View and the model elements related to the View are passed to the constructor when it is invoked. The method describes how the exposed elements are navigated to extract the desired information.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Expose" ac:title="Expose"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expose.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="945c9ebe-05c7-4a1c-b7c9-5fdadbc65f5c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related diagram</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Views and Viewpoints Diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967662 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00181: Extend

- page_id: `243967662`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967662/Extend
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:38.264+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Extend Use Case indicates how and when the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space=''] defined in the extending Use Case can be inserted into the behavior defined in the extended Use Case. The Extend relationship is a relationship for an extending Use Case to the extended Use Case. An Extend relationship can have a condition. If the condition is true, then the Extend [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Action' space=''] is performed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of Extend relationship

The extended Use Case owns one or more specific [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extension Point' space='']. An extension point identifies a point in the behavior of a Use Case where that behavior can be extended.

In the preceding example, the*Penalize for Overdue*Use Case extends the *Register Item Return* Use Case. The *Register Item Return* Use Case owns the *Return Violation* extension point.

You can format the extend symbol properties in the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MT']** dialog.

You can specify Extend properties in the Extend [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']. You can find the description of each property In the same window. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window.

#### TIP: Editing property values

Editing property values

For more information about specifying property values, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MT'].

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']

#### PANEL: Related Resources

Related Resources

Example of the Use Case diagram in*<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\diagrams\use case diagram.mdzip**.*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The Extend relationship is a relationship for an extending Use Case to the extended Use Case. An Extend relationship can have a condition. If the condition is true, then the Extend <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Action" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[action]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is performed. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="550"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-13 at 10.35.34 PM.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">Example of Extend relationship</span></h6><p>The extended Use Case owns one or more specific <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Extension Point" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[extension points]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The <em>Register Item Return</em> Use Case owns the <em>Return Violation</em> extension point.</p><p><br /></p><p>You can format the extend symbol properties in the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog.</p><p>You can specify Extend properties in the Extend <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249005167 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00182: Extended Requirement

- page_id: `249005167`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/249005167/Extended+Requirement
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:34:57.856+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > Requirement-related elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Extended Requirement is a standard Requirement subtype, which adds some properties to a requirement element. These properties, such as source, risk, and verify method are important for the requirement management. Specific projects should add their own properties. All these properties are now available in the standard Requirement Specification window and Requirements Table. If any of these property values is specified, a requirement is automatically converted to ExtendedRequirement.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target">An Extended Requirement is a standard Requirement subtype, which adds some properties to a requirement element. These properties, such as source, risk, and verify method are important for the requirement management. Specific projects should add their own properties. All these properties are now available in the standard Requirement Specification window and Requirements Table. If any of these property values is specified, a requirement is automatically converted to ExtendedRequirement.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967665 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00183: Extension

- page_id: `243967665`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967665/Extension
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:44:14.876+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Extension is used to indicate that the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Properties' space='MT'] of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Metaclass' space=''] are extended through a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space=''], and provides the flexibility to add (and later remove) stereotypes to a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''].

An Extension is a kind of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='']. One end of the Extension is an ordinary Property that ties the Extension to a Class. The other end is an ExtensionEnd tying the Extension to a stereotype that extends the Class.

You can specify an Extension by changing its property values in the Extension[Specification window](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Specification+window). Each property is described in the description area on this window.

You can format the Extension symbol properties in the Symbol Properties dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An Extension is used to indicate that the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Properties" /></ac:link> of a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Metaclass" /></ac:link> are extended through a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Stereotype" /></ac:link>, and provides the flexibility to add (and later remove) stereotypes to a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link>.</p><p>An Extension is a kind of <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Association" /></ac:link>. One end of the Extension is an ordinary Property that ties the Extension to a Class. The other end is an ExtensionEnd tying the Extension to a stereotype that extends the Class.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can specify an Extension by changing its property values in the Extension </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Specification+window">Specification window</a><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. Each property is described in the description area on this window.</span>  </p><p>You can format the Extension symbol properties in the <a>Symbol Properties dialog</a>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968041 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00184: Extension Point

- page_id: `243968041`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243968041/Extension+Point
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:48.841+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Use Case
- labels: ['use-case-extension-points']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

19692200

19692202

19692201

INLINE

An extension point in the Use Case represents the location at which the extension can be inserted. In the Use Case diagram, the extension point is used together with the extend relationship.

To add an extension point to the Use Case

1. Draw an extend relationship from the extending Use Case to the extended Use Case.
2. Subsequent actions depend on whether the extended Use Case has at least one extension point already:
  - If there are no extension points, a dialog appears asking for confirmation to add a new extension point. Click Yes and type the name of the extension point.
  - If there is at least one extension point, the Extension Points dialog opens, displaying the list of extension points of that Use Case. Click Create , type the name of the new extension point, and click Close .

You can also add extension points via the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'] of the Use Case. To do this, click **Extension Point**> [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and then type the name of the new extension point.

19692199

**Related pages**

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='']

In the Use Case diagram, the extension point is used together with the extend relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add an extension point to the Use Case</p><hr /><ol><li>Draw an extend relationship from the extending Use Case to the extended Use Case.</li><li>Subsequent actions depend on whether the extended Use Case has at least one extension point already:<ul><li>If there are no extension points, a dialog appears asking for confirmation to add a new extension point. Click <strong>Yes</strong> and type the name of the extension point.</li><li>If there is at least one extension point, the <strong>Extension Points</strong> dialog opens, displaying the list of extension points of that Use Case. Click <strong>Create</strong>, type the name of the new extension point, and click <strong>Close</strong>.<br /><br /></li></ul></li></ol><p>You can also add extension points via the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> of the Use Case. To do this, click <strong>Extension Point </strong>&gt; <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="11"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_button.png" /></ac:image>, and then type the name of the new extension point. </p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1acb222f-4a34-46f8-95e2-8fecd9f1310d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">19692199</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f0c21b04-53a5-482c-9cb6-957e09ac89e3"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Use Case" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=9919414 space=MED version=8 -->
## PAGE 00185: External

- page_id: `9919414`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/9919414/External
- version_number: 8
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:46:26.803+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > SysML v1 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Visibility representation

299748365

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Visibility representation

299748368

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Visibility representation

299748366

An External block is a block that represents an actor. It facilitates a more detailed modeling of actors like ports or internal structures.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Visibility representation

Visibility representation

In SysML, properties and operations of the Block are public. Visibility representation literals, like +, #, ~, -, are not displayed in the Containment tree or in the element symbol on a diagram.

299748364

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Block Definition Diagram' space='MT']

- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/9919476/External+System

- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > SysML v1 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An External System is a system that interacts with the system under development.

Example

- Monitoring system.
