<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967962 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00189: Final state

- page_id: `243967962`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967962/Final+state
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:46.221+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > State Machine > Pseudo States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The final state symbol (a circle surrounding a smaller solid circle) is used to represent the object destruction. The final state is optional in the diagram because there is a system that runs without interruption after the start of the activities. Also, there can be several final states in the same state diagram, denoting that the life of the object can finish depending on several conditions.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pseudo States' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The final state symbol (a circle surrounding a smaller solid circle) is used to represent the object destruction. The final state is optional in the diagram because there is a system that runs without interruption after the start of the activities. Also, there can be several final states in the same state diagram, denoting that the life of the object can finish depending on several conditions.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36e20852-22dd-420b-8d4b-52b136b22981"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f01b84d9-d9d1-4049-8315-15a6a05d850d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="c2481e8e-3300-47ca-a89e-0b94602a4af1"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Pseudo States" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967666 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00190: Flow Final Node

- page_id: `243967666`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967666/Flow+Final+Node
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:38.455+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Flow Final Node terminates a flow and destroys all tokens that arrive at it. It has no effect on other flows in the activity.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of flow final node element notation

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=9919463 space=MED version=9 -->
## PAGE 00191: Flow Port

- page_id: `9919463`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/9919463/Flow+Port
- version_number: 9
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:46:28.519+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > SysML v1 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

313960321

313960324

313960322

Flow Port is deprecated in accordance with SysML specification and is defined for backward compatibility. The functionality of non-atomic Flow Ports is supported with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space=''] typed by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface Block' space=''] owning [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Property' space='']. Flow Properties are not deprecated. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining interfaces using ports' space='MT']>]]>

A Flow Port is a port that specifies the input and output items that can flow between a Block and its environment. Flow Ports are interactions points through which data, material, or energy “can” enter or leave the owning Block. The specification of what can flow is achieved by typing the Flow Port with a specification of things that flow. This can include typing an atomic Flow Port with a single type (Block, Value Type, or Signal) representing the items that flow in or out, or typing a non-atomic Flow Port with a Flow Specification which lists multiple items that can flow. In general, Flow Ports are intended to be used for asynchronous, broadcast, or send-and-forget interactions. Note that only non-atomic Flow Ports can be conjugated. Once conjugated, all the directions of the typing Flow Specification's items are negated.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

313960320

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Hiding direction arrow on port shape' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='MT']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=9919426 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00193: Flow Specification

- page_id: `9919426`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/9919426/Flow+Specification
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:46:27.810+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > SysML v1 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Flow Specification specifies inputs and outputs as a set of flow properties that list can be displayed in a **flow properties** compartment. A flow specification is used to type Flow Ports, in order to specify items which can flow via the ports.

Flow Specification is deprecated in accordance with SysML specification and is defined for backward compatibility.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related diagrams

Related diagrams

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Block Definition Diagram' space='MT']

## PAGE 00194: Forecast

- page_id: `228985957`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228985957/Forecast
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:30:35.966+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A dependency relationship that specifies a transition from one Asset, Standard, Competence to another future one. It is related to an Actual Enterprise Phase to give it a temporal context.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Dependency

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967970 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00195: Fork and Join

- page_id: `243967970`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967970/Fork+and+Join
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:46.878+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > State Machine > Pseudo States
- labels: ['fork-node', 'join-node']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

727781504

727781506

727781505

The Fork Vertices are used to split an incoming transition into two or more transitions terminating on the orthogonal target vertices (i.e., vertices in different regions of the composite state). The segments going out of a Fork Vertex must not have guards or triggers.

The Join Vertices are used to merge several transitions emanating from the source vertices in different orthogonal regions. The transitions entering a Join Vertex cannot have guards or triggers.

1. A Join Vertex must have at least two incoming Transitions and exactly one outgoing Transition.
2. A Fork Vertex must have at least two outgoing Transitions and exactly one incoming Transition.

727781503

The segments going out of a Fork Vertex must not have guards or triggers.</p><p>The Join Vertices are used to merge several transitions emanating from the source vertices in different orthogonal regions. The transitions entering a Join Vertex cannot have guards or triggers.</p><ol><li>A Join Vertex must have at least two incoming Transitions and exactly one outgoing Transition.</li><li>A Fork Vertex must have at least two outgoing Transitions and exactly one incoming Transition.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4dbdf741-78b3-4c5f-9d81-69a5c98c3fd7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">727781503</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li><li class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Interaction Overview diagram" /></ac:link></li><li class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /></ac:link></li><li class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Protocol State Machine diagram" /></ac:link></li><li class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967855 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00196: Fork and Join Nodes

- page_id: `243967855`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967855/Fork+and+Join+Nodes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:42.804+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Node
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Fork Node splits a flow into multiple concurrent flows, while a Join Node synchronizes them.

1. A Fork Node can have exactly one incoming Activity Edge (or Transition), though it may have multiple outgoing Activity Edges (or Transitions).
2. A Join Node can have exactly one outgoing Activity Edge (or Transition) but may have multiple incoming Activity Edges (or Transitions).

The Fork and Join are represented by the same symbol. Although, you can set the**Use different Fork/Join and Decision/Merge****notations** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='MT'] to draw different notations for the**Fork**and**Join**. This option is set to *true*by default.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example shows the different Fork and Join notations

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A Fork Node splits a flow into multiple concurrent flows, while a Join Node synchronizes them.</p><ol><li>A Fork Node can have exactly one incoming Activity Edge (or Transition), though it may have multiple outgoing Activity Edges (or Transitions).</li><li>A Join Node can have exactly one outgoing Activity Edge (or Transition) but may have multiple incoming Activity Edges (or Transitions).</li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The Fork and Join are represented by the same symbol. Although, you can set the </span><strong>Use different Fork/Join and Decision/Merge</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong> notations</strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[project option]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to draw different notations for the </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>Fork </strong></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">and </span><strong>Join</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. This option is set to <em>true </em>by default.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="fork-join.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example shows the different Fork and Join notations</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967669 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00197: Fragment

- page_id: `243967669`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967669/Fragment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:38.564+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A fragment is an abstract notion of the most general interaction unit. It is a piece of an interaction. Each interaction fragment is conceptually similar to an interaction by itself.

Our modeling tool represents twelve kinds of fragments: Alternatives, Loop, Option, Parallel, Break, Negative, Critical Region, Consider, Ignore, Weak Sequencing, Strict Sequencing, and Assertion.

#### PANEL: Related References

Related References

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating an alternative flow' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Each interaction fragment is conceptually similar to an interaction by itself.</span></p><p>Our modeling tool represents twelve kinds of fragments: Alternatives, Loop, Option, Parallel, Break, Negative, Critical Region, Consider, Ignore, Weak Sequencing, Strict Sequencing, and Assertion. </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="59bc2015-43d9-41f0-a825-33aa65d60798"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related References</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="4fbb2ebd-c062-4358-a8cd-b2c2997293bd" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="335ed78c-5d96-4333-9fc2-5753c5c4e64f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating an alternative flow" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Alternatives]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=9919429 space=MED version=8 -->
## PAGE 00198: Full Port

- page_id: `9919429`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/9919429/Full+Port
- version_number: 8
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:46:28.114+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > SysML v1 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

314864701

314864703

314864702

A Full Port is a port which is considered as a separated element of owning Blocks. It may have internal parts or behaviors that support interactions with owning Blocks.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

314864700

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Hiding direction arrow on port shape' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='MT']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967674 space=MED version=3 -->
## PAGE 00201: Function Behavior

- page_id: `243967674`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967674/Function+Behavior
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:43:48.819+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The function behavior allows modeling external functions that take only inputs and produce outputs. It has no effect on the specified system.

To create a function behavior

1. In the **Model Browser**, right click any root package in your model, in the figure below the root package is *Car*.
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Shortcut menu' space='CDH'], select **Create Element > Function Behavior [IMAGE alt='' src='']**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a package importing a function.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Functions' space='MMA']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967677 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00206: Gate

- page_id: `243967677`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967677/Gate
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:38.818+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The modeling tool allows for displaying the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='']when leaving or entering a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MT'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space=''], or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Combined Fragment' space='']. The Gate is a connection point for representing a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space=''] from the outside to the current [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MT'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space=''], or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Combined Fragment' space=''].

Gates can be used in three cases:

- For passing and returning arguments to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space=''] , which calls some Interaction.
- For displaying “exceptions“ as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space=''] that stops an interaction execution and leaves it.
- For “calling“ sequence blocks represented as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Combined Fragment' space=''] .

Gate has no notation. Gates are created as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space=''] ends when drawing [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space=''] to/from a diagram frame, an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space=''], or a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Combined Fragment' space=''].

To create a formal gate, do one of the following

- Draw a call, send, create, or delete a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space=''] from the diagram frame.
- Draw a reply [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space=''] to the diagram frame.
- Draw a call, send, create, or delete a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space=''] from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Combined Fragment' space=''] (inside [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Combined Fragment' space=''] ).

- You can view the created gates of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space=''] in the Message [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']. In the **Send [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Event' space='']**list box you can see a formal gate. The **Receive**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Event' space='']**Event**lists the actual gate.
- The Gate uses text from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space=''] as an identification name. For example, a message name or a message operation.

To draw create an actual gate of the formal gate, do one of the following

- Draw a call, send, create, or delete a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space=''] to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space=''] , which refers to the diagram with the formal gates. The Select Formal Gate dialog opens.
- Draw a reply [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space=''] from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space=''] , which refers to the diagram with the formal gates. The Select Formal Gate dialog opens.
- Draw a call, send, create, or delete a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space=''] to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Combined Fragment' space=''] (outside [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Combined Fragment' space=''] ). The Select Formal Gate dialog appears.

You can also view the formal and actual gates in the gates Interaction[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'], Interaction Use dialog, Combined Fragment dialog, and the Actual Gates and Formal Gates panes.

To select a formal gate for the actual gate

1. Do one of the following:
  - Draw a message.
  - From the message shortcut menu, choose Select Formal Gate .
2. In the open dialog, select one of the listed formal gates and click OK . An actual gate is created.

The**Select Formal Gates**command is available only if there are formal gates.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT']Activity diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The modeling tool allows for displaying the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[messages ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>when leaving or entering a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[sequence diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interaction Use" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[interaction use]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Combined Fragment" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[combined fragment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The Gate is a connection point for representing a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> from the outside to the current <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[sequence diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interaction Use" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[interaction use]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Combined Fragment" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[combined fragment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>Gates can be used in three cases:</p><ul><li>For passing and returning arguments to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interaction Use" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[InteractionUse]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, which calls some Interaction.</li><li>For displaying “exceptions“ as <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[messages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that stops an interaction execution and leaves it.</li><li>For “calling“ sequence blocks represented as <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Combined Fragment" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CombinedFragments]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul><p>Gate has no notation. Gates are created as <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> ends when drawing <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[messages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to/from a diagram frame, an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interaction Use" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[interaction use]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, or a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Combined Fragment" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[combined fragment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a formal gate, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Draw a call, send, create, or delete a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> from the diagram frame.</li><li>Draw a reply <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to the diagram frame.</li><li>Draw a call, send, create, or delete a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Combined Fragment" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[combined fragment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (inside <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Combined Fragment" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[combined fragment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>).</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b1ee808-c94e-4183-8228-eebc06d67590"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><p>You can view the created gates of <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in the Message <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. In the <strong>Send <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Event" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Event ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>list box you can see a formal gate. The <strong>Receive </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Event" /><ac:link-body><strong>Event</strong> </ac:link-body></ac:link>lists the actual gate.</p></li><li><span>The Gate uses text from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> as an identification name. For example, a message name or a message operation.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To draw create an actual gate of the formal gate, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Draw a call, send, create, or delete a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interaction Use" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[interaction use]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, which refers to the diagram with the formal gates. The <strong>Select Formal Gate</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Draw a reply <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interaction Use" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[interaction use]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, which refers to the diagram with the formal gates. The <strong>Select Formal Gate</strong> dialog opens. </li><li>Draw a call, send, create, or delete a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Message" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Combined Fragment" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[combined fragment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (outside <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Combined Fragment" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[combined fragment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>). The <strong>Select Formal Gate</strong> dialog appears.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e783d43e-08e7-4010-aa09-1222e7aa2a27"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>You can also view the formal and actual gates in the gates Interaction </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link><span>, Interaction Use dialog, Combined Fragment dialog, and the Actual Gates and Formal Gates panes.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To select a formal gate for the actual gate</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Draw a message.</li><li>From the message shortcut menu, choose <strong>Select Formal Gate</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the open dialog, select one of the listed formal gates and click <strong>OK</strong>. An actual gate is created.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d5f9bbd-3931-4ac1-ac23-1b54c618de41"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>The </span><strong>Select Formal Gates</strong><span> command is available only if there are formal gates.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2bcf321e-b978-4308-b7fa-e6212d58c3e1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="4e58255f-45e4-4706-9c1f-f027869c05be"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b2025c5-70c1-47b6-9a1b-4fb242548002"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Class diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Use Case" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /><ac:link-body>Activity diagram<br /></ac:link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967681 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00207: Generalization

- page_id: `243967681`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967681/Generalization
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:38.909+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A generalization is a taxonomic relationship between a more general classifier and a more specific classifier. Each instance of the specific classifier is also an indirect instance of the general classifier. Thus, the specific classifier inherits the features of the more general classifier.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Generalizations can be grouped to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization tree' space='']. 
Generalization may be assigned to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization set' space=''].

You can format the generalization symbol properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MT']**Symbol Properties** dialog.

A generalization can be specified by changing its property values in the generalization [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']. Each property is described in the description area on this window.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Component diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deployment diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Profile diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']

#### INFO: Sample Model

Sample Model

You can see a generalization relationship sample in*<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\case studies\Magic Library.mdzip**.*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Thus, the specific classifier inherits the features of the more general classifier.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="generalization.png" /></ac:image></p><p>Generalizations can be grouped to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Generalization tree" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[generalization tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br />Generalization may be assigned to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Generalization set" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[generalization set]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>You can format the generalization symbol properties in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>A generalization can be specified by changing its property values in the generalization <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967688 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00208: Generalization set

- page_id: `243967688`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967688/Generalization+set
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:42:56.045+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Generalization
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A generalization set is a packageable element whose instances define collections of subsets of generalization relationships. Each generalization set defines a particular set of generalization relationships that describe the way which a general classifier may be divided using specific subtypes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Example of *Movie* and *Audiobook***generalization sets

You can format the generalization symbol properties in the **Symbol Properties** dialog. For more information on how to open the generalization set's Symbol Properties dialog, scroll down to read on .

#### TIP: More Information

More Information

For more information about symbol representation properties, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MT'].

You can specify generalization set properties in the Generalization Set's Specification window. For more information on how to open the Generalization Set's Specification window, scroll down to read on . In the same window, you can find the description of each Generalization Set property in the description area of the Specification Window.

#### TIP: More Information

More Information

For more information about the Specification window usage, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'].

For more information about specifying property values, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MT'].

#### NOTE: Note

Note

For a generalization set, use the name of a selected generalization set (not the relationship path) to open the **Symbol Properties** dialog or Specification window.

#### PANEL: On this page

On this page

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MT']

##### Creating a new generalization set

You can create a generalization set using a generalization Specification window or a command from the generalization [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DH Links panel shortcut menu and Sync Status icons' space='CDH'].

To create a new generalization set using the Specification window

1. Select the generalization and open its Specification window.
2. In the **Generalization Set**property value cell, click the Edit button [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The Select Generalization Set dialog opens. For more information about working with the elements Selection dialog, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='MT'].
3. Select a generalization set owner and click the Create button. If this command is unavailable, turn on the Creation Mode .
4. In the opened list, click Generalization Set . The Generalization Set Specification window opens.
5. Type the generalization set name and set other properties you need. Click Close when you are done.
6. The created generalization set is displayed in the model tree or list and in the Selected elements area of the Select Generalization Set dialog.
7. Select the created generalization set and click **OK**. The generalization set is created and assigned for the current generalization. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Selected Elements

Selected Elements

In the **Generalization Set** property value cell, several generalization sets can be listed, but only the first generalization set name is displayed on the diagram pane near the generalization.

createTo create a new generalization set from the generalization shortcut menu

1. On the diagram pane, select a generalization and open its shortcut menu.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Generalization Set . The Select Generalization Set dialog opens.
3. If it's already there, select the generalization. Otherwise, click the Create Generalization Set button.
4. The Select Owner dialog opens.
5. In the dialog, select an existing or create a new generalization set owner and click OK when you are done.
6. The Generalization Specification window opens. Type the generalization set name and set other properties you need.
7. Click Close when you are done.

#### INFO: Several Generalizations

Several Generalizations

To create a generalization set for several generalizations at a time

1. Select one generalization, then hold down the Shift key and select the other generalizations you desire.
2. While hovering your mouse over one of the generalizations, right click to show the shortcut menu and select Generalization Set.
3. Repeat steps 2-7 from the aforementioned set of instructions.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Assigning a generalization set for a generalization

#### WARNING: Assigning a Generalization Set

Assigning a Generalization Set

Before assigning a generalization set, make sure you have at least one generalization set in your model.

To assign a generalization set for a generalization using the Specification window

1. Select a generalization and open its Specification window.
2. In the Generalization Set property value cell, click the Edit button [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] . The Select Generalization Set dialog opens.
3. Select the desired generalization set and click **OK**. The selected generalization set is displayed in the **Generalization Set** property value cell. For more information about working with the elements Selection dialog, read [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='MT'].
4. Close the Specification window.

To assign a generalization set for a generalization using the shortcut menu

1. On the diagram pane, select a generalization and open its shortcut menu
2. Click **Generalization Set**. In the opened Select Generalization Set dialog, select the generalization set you need. If you do not see your desired Generalized Set then you can from the dialog.

#### TIP: Another Alternative to Assigning Generalization Sets

Another Alternative to Assigning Generalization Sets

Draw or move a generalization line to a generalization set tree or to a generalization that belongs to a generalization set (see the following figure). The newly created generalization is assigned to the same generalization set.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To assign a generalization to a particular generalization set

1. Open the Generalization Set Specification window.
2. In the Generalization property value cell, click the Edit button [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] . The Select Generalization dialog opens.
3. From the list of generalizations, select all you need and click Apply .

You can select generalizations that belong to other sets. Selecting such a generalization removes it from the previous set and adds it to the current one.

##### Opening the Generalization Set Specification window and Symbol Properties dialog

specificationTo open its Specification window

Do one of the following:

- On the diagram pane, double click on the generalization set name revealing the Generalization Set Specification window.
- Invoking the Generalization Set Specification window from the diagram pane shortcut menu
  1. On the diagram pane, right click the generalization set name revealing its shortcut menu.
  2. Select the Specification command opening the Generalization Set Specification window.
- Opening the Generalization Set Specification window from the Generalization Specification window.
  1. Open the Generalization Specification window by double-clicking on the Generalization arrow.
  2. In the Generalization Specification window, select the Generalization Set property cell.
  3. Select the generalization set you desire.
  4. Right-click to open the generalization set shortcut menu. Select Open Specification .
  5. The Generalization Set Specification window opens.

symbolTo open its Symbol Properties dialog

- On the diagram pane, select the generalization set name and from its shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties . The Symbol Properties dialog opens.

Grouping generalizations to generalization set trees

#### NOTE: Make Generalization Set Tree command

Make Generalization Set Tree command

The Make Generalization Set Tree command is available if the general element has at least two generalizations and at least two generalizations has assigned the same generalization set.

To group the generalizations to the generalization set trees

1. Select a general element on the diagram pane and invoke its shortcut menu.
2. Click Refactor > Make Generalization Set Tree .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="SysMLP" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The Select Generalization Set dialog opens.</li><li><p>Select the desired generalization set and click <strong>OK</strong>. The selected generalization set is displayed in the <strong>Generalization Set</strong> property value cell.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe09f902-11fe-473e-ab38-56fd112d0059"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about working with the elements Selection dialog, read <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Selecting elements" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Close the Specification window.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To assign a generalization set for a generalization using the shortcut menu</p><hr /><ol><li>On the diagram pane, select a generalization and open its shortcut menu</li><li><p>Click <strong>Generalization Set</strong>. In the opened Select Generalization Set dialog, select the generalization set you need. If you do not see your desired Generalized Set then you can <ac:link ac:anchor="create"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[can create a new generalization set]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> from the dialog. </p></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="701610da-1a69-4fe1-999e-1d5c432393c5"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Another Alternative to Assigning Generalization Sets</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>Draw or move a generalization line to a generalization set tree or to a generalization that belongs to a generalization set (see the following figure). The newly created generalization is assigned to the same generalization set.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:width="317"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-07-07 at 10.40.14 PM.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To assign a generalization to a particular generalization set</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Generalization Set Specification window.</li><li>In the <strong>Generalization </strong>property value cell, click the Edit button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="SysMLP" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The Select Generalization dialog opens.</li><li>From the list of generalizations, select all you need and click <strong>Apply</strong>.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5d8cabc5-d929-48b7-8cd3-f8511a8b7f87"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can select generalizations that belong to other sets. Selecting such a generalization removes it from the previous set and adds it to the current one.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3>Opening the Generalization Set Specification window and Symbol Properties dialog</h3><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d16676a-5511-4bbd-8a29-c2de0955e417"><ac:parameter ac:name="">specification</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To open its Specification window</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li>On the diagram pane, double click on the generalization set name revealing the Generalization Set Specification window.</li><li>Invoking the Generalization Set Specification window from the diagram pane shortcut menu<ol><li>On the diagram pane, right click the generalization set name revealing its shortcut menu.</li><li>Select the <strong>Specification</strong> command opening the Generalization Set Specification window.</li></ol></li><li>Opening the Generalization Set Specification window from the Generalization Specification window.<ol><li>Open the Generalization Specification window by double-clicking on the Generalization arrow.</li><li>In the Generalization Specification window, select the <strong>Generalization Set</strong> property cell.</li><li>Select the generalization set you desire.</li><li>Right-click to open the generalization set shortcut menu. Select <strong>Open Specification</strong>.</li><li>The Generalization Set Specification window opens.</li></ol></li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);font-size: 16.0px;font-weight: bold;"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);font-size: 16.0px;font-weight: bold;"><br /></span></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fc64bff0-e656-4c39-b0e3-a5ddfb6a5c2b"><ac:parameter ac:name="">symbol</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To open its Symbol Properties dialog</p><hr /><ul><li>On the diagram pane, select the generalization set name and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>. The Symbol Properties dialog opens.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);font-size: 16.0px;font-weight: bold;">Grouping generalizations to generalization set trees</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="29025f5d-d795-4f78-a11c-f2f32351f025"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Make Generalization Set Tree command</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The Make Generalization Set Tree command is available if the general element has at least two generalizations and at least two generalizations has assigned the same generalization set.</span></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span></p><p>To group the generalizations to the generalization set trees</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a general element on the diagram pane and invoke its shortcut menu.</li><li>Click <strong>Refactor</strong> &gt; <strong> Make Generalization Set Tree</strong>. </li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967683 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00209: Generalization tree

- page_id: `243967683`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967683/Generalization+tree
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:38.953+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Generalization
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Usually the general element has more than one specific elements and sometimes specific elements may have their specific elements - the diagram with these generalizations may become messy. To display the generalization relationships and specific elements orderly, the generalization tree can be created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To group generalization paths into a tree, do one of the following

- When drawing generalizations, connect each following generalization to the first one. [ATTACHMENT filename='connect_generalization_to_first.png']
- If all generalizations are drawn separately, do on of the following:
  - Select the generalization and drag it to the other generalization. [ATTACHMENT filename='drag_generalization.png']
  - Select the generalization element and from its shortcut menu select Refactor > Make Sub Tree . [ATTACHMENT filename='make_sub_tree.png']
  - Select all elements you want to add to the tree and, on the diagram toolbar, click the Quick Diagram Layout (Ctrl + Q) button and select one of the tree form layout.

To ungroup a generalization tree, do one of the following

- Right click any relationship in the generalization tree. On the opened shortcut menu, click Ungroup tree .
- Select the end of a concrete generalization relationship and drag it to other element.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization set' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Usually the general element has more than one specific elements and sometimes specific elements may have their specific elements - the diagram with these generalizations may become messy. To display the generalization relationships and specific elements orderly, the generalization tree can be created.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="generalization_tree.png" /></ac:image></p><p> </p><p>To group generalization paths into a tree, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>When drawing generalizations, connect each following generalization to the first one.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="connect_generalization_to_first.png" /></ac:image></li><li>If all generalizations are drawn separately, do on of the following:<br /><ul><li>Select the generalization and drag it to the other generalization.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="drag_generalization.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Select the generalization element and from its shortcut menu select <strong>Refactor</strong> &gt; <strong>Make Sub Tree</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="make_sub_tree.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Select all elements you want to add to the tree and, on the diagram toolbar, click the Quick Diagram Layout (Ctrl + Q) button and select one of the tree form layout.<br /><br /></li></ul></li></ul><p>To ungroup a generalization tree, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Right click any relationship in the generalization tree. On the opened shortcut menu, click <strong>Ungroup tree</strong>.</li><li>Select the end of a concrete generalization relationship and drag it to other element.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="db7e8412-9f3a-4180-b306-a9dae86f712e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Generalization" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Formatting Symbols]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Generalization set" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Generalization Set]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=228985964 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00211: Geo Political Extent Type Kind

- page_id: `228985964`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228985964/Geo+Political+Extent+Type+Kind
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:30:38.084+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Enumeration of the possible kinds of GeoPoliticalExtentType. Its enumeration literals are:

- GeoFeatureType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type of object that encompasses meteorological, geographic, and control features mission significance.
- RegionOfCountryType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type of large, usually continuous segment of a political state, nation, or its territory.
- CountryType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type of political state, nation, or its territory.
- RegionOfWorldType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type of large, usually continuous segment of a surface or space; area.
- FacilityType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type of a real property entity consisting of underlying land and one or more of the following: a building, a structure (including linear structures), a utility system, or pavement.
- SiteType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type of Physical (geographic) location that is or was owned by, leased to, or otherwise possessed. Each site is assigned to a single installation. A site may exist in one of three forms: (1) Land only, where there are no facilities present and where the land consists of either a single land parcel or two or more contiguous land parcels. (2) Facility or facilities only, where the underlying land is neither owned nor controlled by the government. A stand-alone facility can be a site. If a facility is not a stand-alone facility, it must be assigned to a site. (3). Land and all the facilities thereon, where the land consists of either a single land parcel or two or more contiguous land parcels.
- InstallationType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type of base, camp, post, station, yard, center, or other activity, including leased facilities, without regard to the duration of operational control. An installation may include one or more sites.
- OtherType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type not covered by the standard GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKinds.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description </strong></p><p>Enumeration of the possible kinds of GeoPoliticalExtentType. Its enumeration literals are:</p><ul><li>GeoFeatureType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type of object that encompasses meteorological, geographic, and control features mission significance. </li><li>RegionOfCountryType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type of large, usually continuous segment of a political state, nation, or its territory. </li><li>CountryType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type of political state, nation, or its territory. </li><li>RegionOfWorldType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type of large, usually continuous segment of a surface or space; area. </li><li>FacilityType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type of a real property entity consisting of underlying land and one or more of the following: a building, a structure (including linear structures), a utility system, or pavement. </li><li>SiteType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type of Physical (geographic) location that is or was owned by, leased to, or otherwise possessed. Each site is assigned to a single installation. A site may exist in one of three forms: (1) Land only, where there are no facilities present and where the land consists of either a single land parcel or two or more contiguous land parcels. (2) Facility or facilities only, where the underlying land is neither owned nor controlled by the government. A stand-alone facility can be a site. If a facility is not a stand-alone facility, it must be assigned to a site. (3). Land and all the facilities thereon, where the land consists of either a single land parcel or two or more contiguous land parcels. </li><li>InstallationType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type of base, camp, post, station, yard, center, or other activity, including leased facilities, without regard to the duration of operational control. An installation may include one or more sites. </li><li>OtherType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type not covered by the standard GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKinds.</li></ul><p><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p>UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=228985966 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00213: High Level Operational Concept

- page_id: `228985966`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228985966/High+Level+Operational+Concept
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:30:38.614+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Describes the Resources and Locations required to meet an operational scenario from an integrated systems point of view. It is used to communicate overall quantitative and qualitative system characteristics to stakeholders.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Class

It is used to communicate overall quantitative and qualitative system characteristics to stakeholders. </p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UML Class</p><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=228985968 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00215: Implements

- page_id: `228985968`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228985968/Implements
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:30:39.525+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An Abstraction relationship that defines how an element in the upper layer of abstraction is implemented by a semantically equivalent element (i.e. tracing the OperationalActivities to the Functions that implement them) in the lower level of abstraction.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

UML Abstraction

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGBoldBulletKeepNextParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">An Abstraction relationship that defines how an element in the upper layer of abstraction is implemented by a semantically equivalent element (i.e. tracing the OperationalActivities to the Functions that implement them) in the lower level of abstraction. </p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span class="fontstyle0"><strong>Architecture</strong> <strong>Framework</strong></span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong><span class="fontstyle0">Extensions</span></strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span class="fontstyle2">UML Abstraction</span></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967700 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00216: Include

- page_id: `243967700`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967700/Include
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:39.179+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Include relationship is used when there are common parts of the behavior among two or more Use Cases. Each common part is then extracted to a separate Use Case, to be included by all base Use Cases having this part in common. The Include relationship is represented by a base Use Case to an inclusion Use Case.

An Include relationship between Use Cases indicates that an instance of the including Use Case also contains the behavior as specified by the included Use Case.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of Include relationship

In the example above, the*Make Item Reservation*Use Case is a base Use Case. It includes the*Find Item*Use Case. The*Make Item Reservation*Use Case is the including Use Case and the*Find Item*Use Case is the included Use Case.

To use the Include relationship

1. Create two or more Use Cases by dragging and dropping the Use Case element [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-16 at 1.28.57 PM.png'] onto your diagram pane.
2. Click on the Include relationship [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-16 at 1.30.51 PM.png'] and connect it between two Use Cases.
3. The result should look like the aforementioned example.

You can format comment symbol properties in the **Symbol Properties** dialog.

#### TIP: More Information

More Information

For more information about symbol representation properties, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MT'].

You can specify a comment in the Include Specification Window. In the same window, you can find the description of each Include property in the description area of the Specification Window.

#### TIP: More Information

More Information

For more information about the Specification window usage, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'].

For more information about specifying property values, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MT'].

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The Include relationship is used when there are common parts of the behavior among two or more Use Cases. Each common part is then extracted to a separate Use Case, to be included by all base Use Cases having this part in common. The Include relationship is represented by a base Use Case to an inclusion Use Case.</p><p>An Include relationship between Use Cases indicates that an instance of the including Use Case also contains the behavior as specified by the included Use Case.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:width="600"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-16 at 11.35.50 AM.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>Example of Include relationship</h6><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);font-size: 14.0px;">In the example above, the </span><em>Make Item Reservation</em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);font-size: 14.0px;"> Use Case is a base Use Case. It includes the </span><em>Find Item</em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);font-size: 14.0px;"> Use Case. The </span><em>Make Item Reservation</em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);font-size: 14.0px;"> Use Case is the including Use Case and the </span><em>Find Item</em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);font-size: 14.0px;"> Use Case is the included Use Case.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To use the Include relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Create two or more Use Cases by dragging and dropping the Use Case element <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="90"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-16 at 1.28.57 PM.png" /></ac:image>onto your diagram pane.</li><li>Click on the Include relationship <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="80"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-16 at 1.30.51 PM.png" /></ac:image> and connect it between two Use Cases.</li><li>The result should look like the aforementioned example.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>You can format comment symbol properties in the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="19aa51b2-b4f6-4ae5-b87a-0d31db6145b3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">More Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For more information about symbol representation properties, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>You can specify a comment in the Include Specification Window. In the same window, you can find the description of each Include property in the description area of the Specification Window.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="02202a08-01b8-4664-b9d1-3104c6e4ecc5"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">More Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For more information about the Specification window usage, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For more information about specifying property values, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Editing property values" /></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff1aef2b-4429-4784-bb8f-aa49ccc1bf16"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Behavior" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=228985969 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00217: Information

- page_id: `228985969`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228985969/Information
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:30:39.756+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A comment that describes the state of an item of interest in any medium or form -- and is communicated or received.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Comment

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">A comment that describes the state of an item of interest in any medium or form -- and is communicated or received. </p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong><span class="fontstyle0">Architecture Framework</span></strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span> </p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UML Comment</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967704 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00218: Information Flow

- page_id: `243967704`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967704/Information+Flow
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:44:11.346+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Information Flow specifies that one or more information items circulate from its sources to its targets. Information flows require an “information channel” for transmitting information items from the source to the destination.

An information channel is represented in various ways, depending on the nature of its sources and targets. It can be represented by connectors, links, associations, or dependencies. For example, if the source and destination are parts in composite structure diagrams such as a collaboration, the information channel is likely to be represented by a connector between them. Or, if the source and target are objects (which are a kind of Instance Specification), they can be represented by a link that joins the two, and so on. The Information Flow and the information item notation are added. You can draw them using the Information Flows toolbar in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MT'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space='MT'].

##### Setting an Information Flow

Information Flows can be set on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='']in the Class Diagram and on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='']in the Composite Structure Diagrams.

To set an Information Flow

1. Create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space=''] ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='']) relationship between [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''] ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space='']). Make sure the properties for displaying a Conveyed Information on relationships are selected.To display Conveyed Information:Right-click the Association (Connector) and choose the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MT']** command.In the open dialog, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property group toolbar' space='MT'] to **Expert** or **All**.Set the **Show Conveyed Information A** and **Show Conveyed Information B** check boxes to *true*.
2. Specify a Conveyed Information in the following ways: 
- Drag the Class or Information Item on an Association (Connector) or Information Flow and select the Set as Conveyed Information command. 
- Select an Association (Connector) relationship and from the smart manipulator toolbar click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. 
- In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Information (e.g., Class) and drag it onto the Information Flow on the diagram pane.
3. In the **New Conveyed Information** dialog, specify **Conveyed Information**, **Information Flow**, **Direction** and click **OK**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Conveyed informationOne Information Flow may have several Conveyed Information items. If a new Information Flow is set on a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space=''] that is represented in other diagrams, the new Conveyed Information is displayed only on the diagram on which you set it.You can reverse the direction of the [Information Flow](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Information+Flow) on [Connectors](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Connector). [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reversing Information Flow direction' space='MT']>]]> An Information Flow is set.

##### Changing the arrow notation visibility

You can choose whether you want to hide the arrow notation on the realizing elements. The **Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information Is Missing** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='MT'] controls the arrow visibility.

To change the arrow notation visibility on realizing element when the Conveyed Information is hidden or unspecified

1. From the modeling tool main menu, choose Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, go to General > Diagrams .
3. In the Diagrams property group, find the Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information Is Missing option and set it to true.
4. Click OK when you are done.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reversing Information Flow direction' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Item' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967708 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00219: Information Item

- page_id: `243967708`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967708/Information+Item
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:39.362+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Quick search

633879781

INLINE

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Quick search

633879783

INLINE

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Quick search

633879782

INLINE

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Flow' space=''] package provides mechanisms for specifying the exchange of information between entities in a system at a high level of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Navigating between different levels of abstraction' space='MT']. Information Flows describe the circulation of information in a system in a general manner. They do not specify the nature of the information or the mechanisms by which this information is conveyed (message passing, signal, common data store, parameter of operation, etc.). Also, they do not specify sequences or any control conditions. It is intended that representation and realization [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Link' space=''] will be able to specify which model element implements the specified information flow, and how the information will be conveyed while modeling in detail.

An Information Item is an abstraction of all information that can be exchanged between objects. It is a kind of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying internal structure on structured classifiers' space='MT'] intended to represent information in a very abstract way, one which cannot be instantiated.

In a classifier, the Information Item can be represented as a name inside a rectangle. The black triangle icon at the top of the rectangle indicates that it is an Information Item.

Information Items, or any conveyed classifiers, can be displayed on any relationships.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of an Information Item named Wage

One purpose of Information Items is to enable you to define preliminary models before making detailed modeling decisions on types or structures. Another purpose of Information Items and Information Flows is to abstract complex models by using a less specific more general representation of the information exchanged between entities of a system.

To create an Information Item

1. In a model or diagram, select a relationship or create a new one.
2. Set a relationship as an Information Flow, using one of the following methods:
  - Click a relationship. The smart manipulator opens. Select the New Conveyed Information command. [ATTACHMENT filename='smart_manipulator_new_conveyed_information.png']
  - Open the relationship [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'] . Select the Conveyed Information property group and click the Add button.
3. The New Conveyed Information dialog opens. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='open_dialog_button.png'] next to the Conveyed Information text box.
4. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='MT'] dialog opens. Click the Creation Mode button to enable the creation of new elements.
5. Select the package where you want to create a new element.
6. Click the **Create** button and select **Information Item**element in the list. Quick searchStart typing the element name to find the element quicker.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. The Specification window of the newly created element opens. Type the element name and specify other properties according to your needs.
8. After you finish, close all the dialogs one by one. The Information Item element is created.

633879780

INLINE

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Flow' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
