Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="open_dialog_button.png" /></ac:image> next to the <strong>Conveyed Information </strong>text box.</li><li>The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Selecting elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Select Conveyed Information]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>dialog opens. Click the Creation Mode button to enable the creation of new elements.</li><li>Select the package where you want to create a new element.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <strong>Create</strong> button and select <strong>Information Item </strong>element in the list.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e069926-c278-4dfe-b6b2-fa4ee690c03c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Quick search</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Start typing the element name to find the element quicker.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_information_item.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>The Specification window of the newly created element opens. Type the element name and specify other properties according to your needs.</li><li>After you finish, close all the dialogs one by one. The Information Item element is created.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b2755d1d-1e03-44e1-8455-dd8af9b1c845"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">633879780</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Information Flow" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

## PAGE 00220: Information Model

- page_id: `228985970`
- space_key: `MED`

- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:30:39.959+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A structural specification of information types, showing relationships between them. The type of information captured in the Information Model is described using the enumeration Information Model Kind (Conceptual,Logical and Physical).

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

Package

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>A structural specification of information types, showing relationships between them. The type of information captured in the Information Model is described using the enumeration Information Model Kind (Conceptual,Logical and Physical).</p><p><span class="fontstyle0"><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p><strong>Extension</strong></p><p>Package</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=228985971 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00221: Information Model Kind

- page_id: `228985971`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228985971/Information+Model+Kind
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:30:40.223+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Information Model Kind is an enumeration of the possible kinds of Data Model. Its enumeration literals are:

- Conceptual - Indicates that the Data Model associated with the Data Model Kind is a conceptual Data Model that defines the required high-level data concepts and their relationships.
- Logical - Indicates that the Data Model associated with the Data Model Kind is a logical data model that allows analysis of an architecture’s data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation specific or product specific issues. It details the conceptual data model.
- Physical - Indicates that the Data Model associated with the Data Model Kind is a physical data model that is an implementable specification of a data structure. A physical data model realizes a logical data model, taking into account implementation restrictions and performance issues while still enforcing the constraints, relationships and typing of the logical data model.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

Enumeration

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">The Information Model Kind is an enumeration of the possible kinds of Data Model. Its enumeration literals are:</p><ul><li>Conceptual - Indicates that the Data Model associated with the Data Model Kind is a conceptual Data Model that defines the required high-level data concepts and their relationships.</li><li>Logical - Indicates that the Data Model associated with the Data Model Kind is a logical data model that allows analysis of an architecture’s data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation specific or product specific issues. It details the conceptual data model.</li><li>Physical - Indicates that the Data Model associated with the Data Model Kind is a physical data model that is an implementable specification of a data structure. A physical data model realizes a logical data model, taking into account implementation restrictions and performance issues while still enforcing the constraints, relationships and typing of the logical data model.</li></ul><p><span class="fontstyle0"><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p><strong><span class="fontstyle2">Extension</span></strong></p><p>Enumeration</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967713 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00222: Initial Node

- page_id: `243967713`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967713/Initial+Node
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:44:14.730+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Initial node is a control node where flow starts when the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity' space=''] is invoked. An Activity may have more than one Initial node.

You can specify an Initial node by changing its property values in the Initial node[Specification window](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Specification+window). Each property is described in the description area on this window.

You can format Initial Node symbol properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MT'].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An Initial node is a control node where flow starts when the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Activity" /></ac:link> is invoked. An Activity may have more than one Initial node.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can specify an Initial node by changing its property values in the Initial node </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Specification+window">Specification window</a><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. Each property is described in the description area on this window.</span></p><p>You can format Initial Node symbol properties in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /></ac:link>.    </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="initial_node.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967961 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00223: Initial state

- page_id: `243967961`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967961/Initial+state
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:46.152+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > State Machine > Pseudo States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Every object belongs to a particular state as soon as it is created. So, it is useful to explicitly show that particular state. A solid filled circle represents the initial state of an object. There can only be one initial state for an object. The initial state denotes the starting place for a transition, the target of which is a composite state.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pseudo States' space='']

So, it is useful to explicitly show that particular state. A solid filled circle represents the initial state of an object. There can only be one initial state for an object. The initial state denotes the starting place for a transition, the target of which is a composite state.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36e20852-22dd-420b-8d4b-52b136b22981"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f01b84d9-d9d1-4049-8315-15a6a05d850d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="a91c5c61-63c8-4aaa-bfa5-b56b64a56477"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Pseudo States" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967715 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00224: Instance Specification

- page_id: `243967715`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967715/Instance+Specification
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-09T09:56:16.717+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: ['slot', 'slot-specification']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An instance specification specifies the existence of an entity in a modeled system and completely or partiallydescribes the entity.

The description may include:

- The classification of an entity by one or more classifiers of which the entity is an instance. If the only classifier specified is abstract, then the instance specification only partially describes the entity.
- A kind of instance based on its classifier or classifiers. For example, an instance specification whose classifier is a class describes an object of that class, while an instance specification whose classifier is an association describes a link of that association.
- A specification of structural features values of the entity. Not all structural features of all classifiers of the instance specification need to be represented by slots, in which case the instance specification is a partial description.
- A specification of how to compute, derive, or construct the instance (optional).

Modeling tool allows you to create the instances of classifiers such as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enumeration' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actor' space=''],[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Node' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Component' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Manifestations' space=''].

In the Component (or Deployment) diagram Node Instance, Component Instance, Artifact Instance elements are the same Instance Specification elements with an assigned component, node or artifact.

The instances are shown using a rectangle by underlining the name string of an instance element. The instance of an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actor' space='']is shown as an actor “stick man” figure with the actor’s name below the symbol.

You can specify instance properties in the instance [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']. In the same window, you can find a description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window. If you need to handle a huge amount of instance specifications, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance table' space='MT'].

##### Assigning a classifier to an Instance Specification

To assign a classifier to an instance, do one of the following

- On the diagram, click an instance shape, and then on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. In the open list, select the classifier.
- On the diagram, click an instance shape, and then press **Ctrl+T**. In the open list, select the classifier.
- On the diagram, click an instance shape, then click the name area. Type the “:” and then type the classifier name. Press **Enter**or click anywhere on the diagram. The classifier is created and assigned. Type the “:” colon, then press Ctrl+Space or Ctrl+Backspace and then in the open list, select the classifier.
- In the Containment tree, select a classifier and drag it to the instance shape.

Use the Node Instance button to create the Instance Specification withassigned Node and the Node Instance will have a Node shape.

##### Assigning Instance Specification as Default Value

To assign Instance Specification as Default Value

- Drag Instance Specification element on Classifier property on a diagram.

Drag-and-drop feature is available only if Instance Specification classifiers are compatible with the Property type and ifProperty is editable.

Setting the initial value to an attribute of the assigned classifier

To set the initial value to an attribute of the assigned classifier

1. Open the Instance Specification window.
2. In the **Slots**property group, select a property and c lick the **Create Value**button.
3. Type the needed value in the field on the right.

##### Showing slots of the assigned classifier

To show slots of the assigned classifier

1. Select an instance shape and click the Compartments button [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
2. In the menu, select **Slots**.

By default slots of the classifier are suppressed.

##### Displaying slot type on the Instance Specification symbol

To display slot type on the instance symbol

1. Open instance **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MT']** dialog.
2. Set the **Show Slot Type**property to one of the following:

- None
- Name
- Qualified name

##### Displaying specification value on the Instance Specification symbol

To display specification value on the Instance Specification symbol

1. Open instance [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MT'] dialog.
2. Select the **Show Specification Value** check box.

##### Saving Instance values to the default property values of the Class (Block)

When you have a Class (Block) and an Instance of that Class (Block)which contains some particular values, you can save those values as default to the Values property of the Class (Block).

To save the Instance values as default

1. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select an Instance.
  - In the Containment tree, select an Instance.
  - In the Instance table, select an Instance.
2. Right-click an Instance and from the shortcut menu, select Tools > Save to Default Value(s) .
3. In the open dialog, select the needed options:
  - **Save to composite parts recursively**.
  - **Redefine inherited properties**.
  - Save derived property values . [ATTACHMENT filename='save_to_default_values.png']
4. Click OK .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of Instance property values saved as default in the Block value properties. The "Save to composite parts recursively" option was selected.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

table, select an Instance.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="e43a3dbe-ffef-4b3d-b199-534f75d4bfdc">Right-click an Instance and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt;<strong> Save to Default Value(s)</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="42e10ef8-68fa-4f8a-959b-f2130702c1c5">In the open dialog, select the needed options:<ul><li><p><strong>Save to composite parts recursively</strong>.</p></li><li><p><strong>Redefine inherited properties</strong>.</p></li><li><strong>Save derived property values</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_to_default_values.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="029d469c-7f4a-4cb9-aa1b-52a1be8dc5be">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_to_default_values_example.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example of Instance property values saved as default in the Block value properties. The &quot;Save to composite parts recursively&quot; option was selected.</h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="33f657a7-af9e-4d23-885a-ee82969a89ae"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="2273f912-04d4-4179-9813-ccded34f038e"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="36adf7e3-4abf-4bfa-b40f-90bc65080a1a"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Use Case" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="d3ec2144-5a48-4ead-9db4-e73cef952a3b"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="79d346ec-e902-464a-9d29-0d3e5250fbbe"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="9dbb69ac-9f36-44df-8a35-f3b757562fa7"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="9af5e729-62fa-4e89-8c33-0cd04c209083"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Class diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967723 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00225: Interaction

- page_id: `243967723`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967723/Interaction
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:39.800+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Interaction is a unit of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space=''] focusing on the observable exchange of information between connectable elements, a specialization of Interaction Fragment and of Behavior.

An Interaction originates in a modeling tool while creatingCommunicationandSequencediagrams. When you double click on an Interaction, a diagram is opened. 
In theelementdialog box, you can modify the current element and add various specifications to it. For more information about element dialog boxes and panes of the element dialog boxes,click here. 
 
To open the**Interaction**dialog box, choose the**Specification**command from the Interaction shortcut menu. 
 
In the**Interaction**dialog box, the**Messages**pane lists Messages contained in this Interaction.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An Interaction is a unit of <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Behavior" /></ac:link> focusing on the observable exchange of information between connectable elements, a specialization of Interaction Fragment and of Behavior.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">An Interaction originates in a modeling tool while creating </span><a>Communication</a><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> and </span><a>Sequence</a><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> diagrams. When you double click on an Interaction, a diagram is opened. </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">In the </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">element</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> dialog box, you can modify the current element and add various specifications to it. For more information about element dialog boxes and panes of the element dialog boxes, </span><a>click here</a><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">. </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">  </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To open the </span><span class="hcp1" style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Interaction</strong></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> dialog box, choose the </span><strong>Specification</strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> command from the Interaction shortcut menu.   </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">  </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">In the </span><span class="hcp1" style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Interaction</strong></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> dialog box, the </span><span class="hcp1" style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Messages</strong></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> pane lists Messages contained in this Interaction.</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967724 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00226: Interaction operand

- page_id: `243967724`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967724/Interaction+operand
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:39.870+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Interaction
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Interaction operand represents an Interaction with an optional guard expression. The Interaction operand is executed only if the guard expression tests true at runtime. The object that starts the interaction requires all the information necessary to make the decision about whether to proceed. The absence of any guard condition means that the interaction is always executed. 
In the Interaction operand [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'], you can modify the current element and add various specifications to it.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">An Interaction operand represents an Interaction with an optional guard expression. The Interaction operand is executed only if the guard expression tests true at runtime. The object that starts the interaction requires all the information necessary to make the decision about whether to proceed. The absence of any guard condition means that the interaction is always executed. </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">In the <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Interaction operand <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></span></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">, you can modify the current element and add various specifications to it. </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">  </span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967725 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00227: Interaction Use

- page_id: `243967725`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967725/Interaction+Use
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:39.941+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Interaction
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Interactions are units of behavior of an enclosing Classifier. They focus on the passing of information with Messages between the Connectable Elements of the Classifier.

A reference to the interaction can be created.

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MT'] in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']. In the same window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']Specification window.

To add a reference to an interaction use, do one of the following

- Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'] of the interaction use and click the cell of the **Refers To**property value. Then click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and in the open dialog, select the element. Click **OK**when you are done.
- On the diagram, right-click the interaction use shape, and then point to **Refers To**. Then select an element you wish to refere to.
- On the diagram, click an interaction use shape, and then press **Ctrl+T**. Then select an element you wish to refere to.
- On the diagram, click an interaction use shape, and then on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. Then select an element you wish to refere to.
- In the Containment tree, select an element and drag it to the interaction use shape.

To add an actual [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Gate' space='']

1. Add a reference to the diagram, from which frame the formal message is created.
2. Draw an actual message with the selected formal [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Gate' space=''] to the current interaction use.

The rake icon[IMAGE alt='' src='']is displayed on the shape if the interaction use refers to another sequence diagram. The rake icon is not shown by default therefore you need to set the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying rake icon' space='MT']property value to true in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MT']dialog.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967728 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00228: Interface

- page_id: `243967728`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967728/Interface
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:40.051+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An interface is a specifier for the externally-visible operations of a class, component, or other classifiers (including subsystems) without a specification of the internal structure. Each interface often specifies only a limited part of the behavior of an actual class.

The set of interfaces realized by a classifier is its provided interfaces, which represent the obligations that instances of that classifier have to their clients. They describe the services that the instances of that classifier offer to their clients.

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MT'] in the interface [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']. In the same window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'].

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT']Activity diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Each interface often specifies only a limited part of the behavior of an actual class.</p><p>The set of interfaces realized by a classifier is its provided interfaces, which represent the obligations that instances of that classifier have to their clients. They describe the services that the instances of that classifier offer to their clients.</p><p>You can <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Editing property values" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specify interface properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in the interface <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d32cf623-2920-40bb-a45b-fe7b1779bc81"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="9b681f01-b5a5-4167-86c3-d99aa4adbde2"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b2025c5-70c1-47b6-9a1b-4fb242548002"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Class diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Use Case" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /><ac:link-body>Activity diagram<br /></ac:link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=9919435 space=MED version=9 -->
## PAGE 00229: Interface Block

- page_id: `9919435`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/9919435/Interface+Block
- version_number: 9
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:46:28.279+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > SysML v1 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Visibility representation

298771506

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Visibility representation

298771508

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Visibility representation

298771507

An Interface block is a special kind of block for typing proxy ports. It has no behaviors or internal parts. Normally, it contains a set of flow properties that can be shown in the **flow properties** compartment.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Visibility representation

Visibility representation

In SysML, properties and operations of the Block are public. Visibility representation literals, like +, #, ~, -, are not displayed in the Containment tree or in the element symbol on a diagram.

298771505

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Block Definition Diagram' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e8225d9-89e6-41c1-b680-3e0cd1ea48bb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">298771506</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="97159df6-ef09-4ed8-adc1-a51bdb26453b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">298771508</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b64dd990-7c6b-4068-9059-40b8f789d6cd"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">298771507</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>An Interface block is a special kind of block for typing proxy ports. It has no behaviors or internal parts. Normally, it contains a set of flow properties that can be shown in the <strong>flow properties</strong> compartment.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Interface Block" ac:alt="Interface Block"><ri:attachment ri:filename="interface_block.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="45e244e1-7a14-47d4-88f2-ec499c585171"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Visibility representation</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In SysML, properties and operations of the Block are public. Visibility representation literals, like +, #, ~, -, are not displayed in the Containment tree or in the element symbol on a diagram.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c566e6fd-9fc3-431a-9799-9414d24dd150"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">298771505</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="SysML Block Definition Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Block Definition Diagram ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967737 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00230: Interface realization

- page_id: `243967737`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967737/Interface+realization
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:43:23.667+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Interface realization is a specialized [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realization' space=''] relationship between a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Classifier Behavior property' space='MMA'] and an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface' space=''], signifying that the realizing classifier conforms to the contract specified by the Interface.

A classifier that implements an interface specifies instances conforming to the interface and its ancestors. A classifier may implement a number of interfaces.

In the Interface realization [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'], you can modify the current element and add various specifications to it. 
 
To open theInterfaceRealizationdialog box, choose theSpecificationcommand from the Realization shortcut menu. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the Interface Realization [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'], the Implementing Classifier property references the classifier that realizes the Interface to which it points.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An Interface realization is a specialized <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realization" /></ac:link> relationship between a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MMA" ri:content-title="Classifier Behavior property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Classifier Behavior]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interface" /></ac:link>, signifying that the realizing classifier conforms to the contract specified by the Interface.</p><p>A classifier that implements an interface specifies instances conforming to the interface and its ancestors. A classifier may implement a number of interfaces.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> In the Interface realization <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">, you can modify the current element and add various specifications to it. </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">  </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To open the </span><span class="hcp1" style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Interface</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><span class="hcp1" style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Realization</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> dialog box, choose the </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Specification</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> command from the Realization shortcut menu. </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Interface_realization.png" /></ac:image> </span></p><p>In the <span class="hcp1">Interface</span> <span class="hcp1">Realization</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, the <span class="hcp1">Implementing</span> <span class="hcp1">Classifier</span> property references the classifier that realizes the Interface to which it points.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249005169 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00231: Interface Requirement

- page_id: `249005169`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/249005169/Interface+Requirement
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:35:47.209+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > Requirement-related elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Interface Requirement is a requirement that specifies the ports for connecting systems and parts of a system. Optionally, it may include the items that flow across the connector and/or the Interface constraints.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target">An Interface Requirement is a requirement that specifies the ports for connecting systems and parts of a system. Optionally, it may include the items that flow across the connector and/or the Interface constraints.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967739 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00232: Internal Transition

- page_id: `243967739`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967739/Internal+Transition
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:42:25.494+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In all other cases, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Action' space=''] label identifies the event that triggered the corresponding Action expression. These events are called Internal Transitions and are semantically equivalent to self transitions except that the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space=''] is neither exited nor re-entered. This means that the corresponding exit and entry actions are not performed.

#### TIP: Fork And Join

Fork And Join

For more information about defining transitions, read [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Fork and Join' space=''].

Specify the Internal Transition in the Transition's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'].

#### TIP: Fork And Join

Fork And Join

For a more detailed description of this dialog, read [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space=''].

To create and specify an Internal Transition

In your project, if you have a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MT'] created, do the following:

1. Double-click the State or select Specification from the State's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Shortcut menu' space='CDH'] . The State's Specification Window opens.
2. Click the Internal Transitions group.
3. Click the Create button. The Transition's Specification window opens.
4. Specify an Internal Transition .
5. Click Close.

To remove an Internal Transition

1. Double-click the State or select Specification from the State's shortcut menu. The State's Specification Window opens.
2. Click the Internal Transitions group.
3. The Transition's Specification window opens.
4. Click Delete . Then, Click Close .

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Action' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Event' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Fork and Join' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

These events are called Internal Transitions and are semantically equivalent to self transitions except that the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="State" /></ac:link> is neither exited nor re-entered. This means that the corresponding exit and entry actions are not performed. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9018626-7053-43bd-ae53-c4a4ece9de77"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Fork And Join</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about defining transitions, read <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Fork and Join" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the Internal Transition in the Transition's <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b2ddc42b-1ffe-4fd2-abaf-50081e196d4d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Fork And Join</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>For a more detailed description of this dialog, read <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Transition" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To create and specify an Internal Transition</p><hr /><p>In your project, if you have a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /></ac:link> created, do the following:</p><ol><li>Double-click the State or select Specification from the State's <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CDH" ri:content-title="Shortcut menu" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[shortcut menu]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Then, Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff1aef2b-4429-4784-bb8f-aa49ccc1bf16"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Action" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Event" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Fork and Join" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Transition" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967740 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00233: Interruptible activity region

- page_id: `243967740`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967740/Interruptible+activity+region
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:40.525+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Interruptable region contains activity nodes. When a token leaves an interruptable region via edges designated by the region as interrupting edges, all tokens and behaviours in the region are terminated.

Specify the Interruptible Activity Region in the Interruptible Activity Region [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'].

To make an edge as interrupting, simply select an edge and click the "lightning" button, as shown in the image bellow.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Mark edge as interrupting

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An Interruptable region contains activity nodes. When a token leaves an interruptable region via edges designated by the region as interrupting edges, all tokens and behaviours in the region are terminated.</p><p>Specify the Interruptible Activity Region in the Interruptible Activity Region <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</p><p>To make an edge as interrupting, simply select an edge and click the &quot;lightning&quot; button, as shown in the image bellow.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="make_interrupting.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>Mark edge as interrupting</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=9919478 space=MED version=3 -->
## PAGE 00234: Invocation on Nested Port Action

- page_id: `9919478`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/9919478/Invocation+on+Nested+Port+Action
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:46:25.820+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > SysML v1 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Invocation on Nested Port Action is an invocation action that applies the «InvocationOnNestedPortAction» stereotype which extends the UML’s on Port property to support nested ports.

- You must specify both On Port and On Nested Port properties for the Invocation On Nested Port action to be completed.
- The On Nested Port contains the nested structure to the final port.
- The final port from which signal is sent is considered as the On Port.

#### PANEL: Related diagrams

Related diagrams

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Activity Diagram' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967968 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00237: Junction

- page_id: `243967968`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967968/Junction
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:46.710+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > State Machine > Pseudo States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The junction vertices are semantic-free vertices that are used to chain multiple transitions together. They are used to construct the compound transition paths between states. For example, a junction can be used to combine multiple incoming transitions into a single outgoing transition representing a shared transition path (this is known as merge). Conversely, it can be used to split an incoming transition into multiple outgoing transition segments with different guard conditions.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pseudo States' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The junction vertices are semantic-free vertices that are used to chain multiple transitions together. They are used to construct the compound transition paths between states. For example, a junction can be used to combine multiple incoming transitions into a single outgoing transition representing a shared transition path (this is known as merge). Conversely, it can be used to split an incoming transition into multiple outgoing transition segments with different guard conditions.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36e20852-22dd-420b-8d4b-52b136b22981"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f01b84d9-d9d1-4049-8315-15a6a05d850d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="7e19d119-2f65-4f2a-b944-60c34f7c4ba8"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Pseudo States" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967742 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00239: Lifeline

- page_id: `243967742`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967742/Lifeline
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:40.596+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

A lifeline represents an individual participant in the Interaction. The lifeline represents only one interacting entity, symbolized by a rectangle.

The following steps show you how to work with a lifeline:

##### Assigning a type to a lifeline

To assign a type (classifier) to a lifeline, do one of the following:

- In the lifeline Specification window, in the Type property value, click the ... button, and in the open dialog, select the type. When you are finished, click OK .
- On the diagram, from the lifeline shortcut menu, select Type . In the open list, select the type.
- On the diagram, click a lifeline, and then, on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='type_command.png'] . In the open list, select the type.
- On the diagram, click a lifeline, and then press Ctrl+T. In the open list, select the type.
- On the diagram, select a lifeline, and in the name area, type the “:” and the name of the type. Press Enter or click anywhere on the diagram. The type is created and assigned. Type the “:”, press Ctrl+Space, or Ctrl+Backspace. In the open list, select the type.
- In the Containment tree, select an element and drag it to the lifeline shape.

##### Showing/hiding a base classifier

To hide/show a base classifier

- On the diagram, right-click the lifeline. From the shortcut menu, select the Show Classifier check box.

##### Displaying lifelines

You can display lifelines on the Sequence or Communication diagram in the following ways:

- By manually dragging lifeline elements from the Containment tree.
- By automatically using the Display Lifelines dialog.

There are two situations in which you can use the **Display Lifelines** dialog to automatically display lifelines:

- .
- .

newTo display lifelines when creating a new diagram

1. When creating a new Sequence or Communication diagram for an existing classifier, the Display Lifelines dialog opens automatically. [ATTACHMENT filename='Display Lifelines.png']
2. In the opened Display Lifelines dialog, select the lifelines you want to display, and click OK . Selected lifelines are displayed on the diagram pane.

modifyTo display lifelines in a preexisting diagram

1. Select the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MT'] .
2. Open the Display Lifelines dialog by performing one of the following steps:
  - From the diagram shortcut menu, select Display > Display Lifelines . [ATTACHMENT filename='Shortcut menu_ Display Lifelines..png']
  - On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MT'] , click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select Display Lifelines .
3. In the dialog, select the lifelines you want to display, and click OK . Selected lifelines are displayed on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='displayed_lifelines_in_sequence_diagram.png']

##### Deeply nested parts

The “dot notation” is available for the lifelines. Now you can send messages directly to the deeply nested parts.

To display a deeply nested part

1. Create a Sequence diagram. The **Display Lifelines** dialog opens. You can open the **Display Lifelines** dialog in the existing diagram. On the diagram pane, click the right mouse button and from the shortcut menu, select **Related Elements** > **Display Lifelines**.
2. Select a nested part you want to display. Make sure that the nesting part is not selected, otherwise it will be displayed instead of the nested part.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Activation bars on lifelines

By default, when there is a message sent between lifelines in a sequence diagram, the lifelines are displayed with activation bars on them. However, you can hide the activation bars from the diagram. 
 
To hide activation bars from lifelines in a sequence diagram

1. Right-click on the diagram pane. From the shortcut menu, select Diagram properties .
2. The Diagram properties of Sequence Diagram dialog opens. Deselect Show Activations check box, setting the value from true to false . [ATTACHMENT filename='Show Activations_Dialog.png']

**Sample model**

The models used in the figures of this page is the **Communication diagram** and **Sequence diagram** sample models.

To open these samples

- Find in modeling tool: - <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\diagrams\communication diagram.mdzip. 
 - <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\diagrams\sequence diagram.mdzip.

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Communication diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c22fb696-0788-4a58-bb9c-312054fcbaa8"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A lifeline represents an individual participant in the Interaction. The lifeline represents only one interacting entity, symbolized by a rectangle.</p><p>The following steps show you how to work with a lifeline:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="118a1089-8d61-4662-83ea-0d78de432f91" /></p><h3>Assigning a type to a lifeline</h3><p>To assign a type (classifier) to a lifeline, do one of the following:</p><hr /><ul><li>In the lifeline Specification window, in the Type property value, click the <strong>...</strong> button, and in the open dialog, select the type. When you are finished, click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>On the diagram, from the lifeline shortcut menu, select <strong>Type</strong>. In the open list, select the type.</li><li>On the diagram, click a lifeline, and then, on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="11"><ri:attachment ri:filename="type_command.png" /></ac:image>. In the open list, select the type.</li><li>On the diagram, click a lifeline, and then press Ctrl+T. In the open list, select the type.</li><li><p>On the diagram, select a lifeline, and in the name area, type the “:” and the name of the type. Press Enter or click anywhere on the diagram. The type is created and assigned.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="34edd14c-0b71-4460-8e58-42927479b8a4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Type the “:”, press Ctrl+Space, or Ctrl+Backspace. In the open list, select the type.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>In the Containment tree, select an element and drag it to the lifeline shape.</li></ul><h3>Showing/hiding a base classifier</h3><p>To hide/show a base classifier</p><hr /><ul><li>On the diagram, right-click the lifeline. From the shortcut menu, select the <strong>Show Classifier</strong> check box.</li></ul><h3>Displaying lifelines</h3><p>You can display lifelines on the Sequence or Communication diagram in the following ways:</p><ul><li>By manually dragging lifeline elements from the Containment tree.</li><li>By automatically using the <strong>Display Lifelines</strong> dialog.</li></ul><p>There are two situations in which you can use the <strong>Display Lifelines</strong> dialog to automatically display lifelines:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="new"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[When creating a new diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="modify"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[When modifying a pre-existing diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c00bf0e4-ff7a-4b1c-92a9-7022c13b3eea"><ac:parameter ac:name="">new</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To display lifelines when creating a new diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>When creating a new Sequence or Communication diagram for an existing classifier, the <strong>Display Lifelines</strong> dialog opens automatically.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display Lifelines.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>In the opened<strong> Display Lifelines</strong> dialog, select the lifelines you want to display, and click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />Selected lifelines are displayed on the diagram pane.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71fb5382-ed81-481f-96fe-6ee566af98a7"><ac:parameter ac:name="">modify</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To display lifelines in a preexisting diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Open the <strong>Display Lifelines</strong> dialog by performing one of the following steps:<br /><ul><li>From the diagram shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Lifelines</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Shortcut menu_ Display Lifelines..png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>On the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram toolbar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"> <ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image> </span> and select <strong>Display Lifelines</strong>.<br /><br /></li></ul></li><li>In the dialog, select the lifelines you want to display, and click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />Selected lifelines are displayed on the diagram pane.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="Displayed lifelines on the Order Ticket Sequence diagram pane." ac:alt="Displayed lifelines on the Order Ticket Sequence diagram pane."><ri:attachment ri:filename="displayed_lifelines_in_sequence_diagram.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><h3>Deeply nested parts</h3><p>The “dot notation” is available for the lifelines. Now you can send messages directly to the deeply nested parts.</p><p>To display a deeply nested part</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Create a Sequence diagram. The <strong>Display Lifelines</strong> dialog opens.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="98fd5ffa-bb9d-4254-92e5-33de9319cf50"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can open the <strong>Display Lifelines</strong> dialog in the existing diagram. On the diagram pane, click the right mouse button and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Related Elements</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Lifelines</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Select a nested part you want to display.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="97000b27-6df9-4af6-a592-d6842556549d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Make sure that the nesting part is not selected, otherwise it will be displayed instead of the nested part.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Dot Notation.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Activation bars on lifelines</h3><p>By default, when there is a message sent between lifelines in a sequence diagram, the lifelines are displayed with activation bars on them. However, you can hide the activation bars from the diagram.<br /><br />To hide activation bars from lifelines in a sequence diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click on the diagram pane. From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Diagram properties</strong>.</li><li>The <strong>Diagram properties of Sequence Diagram</strong> dialog opens. Deselect <strong>Show Activations</strong> check box, setting the value from <strong>true</strong> to <strong>false</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Show Activations_Dialog.png" /></ac:image></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Sample model</strong></p><p>The models used in the figures of this page is the <strong>Communication diagram</strong> and <strong>Sequence diagram</strong> sample models.</p><p>To open these samples</p><hr /><ul><li>Find in modeling tool:<br />   - <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\diagrams\communication diagram.mdzip.<br />   - &lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\diagrams\sequence diagram.mdzip.</em></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell> <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Related pages</strong><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Communication diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Creating diagrams" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967754 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00240: Link

- page_id: `243967754`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967754/Link
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:42:26.505+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An instance of an association is called a Link. A Link is a tuple with one value for each end of the association, where each value is an instance of the type of the end. Links can be represented in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Relation Map' space='MT'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix' space='MT'].

With our modeling tool, you can display on diagrams the following:

- Links created between Instance specifications (the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying paths' space='MT'] command on the Instance specification [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Shortcut menu' space='CDH'] ).
- Instance specifications at the other end of a Link (the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MT'] command on the Instance specification shortcut menu).

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Object diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Relation Map' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']

#### PANEL: Related References

Related References

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=228985977 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00243: Location Kind

- page_id: `228985977`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228985977/Location+Kind
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:30:42.066+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Enumeration of the possible kinds of location applicable to an ActualLocation. Its enumeration literals are:

- SolidVolume - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is the amount of space occupied by a three-dimensional object of definite shape; not liquid or gaseous.
- Surface - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a portion of space having length and breadth but no thickness or regards to time.
- Line - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a geometric figure formed by a point moving along a fixed direction and the reverse direction.
- Point - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a unidimensional Individual.
- GeoStationaryPoint - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a unidimensional Individual.
- PlanarSurface - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a two-dimensional portion of space.
- PolygonArea - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a space enclosed by a polygon.
- RectangularArea - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a space enclosed by a rectangle.
- ElipticalArea - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a space enclosed by an ellipse.
- CircularArea - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a space enclosed by a circle.
- Other - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a LocationKind that is not on the enumerated list.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description </strong></p><p>Enumeration of the possible kinds of location applicable to an ActualLocation. Its enumeration literals are:</p><ul><li>SolidVolume - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is the amount of space occupied by a three-dimensional object of definite shape; not liquid or gaseous. </li><li>Surface - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a portion of space having length and breadth but no thickness or regards to time. </li><li>Line - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a geometric figure formed by a point moving along a fixed direction and the reverse direction. </li><li>Point - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a unidimensional Individual. </li><li>GeoStationaryPoint - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a unidimensional Individual. </li><li>PlanarSurface - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a two-dimensional portion of space. </li><li>PolygonArea - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a space enclosed by a polygon. </li><li>RectangularArea - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a space enclosed by a rectangle. </li><li>ElipticalArea - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a space enclosed by an ellipse. </li><li>CircularArea - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a space enclosed by a circle. </li><li>Other - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a LocationKind that is not on the enumerated list.</li></ul><p><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=228985978 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00244: Location Type Kind

- page_id: `228985978`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228985978/Location+Type+Kind
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:30:42.291+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Enumeration of the possible kinds of location type that are applicable to a Location. Its enumeration literals are:

- OtherType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of is a LocationKindType that is not on the enumerated list.
- SolidVolumeType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of amount of space occupied by a three-dimensional object of definite shape; not liquid or gaseous.
- SurfaceType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of portion of space having length and breadth but no thickness or regards to time.
- LineType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of geometric figure formed by a point moving along a fixed direction and the reverse direction.
- PointType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of unidimensional Individual.
- GeoStationaryPointType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of unidimensional Individual.
- PlanarSurfaceType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of is a twodimensional portion of space.
- PolygonAreaType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of space enclosed by a polygon.
- RectangularAreaType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of space enclosed by a rectangle.
- ElipticalAreaType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of space enclosed by an ellipse.
- CircularAreaType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of space enclosed by a circle.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Its enumeration literals are:</p><ul><li>OtherType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of is a LocationKindType that is not on the enumerated list. </li><li>SolidVolumeType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of amount of space occupied by a three-dimensional object of definite shape; not liquid or gaseous. </li><li>SurfaceType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of portion of space having length and breadth but no thickness or regards to time. </li><li>LineType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of geometric figure formed by a point moving along a fixed direction and the reverse direction. </li><li>PointType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of unidimensional Individual. </li><li>GeoStationaryPointType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of unidimensional Individual. </li><li>PlanarSurfaceType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of is a twodimensional portion of space. </li><li>PolygonAreaType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of space enclosed by a polygon. </li><li>RectangularAreaType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of space enclosed by a rectangle. </li><li>ElipticalAreaType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of space enclosed by an ellipse. </li><li>CircularAreaType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of space enclosed by a circle.</li></ul><p><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p>
