<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967846 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00245: Loop, Sequence, and Conditional Nodes

- page_id: `243967846`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967846/Loop+Sequence+and+Conditional+Nodes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:42.659+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Node
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page:**

##### Loop Node

A Loop Node is a constructed activity node representing a loop with the **setup**, **test**, and **body** sections.

- Each section is a well-nested subregion of an activity whose nodes follow any predecessors of the loop and precede any successors of the loop.
- The setup section usually initiates variables that are used in the loop's exit-condition. It is executed once on entry to the loop, and the test and body sections are executed repeatedly until the test produces a false value.
- The test section defines the loop exit-condition, it may precede or follow the body section.
- The body can contain actions to be executed repeatedly until the test produces a false value.
- The results of the final execution of the test or body are available once the execution of the loop is completed.

##### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a Loop Node

1. Expand the Activity diagram palette, click the downward arrow button next to the Conditional Node/Loop Node/Sequence Node icon, and select **Loop Node** from the shortcut menu . [ATTACHMENT filename='select_loop_node.png']
2. Click on the Activity diagram pane. The Loop Node is created.

To learn more about using the Loop Node for simulation, open the SmallTestSamples.mdzip sample present in the*<install_root>\samples\simulation*directory and run the*TestActivity_LoopNode*Simulation Confirguration.

##### Sequence Node

A Sequence Node is a structured activity node that executes its actions in order.

When the Sequence Node is enabled, its executable nodes are executed in the specified order. When combined with flows, actions must also satisfy their control and data flow inputs before starting execution.

To create a Sequence Node

1. Expand the Activity diagram palette, click the downward arrow button next to the **Conditional Node/Loop Node/Sequence Node**icon, and select **Sequence Node** from the shortcut menu. 
 [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Click on the Activity diagram pane. The Sequence Node is created.

##### Conditional Node

A Conditional Node is a structured activity node representing an exclusive choice among a number of alternatives.

A Conditional Node consists of one or more clauses, each consisting of a test section and a body section. When the Conditional Node begins execution, the test sections of the clauses are executed.

- If one or more test sections yield a true value, one of the corresponding body sections will be executed.
- If more than one test section yields a true value, only one body section will be executed.
- The choice is nondeterministic unless the test sequence of clauses is specified. If no test section yields a true value, then no body section is executed; this may be a semantic error if output values are expected from a Conditional Node.

In general, a test section may be executed in any order, including simultaneously if the underlying execution architecture supports it. Therefore, the result may be nondeterministic if more than one test section can be true concurrently. To enforce the ordering of an evaluation, sequencing constraints may be specified among clauses.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a Conditional Node

1. Expand the Activity diagram palette, click the downward arrow button next to the **Conditional Node/Loop Node/Sequence Node**icon, and select **Conditional** **Node** from the shortcut menu. 
 [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Click on the Activity diagram pane. The Conditional Node is created.

To learn about using the Conditional Node for simulation, open the SmallTestSamples.mdzip sample present in the <install_root>\samples\simulation directory and run the TestActivity_ConditionalNode simulation configuration.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="871ec07c-6424-4eb6-8e61-aa4f9befec2c" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Loop Node</h3><p>A Loop Node is a constructed activity node representing a loop with the <strong>setup</strong>, <strong>test</strong>, and <strong>body</strong> sections.</p><ul><li>Each section is a well-nested subregion of an activity whose nodes follow any predecessors of the loop and precede any successors of the loop.</li><li>The <strong>setup </strong>section usually initiates variables that are used in the loop's exit-condition. It is executed once on entry to the loop, and the <strong>test </strong>and <strong>body </strong>sections are executed repeatedly until the test produces a <em>false</em> value.</li><li>The <strong><span class="hcp1">test </span></strong>section defines the loop exit-condition, it may precede or follow the <strong><span class="hcp1">body </span></strong>section.</li><li>The <strong>body</strong> can contain actions to be executed repeatedly until the <strong>test</strong> produces a <em>false</em> value.</li><li>The results of the final execution of the <strong><span class="hcp1">test </span></strong>or <strong><span class="hcp1">body </span></strong>are available once the execution of the loop is completed.</li></ul><h3><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Loop_node.png" /></ac:image></h3><p>To create a Loop Node</p><hr /><ol><li>Expand the Activity diagram palette, click the downward arrow button next to the <strong>Conditional Node/Loop Node/Sequence Node </strong><span>icon, and select <strong>Loop Node</strong> from the shortcut menu</span>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_loop_node.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Click on the Activity diagram pane. The Loop Node is created.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0f90c004-5f67-45cd-baae-90b164f87116"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To learn more about using the Loop Node for simulation, o<span>pen the SmallTestSamples.mdzip sample present in the </span><em style="text-align: left;">&lt;install_root&gt;\samples\simulation </em><span>directory and r</span><span>un the </span><em>TestActivity_LoopNode</em><span> Simulation Confirguration.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span style="font-size: 16.0px;letter-spacing: -0.006em;">Sequence Node</span></h3><p>A Sequence Node is a structured activity node that executes its actions in order.</p><p>When the Sequence Node is enabled, its executable nodes are executed in the specified order. When combined with flows, actions must also satisfy their control and data flow inputs before starting execution.</p><p>To create a Sequence Node</p><hr /><ol><li><span>Expand the Activity diagram palette, click the downward arrow button next to the <strong>Conditional Node/Loop Node/Sequence Node </strong><span>icon, and select <strong>Sequence Node</strong> from the shortcut menu</span>.<br /> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_sequence_node.png" /></ac:image> </span></li><li>Click on the Activity diagram pane. The Sequence Node is created. </li></ol><h3><span>Conditional Node</span></h3><p>A Conditional Node is a structured activity node representing an exclusive choice among a number of alternatives.</p><p>A Conditional Node consists of one or more clauses, each consisting of a <span class="hcp1">test</span> section and a <span class="hcp1">body</span> section. When the Conditional Node begins execution, the <span class="hcp1">test</span> sections of the clauses are executed.</p><ul><li>If one or more <span class="hcp1">test</span> sections yield a <em>true</em> value, one of the corresponding <span class="hcp1">body</span> sections will be executed. </li><li>If more than one <span class="hcp1">test</span> section yields a <em>true</em> value, only one <span class="hcp1">body</span> section will be executed. </li><li>The choice is nondeterministic unless the test sequence of clauses is specified. If no <span class="hcp1">test</span> section yields a <em>true</em> value, then no <span class="hcp1">body</span> section is executed; this may be a semantic error if output values are expected from a Conditional Node.</li></ul><p>In general, <span class="hcp1">a test</span> section may be executed in any order, including simultaneously if the underlying execution architecture supports it. Therefore, the result may be nondeterministic if more than one test section can be true concurrently. To enforce the ordering of an evaluation, sequencing constraints may be specified among clauses.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Conditional_node.png" /></ac:image></p><p>To create a Conditional Node </p><hr /><ol><li><span>Expand the Activity diagram palette, click the downward arrow button next to the <strong>Conditional Node/Loop Node/Sequence Node </strong><span>icon, and select <strong>Conditional</strong> <strong>Node</strong> from the shortcut menu</span>.<br /> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_conditional_node.png" /></ac:image> </span></li><li>Click on the Activity diagram pane. The Conditional Node is created. </li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="11b919ae-b758-4f6a-9871-60a57d094528"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To learn about using the Conditional Node for simulation, open the SmallTestSamples.mdzip sample present in the &lt;install_root&gt;\samples\simulation directory and run the TestActivity_ConditionalNode simulation configuration.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967928 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00246: Managing contents of smart package

- page_id: `243967928`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967928/Managing+contents+of+smart+package
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:44:13.162+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Smart Package
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

935555568

935555571

935555570

The following sections outline how to manage the smart package's contents once it is created. 
5

##### Including elements into a smart package

An element can be included in the smart package in one of two ways:

- Manually - the user selects to include the particular element. Manually included elements constitute the static contents.
- Automatically - the element meets the set of criteria defined by the user. Automatically included elements constitute the dynamic contents.

The membership in the smart package is not the UML ownership association. The owner of the element will not change after the element is included in the smart package.

To include an element into a smart package manually

Do either:

- In the Containment tree, select an element and drag it to the smart package. 
See the element included in the contents of the smart package. The element becomes a value of the **Additional Elements**property as well. You can include more than one element at once.To include *adjacent*elements, select a single element, and then hold down the Shift key while you click any other elements you want to add.To include *nonadjacent*elements, select a single element, and then hold down the Ctrl key while you click any other elements you want to add.

- Open the Specification window of the smart package and click the cell of the Additional Elements property value. Then click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] and in the open dialog, select one or more elements. Click OK when you are done. See the element(s) included in the contents of the smart package.

To include elements into a smart package automatically

1. Open the Specification window of the smart package.
2. Under the Content category, click the cell of the Query property value.
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] . The Query dialog opens.
4. In the dialog, define a set of criteria (query) for gathering the elements into the contents of the smart package. For instructions about defining criteria in the **Query**dialog, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying criteria for querying model' space='MT']. You can select to[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting criteria' space='MT'] (logical relationships calculated from inherited or inner elements of the model element) to provide additional information.
5. Close the Specification window. See the contents of the smart package updated with elements that meet the defined set of criteria. If the contents of the smart package are not updated, right-click the smart package and select **Refresh Contents** in the shortcut menu. If the smart package contains manually included elements (values of the **Additional Elements** property), they are joined with the automatically included elements, skipping duplicates.If the smart package has excluded elements (values of the **Excluded Elements** property), they do not appear in the dynamic contents of the smart package.

##### Removing elements from a smart package

To remove an element from a smart package

Do either:

- In the Containment tree, under the smart package, right-click the element. From the shortcut menu, select **Delete from Contents**or press**Delete**. You can exclude more than one element at once.To exclude *adjacent*elements, select a single element, and hold down the Shift key while you click other elements you want to exclude.To exclude *nonadjacent*elements, select a single element, and hold down the Ctrl key while you click other elements that you want to exclude.

- Open the Specification window of the smart package and click the cell of the Excluded Elements property value. Then click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] and in the open dialog, select one or more elements. Click OK when you are done.
- Open the Specification window of the smart package and click the cell of the **Additional Elements**property value. Then click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and in the open dialog, unselect one or more elements. Click **OK**when you are done. This is only valid for manually added elements. See the element removed from the contents of the smart package, though it is not removed from the model. You can still see the element displayed under the owner of the element in the Containment tree.

##### Freezing the contents of a smart package

If you do not want the contents of the Smart Package to be updated automatically, you can suspend the calculation of the Smart Package contents. As a result, elements gathered according to a set of criteria specified in the **Query** dialog are no longer automatically added to the Smart Package contents. You can undo this at any time by changing the **Frozen** state.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Specification window of the Smart Package with the suspended contents.

To freeze the contents of a Smart Package

- In the Containment tree, right-click the Smart Package with the dynamic contents, and from the shortcut menu, select **Frozen**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The contents of the smart package will no longer be automatically updated after the relevant changes are made in the model.

You can update the contents of the Smart Package on demand without the need to change the **Frozen** state to disabled by performing the **Refresh Contents** action.

To refresh the contents of a Smart Package on demand

- In the Containment tree, right-click the Smart Package with the dynamic contents, and from the shortcut menu, select Refresh Contents. The contents of the Smart Package is successfully refreshed. [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh_smart_package_content_frozen_enabled.png']

##### Making a snapshot of the smart package

You can create a copy of the smart package with the static contents, that is, a snapshot.

To make a snapshot of a smart package

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the smart package with dynamic contents, and from the shortcut menu, select Snapshot Contents . The element Selection dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, select the owner of the new smart package and click OK . See the new smart package created under the selected owner in the Containment tree with the name Snapshot of <smart package name> , for example, Snapshot of My Bookmarks . The contents of the snapshot equal the contents of the smart package, but the list of elements is static.

935555565

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967982 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00247: Managing regions

- page_id: `243967982`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967982/Managing+regions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:47.157+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > State Machine > State
- labels: ['create-region', 'managing-regions', 'remove-region', 'insert-state', 'hide-region']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#createregionTo create a region

Do one of the following:

- In the Containment tree, right-click the State, select Create Element , and then click Region .
- On the diagram, right-click the State, then select the Add New Region .
- On the diagram, select the State symbol and click the **Create Region**button [ATTACHMENT filename='worddav3124c17bcd90e77e6ec5a12cec700032.png'] .
- In the State Specification window, click the Inner Elements property group, then click Create and select Region . Specify the region and click Close or Back .

To remove a region

Do one of the following:

- On the diagram, right-click the State and select Remove Region .
- In the State Specification window, click the Inner Elements property group. Then click the region and click the Delete button.

To insert a State to the region quickly

- In the Containment tree or on the diagram, select the State, and drag it to the State's region.

To display or hide the region name

1. On the diagram, right-click the State and select Symbol Properties .
2. In the Symbol Properties dialog, in the right-top corner, click to expand the menu, and select the Expert mode.
3. Select or clear the Show Region Name check box.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='MT']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f528246b-f6c6-4586-a7b1-84ae1919afd6"><ac:parameter ac:name="">#createregion</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To create a region</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li>In the Containment tree, right-click the State, select <strong>Create Element</strong>, and then click <strong>Region</strong>.</li><li>On the diagram, right-click the State, then select the <strong>Add New Region</strong>.</li><li>On the diagram, select the State symbol and click <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="53ac4cb4-c808-4c1a-84bd-c5d1f3180631">the <strong>Create Region</strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="53ac4cb4-c808-4c1a-84bd-c5d1f3180631"> button<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="53ac4cb4-c808-4c1a-84bd-c5d1f3180631"> </ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddav3124c17bcd90e77e6ec5a12cec700032.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="State" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="53ac4cb4-c808-4c1a-84bd-c5d1f3180631">.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>In the State Specification window, click the<strong> Inner Elements</strong> property group, then click <strong>Create</strong> and select <strong>Region</strong>. Specify the region and click <strong>Close</strong> or <strong>Back</strong>.</li></ul><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To remove a region</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li>On the diagram, right-click the State and select <strong>Remove Region</strong>.</li><li>In the State Specification window, click the <strong>Inner Elements</strong> property group. Then click the region and click the <strong>Delete</strong> button.</li></ul><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To insert a State to the region quickly</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Containment tree or on the diagram, select the State, and drag it to the State's region.</li></ul><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To display or hide the region name</p><hr /><ol><li>On the diagram, right-click the State and select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog, in the right-top corner, click to expand the menu, and select the <strong>Expert</strong> mode.</li><li>Select or clear the <strong>Show Region Name</strong> check box.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:title="Displaying region name on the state symbol" ac:alt="Displaying region name on the state symbol" ac:height="302" ac:width="488"><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddav7e2522d1bb1a6c372823308e03937e9d.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="State" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a1cb6614-da6a-4a7b-adf1-3ec18e11a18c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Stereotype" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967763 space=MED version=3 -->
````
## PAGE 00248: Manifestations

- page_id: `243967763`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967763/Manifestations
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:44:16.572+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1058526979

1058526981

1058526980

An artifact embodies or manifests a number of model elements. It owns the manifestations, each representing the utilization of a packageable element.

To create the manifestations, simply draw the Manifestation link from an artifact to a component.

To display the manifested artifacts on the component shape

- On the diagram pane, select the component shape, click the Compartments button, and from the list, select Artifacts .
- In the component Symbol Properties dialog, cancel the selection of the Suppress Artifacts check box.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1058527124

**Related pages**

- Model Elements
- Component diagram
- Specification Window
- Artifact
- Compartment

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

It owns the manifestations, each representing the utilization of a packageable element.</p><p>To create the manifestations, simply draw the Manifestation link from an artifact to a component.</p><p><br /></p><p>To display the manifested artifacts on the component shape</p><hr /><ul><li>On the diagram pane, select the component shape, click the Compartments button, and from the list, select <strong>Artifacts</strong>.</li><li>In the component <strong>Symbol Properties </strong>dialog, cancel the selection of the <strong>Suppress Artifacts</strong> check box.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="manifestation.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1a790420-28ce-4670-a9b5-66ce00246c2c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1058527124</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Model+elements">Model Elements</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Component+diagram">Component diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Specification+window">Specification Window</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Artifacts">Artifact</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Compartments">Compartment</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967765 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00254: Message

- page_id: `243967765`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967765/Message
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:41.004+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: ['synchcall', 'asynchcall', 'asynchsignal', 'reply', 'createmessage', 'deletemessage', 'message-syntax', 'message-sort']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Example

372179598

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Example

372179600

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Example

372179599

#### EXCERPT: Example

INLINE

A Message defines a particular communication between [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Lifeline' space=''] of an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space=''], such as raising a Signal, invoking an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space=''], or creating or destroying an Instance. Messages specify the kind of communication, the sender, and the receiver.

A Message is represented by arrows between the lifelines. The style of the Message line and arrowhead reflect the types of the Message.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] [IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can see the description of a selected property in the description area of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']. To see descriptions, be sure the **Show Description** option is selected.

##### Message sorts

| Message Sort | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| synchCall | The Message was generated by a synchronous call to an Operation. Synchronous Messages can overtake each other. When a Message represents an Operation, the arguments of the Message must correspond to the parameters of the Operation. |  |
| asynchCall | Asynchronous means that the caller continues immediately after the call. asynchCall Messages do not have reply Messages. |  |
| asynchSignal | The Message was generated by an asynchronous send Action. The argument of the Message must correspond to the attributes of the Signal. |  |
| reply | A reply Message to an Operation call. |  |
| createMessage | The Message designates the creation of another Lifeline object. No other Messages on a given Lifeline in an Interaction operand may appear above a Lifeline. |  |
| deleteMessage | The Message designates the termination of another Lifeline. |  |

##### Message syntax

The Message name is displayed on the message path on the diagram pane. The syntax of the message name is as follows:

*<messageident> ::= [<attribute> '='] <signal or operation name> ['(' [<argument> [','*

*<argument>]* ')'] [':' <return value>]*

*<argument> ::= ([<parameter name> '='] <argument-value>) / '-'*

#### TIP: Example

Example

*v=fo(P1="a", P2=True):12*

****

**Common actions with Messages**

To set an action type for a Message

- In the Message [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'] , click the Message Sort property value cell and select the action type from the drop-down list.
- On the diagram pane, right-click the Message and select a desired action type from the shortcut menu.

To show / hide Message numbers

1. Right-click the diagram pane to open its shortcut menu.
2. Then do one of the following:

- In a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MT'] or Time diagram , select / clear Show Message Numbers .
- In a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Communication diagram' space='MT'] , select / clear Numbering > Show Message Numbers .

372179596

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Lifeline' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="822ba8cc-c0ec-4d97-8f11-8258e93001ed"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">372179598</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b139e57a-2206-4ce5-aee2-a45e8fa1106e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">372179600</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36cf709c-1a3e-4970-b217-d30ba5559c34"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">372179599</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="06e751a1-0bf7-4bf6-8901-22b44fe37360"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>A Message defines a particular communication between <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Lifeline" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lifelines]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interaction Use" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Interaction]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, such as raising a Signal, invoking an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operation" /></ac:link>, or creating or destroying an Instance. Messages specify the kind of communication, the sender, and the receiver.</p><p>A Message is represented by arrows between the lifelines. The style of the Message line and arrowhead reflect the types of the Message.</p><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:title="Example of Message in Sequence diagram" ac:alt="Example of Message in Sequence diagram" ac:height="192" ac:width="292"><ri:attachment ri:filename="example_message_sequence_diagram.png" /></ac:image>    <ac:image ac:border="true" ac:title="Example of Message in Communication diagram" ac:alt="Example of Message in Communication diagram" ac:height="230" ac:width="367"><ri:attachment ri:filename="example_message_communication_diagram.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br />You can see the description of a selected property in the description area of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. To see descriptions, be sure the <strong>Show Description</strong> option is selected.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Message sorts</span></h3><div class="WordSection3"><p><br /></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup> <col /> <col /> <col /> </colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p>Message Sort</p></th><th>Description</th><th>Example</th></tr><tr><td colspan="1">synchCall</td><td colspan="1"><p>The Message was generated by a synchronous call to an Operation. Synchronous Messages can overtake each other. When a Message represents an Operation, the arguments of the Message must correspond to the parameters of the Operation.</p></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="105" ac:width="230"><ri:attachment ri:filename="synchCall_message.png" /></ac:image></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">asynchCall</td><td colspan="1">Asynchronous means that the caller continues immediately after the call. asynchCall Messages do not have reply Messages.</td><td rowspan="2"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="109" ac:width="291"><ri:attachment ri:filename="asynchCall_message.png" /></ac:image></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">asynchSignal</td><td colspan="1">The Message was generated by an asynchronous send Action. The argument of the Message must correspond to the attributes of the Signal.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">reply</td><td colspan="1">A reply Message to an Operation call.</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="127" ac:width="226"><ri:attachment ri:filename="reply_message.png" /></ac:image></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">createMessage</td><td colspan="1">The Message designates the creation of another Lifeline object. No other Messages on a given Lifeline in an Interaction operand may appear above a Lifeline.</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="149" ac:width="281"><ri:attachment ri:filename="createMessage.png" /></ac:image></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">deleteMessage</td><td colspan="1">The Message designates the termination of another Lifeline.</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="135" ac:width="201"><ri:attachment ri:filename="deleteMessage.png" /></ac:image></div></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><div class="WordSection1"><h3>Message syntax</h3><p>The Message name is displayed on the message path on the diagram pane. The syntax of the message name is as follows:</p><p><em>&lt;messageident&gt; ::= [&lt;attribute&gt; '='] &lt;signal or operation name&gt; ['(' [&lt;argument&gt; [','</em></p><p><em>&lt;argument&gt;]* ')'] [':' &lt;return value&gt;]</em></p><p><em>&lt;argument&gt; ::= ([&lt;parameter name&gt; '='] &lt;argument-value&gt;) / '-'</em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="32eca1a1-94ec-4a40-9b52-7326980fbe2d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p> <em>v=fo(P1=&quot;a&quot;, P2=True):12</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong style="color: rgb(0,0,0);font-size: 16.0px;line-height: 1.5;"> <br /></strong></p><p><strong style="color: rgb(0,0,0);font-size: 16.0px;line-height: 1.5;">Common actions with Messages</strong></p><p>To set an action type for a Message</p><hr />Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>In the Message <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, click the <strong>Message Sort</strong> property value cell and select the action type from the drop-down list.</li><li>On the diagram pane, right-click the Message and select a desired action type from the shortcut menu.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To show / hide Message numbers</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the diagram pane to open its shortcut menu.</li><li>Then do one of the following:</li></ol><ul><li>In a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Sequence ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>or <span class="confluence-link">Time diagram</span>, select / clear <strong>Show Message Numbers</strong>.</li><li>In a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Communication diagram" /></ac:link>, select / clear <strong>Numbering</strong> &gt; <strong>Show Message Numbers</strong>.</li></ul></div></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c4de03a6-4eaa-4069-9eb0-ec522c4f826a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">372179596</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="73f9a815-d600-41a3-8baf-9069d61d4884" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Lifeline" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lifelines]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interaction Use" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Interaction]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operation" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967781 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00255: Message name parsing

- page_id: `243967781`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967781/Message+name+parsing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:41.274+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Message
- labels: ['parsing-rules']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

372310483

372310488

372310487

INLINE

Arguments, parameters, and return values specified on a Message in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MT'] can be parsed and new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space='']can be created. Parsing and mapping Message names to real model elements (operations, parameters, etc.) allows for easy transition from prototype Sequence diagrams to complete Sequence diagrams representing actual Interactions between system parts.

##### Message name parsing

On a Message name area on a diagram pane, you can type the name of a new Operation together with arguments, parameters, and return value. After you click on the button near the Message name, the new Operation will be created, together with its specifications.

##### Message syntax

The whole Message name should be written according to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space=''].

##### Message Parsing notification message

If any Message name syntax errors are found, users are warned and given suggestions for fixing these mistakes. The Message Parsing notification message opens, showing the .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the preceding example, you can see that the following appears on the Message name:

*getUserAddress(city=”Allen”, street=”700 Central Expy S”, No=”110”)*. After you click the Create New Operation (this message button [IMAGE alt='' src='']), the new *getUserAddress* Operation will be created. The following Message arguments will be created - *Allen*, *700 Central Expy S*, and *110*, together with the corresponding parameters - *city*, *street*, *No*.

To create a new operation from the diagram pane

1. Create a call Message (asynchCall or synchCall) between two Lifelines. The Lifeline that the call Message is connected to must have a type specified.The preceding figure shows that the *Address* type is assigned to the second Lifeline.
2. On the diagram pane, on the message, type the message name according to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space=''].
3. After you have typed the Message name, exit the edit mode by clicking on the diagram pane.
4. Select the Message label again. The Create New Operation according to this message button [IMAGE alt='' src=''] is displayed next to the Message name.
5. Click the Create New Operation according to this message button [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . A new Operation is created and represented in the message [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'] , the **Signature (operation)** property cell . Note that a new Operation is created only after you click the Create New Operation according to this message button [IMAGE alt='' src='']. If you do not click this button, the Message label will be treated as a Message name.If the syntax is written incorrectly, the opens.

##### Parsing rules

The following table describes Message name samples and parsing results:

| Purpose | Message name typed on the diagram pane | Action after typing on the message label | Result |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Create a new Operation | login () | Click the Create New Operation according to this message button . | The new operation is created: login ()The Operation is referenced in the Message , the Signature (operation) property cell.For the Lifeline type, the login operation is created. |
| After click on the Create New Operation according to this message button , a new Operation is always created. A new Operation is created even if an Operation with thesame name already exists. |  |  |  |
| Create a new Operation with an argument | login (“John”) | Click the Create New Operation according to this message button . | The new operation is created: login (unnamed1=”John”)The Operation is referenced in the Message , the Signature (operation) property cell.In the message Specification window > the Arguments property group, the following argument is created: John. For the Lifeline type, the login Operation is created.The login Operation’s parameter is created and with the message argument. |
| Different kinds of arguments are supported. For the following arguments the following value specifications are created:John - Opaque Expression12345 - Literal IntegerTrue - Literal Boolean“John” - Literal String (always when quotation-marks are used).For more information about value specification, see . |  |  |  |
| Create a new Operation with a parameter and an argument | login (username=”John”) | Click the Create New Operation according to this message button . | The new operation is created: login (username=”John”) The Operation is referenced in the Message , the Signature (operation) property cell.In the Message Specification window > the Arguments property group, the following argument is created: John. For the Lifeline type, the login operation is created. The login operation’s parameter is created and with the Message argument. |
| Create a new Operation with a return parameter | login () : true | Click the Create New Operation according to this message button . | The new operation is created: login () : trueThe Operation is listed in the Message , the Signature (operation) property cell.In the Message Specification window > the Arguments property group the following argument is created: true.For the classifier assigned to the Lifeline, the login Operation is created, together with the parameter.The login Operation’s parameter with the return direction is created. The Operation’s parameter is with the Message argument. |
| Create a new Operation for the Message together with a reply Message | status = login () | Click the Create New Operation according to this message button . | The new operation is created: status = login ()The Operation is listed in the message , the Signature (operation) property cell.In the Message Specification window > the Reply message property cell, the replay Message is created. (The Replay message property cell is displayed in Expert mode only.)3. The status argument is created in the replay Message. |
| Create a new argument for the MessageIf this is valid, the argument is added straight from the M essage label for the already- existing O peration. | Before was:login (username=John)Add theargument 1282:login (username=John, 1284) | Exit the Message label edit mode by clicking thediagram pane. | A new argument is created and the Message is highlighted with yellow color; that is, the validation warning appears informing you that a parameter is missing and the Message argument is not synchronized with the operation parameter.To resolve the validation warning:Click the Message. The Message smart manipulator toolbar displays.Click the button to synchronize the Message argument with the Operation parameter. |
| Update the argument | Before was:login (John)Now change the argument to:login (Joseph) | Exit the message label edit mode by clicking the diagram pane. | The argument is updated. |

##### incorrect_synaxNotification message about incorrect syntax

If the Message syntax is incorrect, you will receive the following notification message at the bottom right corner of the modelin tool's window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the first line of the Message Parsing notification message, you will see an incorrect message name. In the example, the incorrect Message name is *getUser Address(city=)*. Due to the incorrect Message name, parsing cannot be executed and a new Operation cannot be created. In the second paragraph of the Message Parsing notification message, you will see the explanation of which syntax is expected. Next, you can see an example of the correct Message syntax.

372310480

If you do not click this button, the Message label will be treated as a Message name.</p><p>If the syntax is written incorrectly, the <ac:link ac:anchor="incorrect_synta"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Message Parsing window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> opens.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Parsing rules</h3><p><br />The following table describes Message name samples and parsing results:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup> <col /> <col /> <col /> <col /> </colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Purpose</th><th><p>Message name typed on the diagram pane</p></th><th><p>Action after typing on the message label</p></th><th>Result</th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><p>Create a new Operation</p></td><td><em>login ()</em></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click the Create New Operation according to this message button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_new_message.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p></div></td><td><p>The new operation is created: <em style="line-height: 1.42857;">login ()</em></p><ol><li>The Operation is referenced in the Message <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, the <strong>Signature (operation)</strong> property cell.</li><li>For the Lifeline type, the login operation is created.</li></ol></td></tr><tr><td colspan="3"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b252c9c-01ab-4c37-b6c9-085eed94e15b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>After click on the Create New Operation according to this message button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_new_message.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>, a new Operation is always created. For the following arguments the following value specifications are created:</p><p><em>John</em> - Opaque Expression</p><p><em style="line-height: 1.42857;">12345</em> - Literal Integer</p><p><em style="line-height: 1.42857;">True</em> - Literal Boolean</p><p><em style="line-height: 1.42857;">“John”</em> - Literal String (always when quotation-marks are used).</p><p>For more information about value specification, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Assigning value specification as property value" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td>Create a new O<span style="line-height: 1.42857;">peration with </span>a parameter and an argument</td><td><p><em>login (username=”John”)</em></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper">Click the Create <span style="line-height: 1.42857;">New Operation </span> <span style="line-height: 1.42857;">according to this </span> <span style="line-height: 1.42857;">message button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_new_message.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</span></div></td><td><p> The new operation is created: <em style="line-height: 1.42857;"> <span style="line-height: 1.42857;">login (username=”John”)</span> </em></p><ol><li>The Operation is referenced in the Message <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, the <strong>Signature (operation)</strong> property cell.</li><li>In the Message Specification window &gt; the <strong>Arguments</strong> property group, the following argument is created: <em>John</em>. </li><li>For the Lifeline type, the <em>login</em> operation is created. </li><li>The <em>login</em> operation’s parameter is created and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Parameters synchronization" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[synchronized]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with the Message argument.</li></ol></td></tr><tr><td><p>Create a new Operation with a return parameter</p></td><td><em>login () : true</em></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><span>Click the Create </span> <span>New Operation </span> <span>according to this </span> <span>message button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_new_message.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</span></div></td><td><p>The new operation is created: <em>login () : true</em></p><ol><li>The Operation is listed in the Message <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, the <strong>Signature (operation)</strong> property cell.</li><li>In the Message Specification window &gt; the <strong>Arguments</strong> property group the following argument is created: <em>true</em>.</li><li>For the classifier assigned to the Lifeline, the <em>login</em> Operation is created, together with the parameter.</li><li>The <em>login</em> Operation’s parameter with the return direction is created. The Operation’s parameter is <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Parameters synchronization" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[synchronized]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with the Message argument.</li></ol></td></tr><tr><td><p>Create a new Operation for the Message together with a reply Message</p></td><td><em>status = login ()</em></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><span>Click the Create </span> <span>New Operation </span> <span>according to this </span> <span>message button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_new_message.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</span></div></td><td><p>The new operation is created: <em>status = login ()</em></p><ol><li>The Operation is listed in the message <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, the <strong>Signature (operation)</strong> property cell.</li><li>In the Message Specification window &gt; the <strong>Reply message</strong> property cell, the replay Message is created. (The <strong>Replay message</strong> property cell is displayed in Expert mode only.)</li></ol><p>3. The <em>status</em> argument is created in the replay Message.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Create a new argument for the Message</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc2828c6-d551-4405-b485-f7f01c4e8142"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If this is </span> <span>valid, the </span> <span>argument is </span> <span>added straight </span> <span>from the M</span> <span>essage label </span> <span>for the already-</span> <span>existing O</span> <span>peration.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td><td><p>Before was:</p><p><em>login (username=John)</em></p><p>Add the<br />argument <em>1282</em>:</p><p><em>login (username=John, 1284)</em></p></td><td><p>Exit the Message label edit mode by clicking the<br />diagram pane.</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>A new argument is created and the Message is highlighted with yellow color; that is, the validation warning appears informing you that a parameter is missing and the Message argument is not synchronized with the operation parameter.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="message_parsing3.png" /></ac:image></p><p>To resolve the validation warning:</p><ol><li>Click the Message. The Message smart manipulator toolbar displays.</li><li><p>Click the button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="validation_invalid.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to synchronize the Message argument with the Operation parameter.</p></li></ol></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Update the argument</p></td><td><p>Before was:</p><p><em>login (John)</em></p><p>Now change the argument to:</p><p><em>login (Joseph)</em></p></td><td><p>Exit the message label edit mode by clicking the diagram pane.</p></td><td>The argument is updated.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c29e24d-add7-4b14-9860-87ebefc4f27d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">incorrect_synax</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Notification message about incorrect syntax</h3><p><br />If the Message syntax is incorrect, you will receive the following notification message at the bottom right corner of the modelin tool's window.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Example of Message Parsing notification message" ac:alt="Example of Message Parsing notification message"><ri:attachment ri:filename="message_parsing2.png" /></ac:image></p><p>In the first line of the Message Parsing notification message, you will see an incorrect message name. In the example, the incorrect Message name is <em>getUser Address(city=)</em>. Due to the incorrect Message name, parsing cannot be executed and a new Operation cannot be created. In the second paragraph of the Message Parsing notification message, you will see the explanation of which syntax is expected. Next, you can see an example of the correct Message syntax.</p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d91674ef-cf72-4b83-98fd-96a77a5e21fa"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">372310480</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Lifeline" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lifelines]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interaction Use" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Interaction]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operation" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Message" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967813 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00256: Message Occurrence Specification

- page_id: `243967813`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967813/Message+Occurrence+Specification
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:44:11.192+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Message Occurrence Specification specifies the occurrence of message events, such as sending and receiving signals or invoking or receiving operation calls. A Message Occurrence Specification is a kind of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Messages' space='MT'] end. Messages are generated either by synchronous operation calls or asynchronous signal sends. They are received by the execution of the corresponding accept event actions. 
In the Message [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'], you can modify an element and add various specifications to it.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">A Message Occurrence Specification specifies the occurrence of message events, such as sending and receiving signals or invoking or receiving operation calls. A Message Occurrence Specification is a kind of <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Messages" /></ac:link> end. Messages are generated either by synchronous operation calls or asynchronous signal sends. They are received by the execution of the corresponding accept event actions. </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">In the M<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">essage <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></span></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">, you can modify an element and add various specifications to it.</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967801 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00257: Messages in Communication diagrams

- page_id: `243967801`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967801/Messages+in+Communication+diagrams
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:44:11.604+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Message
- labels: ['advanced-numbering']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

372507889

372507891

372507890

INLINE

Messages in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Communication diagram' space='MT'] can only be depicted on connectors; thus, you should draw a connector before assigning a Message to it.

The following steps show you how to work with messages:

##### Assigning a message to a connector

assignTo assign a message to a connector

1. On the diagram palette, click the button corresponding to the desired Message type.
2. Click a desired connector on the diagram pane. A Message arrow will be placed on the selected connector. A Message flow has two directions: right and left. Choose one of them by clicking the associated button on the diagram palette.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Setting/removing the advanced numbering of messages

set_removeTo set/remove the advanced numbering of messages

1. From the diagram shortcut menu, select Numbering .
2. Select to set, or clear to remove the Use Advanced Numbering .

##### Changing current message numbering

To change current message numbering

1. From the diagram shortcut menu, select Numbering > Change Numbering .
2. Increase, decrease, and / or change the level of numbering in the Change Communication Numbering dialog. [ATTACHMENT filename='change_communication_numbering.png']

If the **Edit** button is inactive, of Messages.

##### Displaying messages

If you want to display messages on connectors in the Communication diagram you must first implement the following conditions (see the figure below):

- Messages are assigned to connectors . ]]>
- The lifeline shapes are already displayed on the diagram pane. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Lifeline' space='']]]>
- The paths between lifelines are displayed on the diagram pane. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying paths' space='MT']]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To display messages

1. Select the connector between lifelines.
2. Perform one of the following actions: [ATTACHMENT filename='display_messages_command.png']
  - Right-click the connector to open the shortcut menu, and select Display > Display Messages .
  - On the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and select Display Messages .
3. In the opened Select Messages dialog, select the messages you want to display, and click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='select_messages_dialog.png'] Messages of the selected connector are displayed on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='displayed_messages.png']

372507887

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Communication diagram** sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download *[ATTACHMENT filename='communication diagram.mdzip'].*
- Find in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\diagrams\communication diagram.mdzip.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967791 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00258: Messages in Sequence and Time Diagrams

- page_id: `243967791`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967791/Messages+in+Sequence+and+Time+Diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:41.569+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Message
- labels: ['nested-activation']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

372457582

372457584

1277748670

**On this page:**

372457583

INLINE

Messages allow displaying an interaction between objects. A Message is labeled with either the message name or the assigned [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space=''](signal) name and its arguments.

##### Drawing Message from/to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Lifeline' space='']to/from diagram frame

Outgoing and incoming messages communicate with the outside environment by connecting to the border gates (the diagram frame).

##### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the example above, you can see the Message *card inserted* that is drawn from the diagram frame border to the Lifeline *controller*. The Message *authorize* is created from the Lifeline *controller* to the diagram frame.

For a Message to be correct, its receive end should be known. However, we never know who will connect to the gate from the outside. Thus, we are using the reply Message when creating the Message from the Lifeline to the diagram frame (see Message *authorize* in the figure).

##### Creating nested activation

Nested activations allow you to model:

- A parallel execution of operations that belong to a single [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''] .
- Callback messages.

The nested activation can be created between at least two messages that point to the same activation.

Nested activations can be created for the following messages:

1. Synchronous Call Message (synchCall)
2. Asynchronous Call Message (asynchCall)
3. Asynchronous Signal Message (asynchSignal)

createTo create a nested activation

Ensure that you have at least two messages pointing to the same activation in your model.

1. Select any subsequent Message.
2. Do one of the following:

- 
  - From the Message shortcut menu, select Create Nested Activation .
  - On the Message smart manipulator toolbar, click the Create Nested Activation button.

The Message will be connected to the nested activation.

To merge a nested activation with a parent activation

1. Select a Message that has a nested activation.
2. Do one of the following:

- 
  - From the Message shortcut menu, select Reduce Nesting Level .
  - On the Message smart manipulator toolbar, click the Reduce Nested Activation button.

The Message will be connected to the parent activation.

Nested activations can be used in the following cases:

- 
- 
- 

##### Modeling parallel executions

To model parallel executions for a non-active Lifeline

A non-active Lifeline is one that has a non-active Class as a type assigned. The non-active Class is the one whose Is Active property is set to false. This property is available in the Expert mode.

The figure below shows the creation of a simple nested activation, described in the procedure .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

active_lifelineTo model parallel executions for an active Lifeline

An active Lifeline is one that has an active Class as a type assigned. The active Class is the one whose Is Active property is set to true. This property is available in the Expert mode.

1. Select a Lifeline and from its shortcut menu, select Show Entire Activation . All activations of the selected Lifeline become all-in-one.
2. Create an outgoing Message or select an existing Message pointed to an active object. Be sure this is not the first one for the outgoing activation.
3. From the Message shortcut menu, select Create Nested Activation . [ATTACHMENT filename='nested_activations_3.png']

##### Modeling callback Messages

callback_messageTo model a callback Message

1. Create or select an incoming Message that is modeled as a callback Message.
2. From the Message shortcut menu, select Create Nested Activation . [ATTACHMENT filename='nested_activations_4.png']

##### Moving Messages

**Moving an individual Message**

You can move an individual Message up or down the lifeline simply by dragging it along its execution specification. If the Message is dragged above or below its execution specification, the latter will break and the Message will be connected to a new execution specification.

To move an individual Message

1. Select the Message and drag it up or down the execution specification to reposition it. [ATTACHMENT filename='move_individual_message_1.png']
2. (Optional) Drag the Message above or below its execution specification to break the Message off its execution specification and connect it to a new one. [ATTACHMENT filename='move_individual_message_2.png']

**Moving a Message together with subsequent Messages**

If you want to move a Message up or down along with all of the subsequent Messages, as well as break the existing execution specification and connect the Messages to the new execution specifications, you can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pusher and magnet' space='MT'].

**Moving dependent Messages**

You can also move a Message together with its dependent Messages up or down the lifeline by dragging it along its execution specification. If the Message is dragged above or below its execution specification, the latter will break and the Message, along with its dependent Messages, will be connected to the new execution specifications.

To move dependent Messages

1. On the diagram palette, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='move_dependent_messages_button.png'] Move Dependent Messages button.
2. On the diagram pane, select the Message you want to move. Dependent Messages are then selected automatically. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Drag the selected Messages up or down the execution specification to reposition them.
4. (Optional) Drag the Messages above or below their execution specification to break the Messages off their execution specification and connect them to the new ones. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If a Message is moved below or above its execution specification to break the latter and connect the Message to a new execution specification, the Message no longer depends on a Message it was dependent on prior to the breaking of the execution specification.

372457581

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='']

figure).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Creating nested activation</h3><p>Nested activations allow you to model:</p><ul><li>A parallel execution of operations that belong to a single Signal Message (asynchSignal)</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="21131900-cfe8-4130-82ad-64b575581986"><ac:parameter <strong>Reduce Nesting Level</strong>.</li><li>On the Message smart manipulator toolbar, click the Reduce Nested Activation button.</li></ul></li></ul><p>The Message will be connected to the parent activation.</p><p>Nested activations can be used in the following cases:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="non_active_lifeline"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[To model parallel executions for a non-active Lifeline]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="active_lifiline"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[To model parallel executions for an active Lifeline]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="callback_message"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[To model a callback Message]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><h3>Modeling parallel executions</h3><p>To model parallel executions for a non-active Lifeline</p><hr /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea1fbb43-ca56-470f-b6ca-baf8c9521103"><ac:rich-text-body><p>A non-active Lifeline is one that has a non-active Class as a type assigned. The non-active Class is the one whose Is Active property is set to false. This property is available in the Expert mode.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The figure below shows the creation of a simple nested activation, described in the procedure <ac:link ac:anchor="create"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[To create a nested activation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Parallel executions for non-active object" ac:alt="Parallel executions for non-active object"><ri:attachment ri:filename="nested_activations_2.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ddb1f214-f8bf-4195-9a51-893edab3fd5e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">active_lifeline</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To model parallel executions for an active Lifeline</p><hr /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dab381e8-79fc-488b-b5a0-6cf360c40cec"><ac:rich-text-body><p>An active Lifeline is one that has an active Class as a type assigned. The active Class is the one whose Is Active property is set to true. This property is available in the Expert mode.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ol><li>Select a Lifeline and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Show Entire Activation</strong>. All activations of the selected Lifeline become all-in-one.</li><li>Create an outgoing Message or select an existing Message pointed to an active object. Be sure this is not the first one for the outgoing activation.</li><li>From the Message shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Nested Activation</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Parallel executions for active object" ac:alt="Parallel executions for active object"><ri:attachment ri:filename="nested_activations_3.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><h3>Modeling callback Messages</h3><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e7fc8603-2b0a-4e50-9072-99d891797b1a"><ac:parameter ac:name="">callback_message</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To model a callback Message</p><hr /><ol><li>Create or select an incoming Message that is modeled as a callback Message.</li><li>From the Message shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Nested Activation</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Callback messages" ac:alt="Callback messages"><ri:attachment ri:filename="nested_activations_4.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><h3>Moving Messages</h3><p><strong>Moving an individual Message</strong></p><p>You can move an individual Message up or down the lifeline simply by dragging it along its execution specification. If the Message is dragged above or below its execution specification, the latter will break and the Message will be connected to a new execution specification. </p><p><br /></p><p>To move an individual Message</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Message and drag it up or down the execution specification to reposition it. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_individual_message_1.png" /></ac:image></li><li>(Optional) Drag the Message above or below its execution specification to break the Message off its execution specification and connect it to a new one. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_individual_message_2.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><strong>Moving a Message together with subsequent Messages</strong></p><p>If you want to move a Message up or down along with all of the subsequent Messages, as well as break the existing execution specification and connect the Messages to the new execution specifications, you can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Pusher and magnet" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Magnet and Pusher buttons]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </p><p><strong>Moving dependent Messages</strong></p><p>You can also move a Message together with its dependent Messages up or down the lifeline by dragging it along its execution specification. If the Message is dragged above or below its execution specification, the latter will break and the Message, along with its dependent Messages, will be connected to the new execution specifications. </p><p><br /></p><p>To move dependent Messages</p><hr /><ol><li>On the diagram palette, click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_dependent_messages_button.png" /></ac:image> <strong>Move Dependent Messages</strong> button.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">On the diagram pane, select the Message you want to move. Dependent Messages are then selected automatically. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_dependent_messages_1.png" /></ac:image> </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Drag the selected Messages up or down the execution specification to reposition them.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(Optional) Drag the Messages above or below their execution specification to break the Messages off their execution specification and connect them to the new ones. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_dependent_messages_2.png" /></ac:image> </span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1192f9c9-0174-4794-9d72-aae559299cb8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If a Message is moved below or above its execution specification to break the latter and connect the Message to a new execution specification, the Message no longer depends on a Message it was dependent on prior to the breaking of the execution specification.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> <br /></span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="19082c2e-bcb5-4eab-b708-e2c4c13238a8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">372457581</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Lifeline" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lifelines]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interaction Use" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Interaction]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operation" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Message" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967814 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00259: Metaclass

- page_id: `243967814`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967814/Metaclass
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-09T13:43:23.261+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The abstract syntax of UML is specified using a UML model called the UML metamodel. Classes in a metamodel are called metaclasses. The detailed descriptions of each metaclass are provided in the [ATTACHMENT filename='UML specification.pdf'].

The metaclass icon in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MT'] is [IMAGE alt='' src='']. Its shape is displayed with <metaclass> stereotype.

All metaclasses in our modeling tools are stored in the **Auxiliary Resources**, expand the **UML Standard Profile** Package > **UML 2 Metamodel** Package.

INLINE

To show the auxiliary resources

- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MT'] , click [ATTACHMENT filename='options_button_in_model_browser.png'] and enable the Show Auxiliary Resources option.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The abstract syntax of UML is specified using a UML model called the UML metamodel. Classes in a metamodel are called metaclasses. The detailed descriptions of each metaclass are provided in the <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="UML specification.pdf" /></ac:link>.</p><p>The metaclass icon in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link> is <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="metaclass_icon.png" /></ac:image>. Its shape is displayed with &lt;metaclass&gt; stereotype.</p><p>All metaclasses in our modeling tools are stored in the <strong>Auxiliary Resources</strong>, expand the <strong>UML Standard Profile</strong> Package &gt; <strong>UML 2 Metamodel</strong> Package.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b3403b2-dbc7-4bb2-a5d3-ee8762888f81"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To show the auxiliary resources</p><hr /><ul><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link>, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button_in_model_browser.png" /></ac:image> and enable the <strong>Show Auxiliary Resources</strong> option.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=228985984 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00260: Metadata

- page_id: `228985984`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228985984/Metadata
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:30:43.991+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A comment that can be applied to any element in the architecture. The attributes associated with this element details the relationship between the element and its related dublinCoreElement, metaDataScheme, category and name. This allows the element to be referenced using the Semantic Web.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Comment

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">A comment that can be applied to any element in the architecture. The attributes associated with this element details the relationship between the element and its related dublinCoreElement, metaDataScheme, category and name. This allows the element to be referenced using the Semantic Web.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span class="fontstyle0"><strong>Architecture</strong> <strong>Framework</strong></span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UML Comment</p><p><br /></p>
````

**Architecture Framework**

**Extension**

Data Type
