<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967821 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00270: Model

- page_id: `243967821`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967821/Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:42.217+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A model contains a (hierarchical) set of elements that describes the physical system being modeled. It can also contain a set of elements that represent the environment of the system (typically [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actor' space='']together with their interrelationships), such as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='']and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency' space=''].

A model is presented as a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='']with a small triangle in the upper right corner of the large rectangle. The triangle can be shown in the tab.

The model is defined as a package; that is, it has package properties in the Model [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'], as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Object diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actor' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>A model contains a (hierarchical) set of elements that describes the physical system being modeled. It can also contain a set of elements that represent the environment of the system (typically <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Actors ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>together with their interrelationships), such as <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Association" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Associations ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Dependency" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Dependencies]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>A model is presented as a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[package ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>with a small triangle in the upper right corner of the large rectangle. The triangle can be shown in the tab.</p><p>The model is defined as a package; that is, it has package properties in the Model <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, as shown below.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Model Specification window" ac:alt="Model Specification window"><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_specification.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bcb055ec-bcd9-463c-97e8-112f9e8d79a2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Object diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Class diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Dependency" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Actors]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Package" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Association" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Associations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967823 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00274: Node

- page_id: `243967823`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967823/Node
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:42.326+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Any computer or device that is relevant to an implemented system can be shown as a Node. A Node symbol is a three-dimensional square with a name on it. Devices in a system are typically represented with a stereotype that specifies the device type. The nodes can be represented as types and as instances.

You can specify Node properties in the Node **Specification** window, where you can also read descriptions of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the **Specification** window.

#### PANEL: Related References

Related References

true

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Any computer or device that is relevant to an implemented system can be shown as a <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="fb9a69ba-c49d-48c3-b109-13b94b31b301">Node</ac:inline-comment-marker>. A Node symbol is a three-dimensional square with a name on it. Devices in a system are typically represented with a stereotype that specifies the device type. The nodes can be represented as types and as instances.</p><p>You can specify Node properties in the Node <strong>Specification</strong> window, where you can also read descriptions of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the <strong>Specification</strong> window.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b3f18721-0655-48c2-b4dd-607c8ea97138"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related References</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="813fa02d-cd4e-4cb1-9e0d-c3345f8042d5"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36e20852-22dd-420b-8d4b-52b136b22981"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967857 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00275: Note

- page_id: `243967857`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967857/Note
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:42.863+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

562315847

562315850

562315849

A note is a graphical symbol containing textual information. It is used to add any explanatory information needed for your element or diagram. A note is usually connected to the element symbol using an anchor line, as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the note, you can also display and edit the elements the note is anchored to, such as documentation, constraint, and tagged value, as well as element properties.

Text in the note can be displayed as plain or HTML text.

You can format the note symbol properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MT'].

The Note anchored to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram frame' space='MT'] can represent the information of the diagram context element (for example, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine' space='']).

To switch the representation to the diagram information

1. Right-click the Note.
2. In the shortcut menu clear the selection of Represent Diagram Context .

To switch the note text between HTML and Plain text modes

- On the diagram pane, select the note symbol and click either HTML or Plain button.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can edit the HTML text in the note using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='HTML editor' space='MT'].

To display the element documentation on a note

1. Draw a note to an element whose documentation you want to display.
2. From the note shortcut menu, select Text Display Mode > Show Documentation .

You can edit element documentation directly on the note symbol.

INLINE

To display element properties, constraints, and/or tagged values on a note symbol

To see compartment editing results, make sure element properties, constraints, and/or tagged values are displayed on the note symbol.

1. Draw a note to an element whose documentation you want to display.
2. Select a note and click the Compartments button on the left.
3. In the list, click to select the following:

- 
  - **Element Properties**
  - **Constraints**
  - **Tagged Values**

Element properties, constraints, and / or tagged values are displayed on the note symbol in the separate compartments.

You can choose which element properties, constraints, or tagged values to display on the note symbol by editing an appropriate compartment.

To edit a compartment on a note symbol

1. Open the Compartment Edit dialog by doing one of the following:
  - From the note shortcut menu, select Edit Compartments .
  - Select a note and click Edit Element Properties on the right. [ATTACHMENT filename='note_compartment_edit.png']
2. Move elements between Hidden and Selected lists using the > , < , >> , or << buttons . Only items from the selected list will be displayed in the compartment on the note’s symbol.
3. Click OK after you are done.

To add a hyperlink to an element on a note symbol

- Select one or more elements in the Containment tree and drag them to the note symbol on the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After the hyperlinks on the note are created, the text format of the note changes to HTML.

562315846

**Realted pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram frame' space='MT']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967862 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00276: Object Flow

- page_id: `243967862`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967862/Object+Flow
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:42:27.114+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

854866585

854866587

854866586

You can view Object Flows that are connected via Pins in the Action's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'], as well as the **Display Related Elements** and **Display Paths** commands are available from the Action's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Shortcut menu' space='CDH'].

To select output/input pin automatically when drawing an Object Flow

- When drawing an Object Flow, our modeling tool automatically selects Output or Input Pins, creates a new Pin, under certain conditions, or you can choose a Pin from the list of options. For example, a list of available options to select input Pins opens when drawing Object Flow to the Action that has more than one hidden Input Pin.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To split Object Flows

- From an Object Flow shortcut menu on a diagram pane, select Refactor and then select Split Object Flow . [ATTACHMENT filename='split_object_flow.png']

- The **Split Object Flow** command splits Object Flow into two connectors that are marked with an A letter.
- Double-click the connector to select the other connector on the diagram pane.
- Splitting of object flows helps to navigate in a complicated diagram.
- For more information about Refactoring, read [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Refactoring' space='MT'] .

To join Object Flow connectors

- From a connector's shortcut menu, select Refactor and then select Join Connectors . [IMAGE alt='' src='']

To set type of Pins by using a drag-and-drop operation

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MT'], select a type of pins.
2. Drag the type to an object flow on a diagram. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- If type of pins is set already, after drag-and-drop type will be changed to the new one.
- To display a type of a pin on a diagram, from the pin shape shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties and then select the Show Type check box.

To create Central Buffer Nodes on Object Flows by using a drag-and-drop operation

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MT'] , select a type of a Central Buffer Node, and drag it to an Object Flow path on a diagram pane.
2. Wait for a few seconds and drop it.
3. Select the Split the flow command. [ATTACHMENT filename='select_split_flow.png']
4. The Split Object Flow message opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='message_split_object_flow.png']
5. Select to insert the Central Buffer Node before or after the Object Flow. [ATTACHMENT filename='splitted_flow.png']

854866584

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

other connector on the diagram pane. </span></li><li>Splitting of object flows helps to navigate in a complicated diagram.</li><li>For more information about Refactoring, read <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Refactoring" /></ac:link>. </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span><br /></span></p><p><span>To join Object Flow connectors</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span>From a connector's shortcut menu, select </span><strong>Refactor </strong><span>and then select </span><strong>Join Connectors</strong><span>. </span><br /><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="join_object_flow.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To set type of Pins by using a drag-and-drop operation</p><hr /><ol><li><p>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link>, select a type of pins.</p></li><li><p>Drag the type to an object flow on a diagram.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="drag_type_to_object_flow.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b084fe7-06b5-4a43-b491-34d71b6cb196"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If type of pins is set already, after drag-and-drop type will be changed to the new one.</li><li>To display a type of a pin on a diagram, from the pin shape shortcut menu, select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> and then select the <strong>Show Type</strong> check box.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span><br /></span></p><p><span>To create Central Buffer Nodes on Object Flows by using a drag-and-drop operation</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link>, select a type of a Central Buffer Node, and drag it to an Object Flow path on a diagram pane.</li><li>Wait for a few seconds and drop it.</li><li>Select the <strong>Split the flow</strong> command. <br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_split_flow.png" /></ac:image></li><li>The <strong>Split Object Flow</strong> message opens. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="message_split_object_flow.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Select to insert the Central Buffer Node before or after the Object Flow.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="splitted_flow.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3bbc90d4-fec6-4ceb-8088-f4ae2cee168b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">854866584</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967877 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00277: Object Node

- page_id: `243967877`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967877/Object+Node
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:42:26.298+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: More Information

853341294

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: More Information

853341297

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: More Information

853341295

An Object Node is an Activity node indicating that an instance of a particular classifier, possibly in a particular State, may be available at a particular point in the Activity. It can be used in a variety of ways (depending on where the objects are flowing from and to), as described in the semantics section.

#### TIP: More Information

More Information

For more information about working with symbols, please see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='MT'].

As UML2 does not support generic Object Node, our modeling tool creates the particular Central Buffer Node element.

You can specify Object Node properties in the Central Buffer Node [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']. You can find the description of each [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MT'] in the same window. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window.

To set a classifier to an object node

1. Double-click the Object Node or select Specification from the shape's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Shortcut menu' space='CDH'] . The Central Buffer Node Specification window opens.
2. Select the classifier you wish to assign to an Object Node from the Type drop-down list.

To assign a state or final state to an object node

1. Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] button in the Central Buffer Node Specification window, In State value property. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='MT']**Select Elements** dialog opens.
2. Select a State from the existing model elements, or click Create to create a new one. The State Specification window opens. Specify a new state, to be assigned to an Object Node.

To convert an object node to a pin

1. Select an Object Node on the diagram pane.
2. From the Object Node's shortcut menu, select the Refactor command, then select Convert To .
3. In the open list, select to convert the Object Node to the Input Pin or Output Pin. [ATTACHMENT filename='Object.png']

853341292

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=9919447 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00278: Objective Function

- page_id: `9919447`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/9919447/Objective+Function
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:46:24.727+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > SysML v1 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Objective Function (also known as 'optimization' or 'cost function') is used for determining the overall value of an alternative in terms of weighted criteria and/or Moe's.

#### PANEL: Related diagrams

Related diagrams

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Parametric Diagram' space='MT']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967479 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00279: Opaque Action

- page_id: `243967479`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967479/Opaque+Action
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:32.993+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Action
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

907130055

907130058

907130057

The Opaque Action is introduced for implementation-specific actions, or for use as a temporary placeholder before other actions are chosen.

The Opaque Action has no specific notation.

There is an additional property named **Body and Language** in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'] of an Opaque Action.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

907130054

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967879 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00280: Opaque Behavior

- page_id: `243967879`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967879/Opaque+Behavior
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:43.236+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A behavior with implementation-specific semantics. The opaque behavior is introduced for implementation-specific behavior or for use as a place-holder before one of the other behaviors is chosen.

To create an opaque behavior

1. In the Containment tree, select a package and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element > Opaque Behavior .
2. Enter a name for the new opaque behavior.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'] of the opaque behavior, click the cell of the Body and Language property value, and then click [ATTACHMENT filename='add.png'] . The Body and Language dialog opens.
4. From the Language drop-down list, select a language.
5. In the Body area, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying criteria for querying model' space='MT'] .

To change an opaque behavior display mode on state or transition shapes

1. In the State or Transition shape shortcut menu, select [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MT'] .
2. In the Opaque Behavior Display Mode property value cell, select either Body or Name .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a symbol of an opaque behavior

- In the Containment tree, select the opaque behavior and drag it to the diagram pane.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>A behavior with implementation-specific semantics. The opaque behavior is introduced for implementation-specific behavior or for use as a place-holder before one of the other behaviors is chosen.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an opaque behavior</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment tree, select a package and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Element</strong> &gt; <strong>Opaque Behavior</strong>.</li><li>Enter a name for the new opaque behavior.</li><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> of the opaque behavior, click the cell of the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Body and Language</strong> property value, and then click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add.png" /></ac:image>. The <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Body and Language</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>From the <strong>Language</strong> drop-down list, select a language.</li><li>In the <strong>Body</strong> area, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specifying criteria for querying model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[define the expression]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To change an opaque behavior display mode on state or transition shapes</p><hr /><ol><li>In the State or Transition shape shortcut menu, select <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>In the <strong>Opaque Behavior Display Mode</strong> property value cell, select either <strong>Body</strong> or <strong>Name</strong>.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="opaque_behavior_body.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To create a symbol of an opaque behavior</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Containment tree, select the opaque behavior and drag it to the diagram pane.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36e20852-22dd-420b-8d4b-52b136b22981"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967571 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00281: Operation

- page_id: `243967571`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967571/Operation
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:44:12.544+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Class
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1778044973

1778044987

1778044977

Entries in the operation compartment are strings that show the operations defined on classes as well as those that are supplied by the classes. An operation is a service that can be requested to perform by an instance of the class. It has a name and a list of arguments.

Usually, class attributes are accessed through the operations. The operations are used to perform specific actions, such as system calls, utility functions, and queries. The operation signature provides all information needed to use that operation.

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MT'] in the operation [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']. In the same window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window.

To create a new operation, do one of the following

- On the diagram, select the class, click the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulation' space='MT'] > Operation .
- In the class Specification window > Operations property group, click the Create button.
- In the Containment tree, from the class shortcut menu, select Create Element > Operation .
- On the diagram, select the class and press the Ctrl+Alt+O.

To define the type of an operation, do one of the following

- Select an operation, type ":" and then specify the type. If you add a non-existent type, a new class is created for that type. After typing the ":", press Ctrl+Space or Ctrl+Backspace to get a list of suggested types.
- In the Operation Specification window, the Type property, select the operation type.

To quickly create a behavior or a diagram for an operation

1. From the operation shortcut menu, select Create Method and either Behavior , or Diagram.
2. Select one of either behaviors or diagrams you wish to create.

To add an operation parameter, do one of the following

- In the Operation Specification window, Parameters property group, click the Create button.
- On the diagram, select an operation and type a parameter text in parenthesis.
- Select an operation in the Containment tree and for the shortcut menu, select Create Element > Parameter .

1778044972

**Related Pages**

- Model Elements
- Specification Window
- Parameter

In the same window, you can find the description of each property. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a new operation, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>On the diagram, select the class, click the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Smart manipulation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> &gt; <strong>Operation</strong>.</li><li>In the class Specification window &gt; <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Operations</strong> property group, click the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Create</strong> button.</li><li>In the Containment tree, from the class shortcut menu, select <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Create Element</strong> &gt; <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Operation</strong>.</li><li>On the diagram, select the class and press the Ctrl+Alt+O.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To define the type of an operation, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li><p>Select an operation, type &quot;:&quot; and then specify the type. If you add a non-existent type, a new class is created for that type.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ae10058-c3f2-487e-9059-2aa9a80c07d5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>After typing the &quot;:&quot;, press Ctrl+Space or Ctrl+Backspace to get a list of suggested types.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>In the Operation Specification window, the <strong>Type</strong> property, select the operation type.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To quickly create a behavior or a diagram for an operation</p><hr /><ol><li>From the operation shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Method</strong> and either <strong>Behavior</strong>, or <strong>Diagram.</strong></li><li><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">Select one of either behaviors or diagrams you wish to create.</span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an operation parameter, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Operation Specification window, <strong>Parameters</strong> property group, click the <strong>Create</strong> button.</li><li>On the diagram, select an operation and type a parameter text in parenthesis.</li><li>Select an operation in the Containment tree and for the shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Element</strong> &gt; <strong>Parameter</strong>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69beafb2-9b24-44d0-a869-a7813cf1565c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1778044972</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/UML+elements">Model Elements</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Specification+window">Specification Window</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Parameter">Parameter</a><br /><br /></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=228985989 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00282: Operational Activity

- page_id: `228985989`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228985989/Operational+Activity
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:30:45.301+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An activity is an action performed in conducting the business of an enterprise. It is a general term that does not imply a placement in a hierarchy (e.g., it could be a process or a task as defined in other documents and it could be at any level of the hierarchy of the Operational Processes). It does not describe hardware/software system Functions.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Properties**

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| subject : ActivitySubject[*] | An object acting upon this OperationalActivity. |

**Extensions**

UML Activity

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span class="fontstyle0"><strong>Description</strong></span></p><p>An activity is an action performed in conducting the business of an enterprise. It is a general term that does not imply a placement in a hierarchy (e.g., it could be a process or a task as defined in other documents and it could be at any level of the hierarchy of the Operational Processes). It does not describe hardware/software system Functions.</p><p><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p><span class="fontstyle0"><strong>Properties</strong></span></p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Property</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><span class="fontstyle0">subject : ActivitySubject[*]</span></td><td><span class="fontstyle0">An object acting upon this OperationalActivity.</span></td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong>Extensions</strong></p><p>UML Activity</p><p><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00288: Operational Connector

- page_id: `228985996`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228985996/Operational+Connector
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:30:47.095+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Operational Connector is a Connector that goes between Operational Roles representing a need to exchange resources. It can carry a number of Operational Exchanges.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Connector

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 00291: Operational Exchange

- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228985999/Operational+Exchange
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:30:47.896+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Operational Exchange asserts that a flow can exist between Operational Performers (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy).

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

UML Information Flow

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 00293: Operational Information

- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228986001/Operational+Information

- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

Class

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=228986006 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00298: Operational Mitigation

- page_id: `228986006`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228986006/Operational+Mitigation
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:30:50.526+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A set of security measures intended to address against specific cyber risks. Comprises a subset of Security Controls that are required to protect the asset at Operational Performer (Operational Role).

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Class

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A tuple that indicates an open clash between two opposing groups (or individuals). This means that elements in the model are fighting against each other or are providing strong resistance to progress towards some goals. Note to entry: By individual, this does not indicate instances in the model.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

Dependency

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967882 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00314: Package

- page_id: `243967882`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967882/Package
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:43.458+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A package groups classes and other model elements together. All types of UML model elements can be organized into packages. Each diagram must be owned by one package and the packages themselves can be nested within other packages. Subsystems and models are special kinds of packages.

The packages can have dependency, generalization, realize, containment, and association relationships. These relationships are usually derived from the relationships between the classes inside those packages.

#### PANEL: Related References

Related References

true

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']

These relationships are usually derived from the relationships between the classes inside those packages.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1f41b4be-8cd9-4426-bbee-f0b2123bfdb9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related References</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="dbd3a28b-aa06-483b-9fe6-526f3f43eba9"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36e20852-22dd-420b-8d4b-52b136b22981"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967640 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00315: Package import

- page_id: `243967640`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967640/Package+import
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:37.555+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Dependency
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1890241659

1890241672

1890241661

In UML, package import is defined as a directed relationship between an importing namespace and a package whose members are imported into that namespace. The visibility of a package import is either public or private. The predefined keywords are «import» for a public package import, and «access» for a private package import.

A package import is displayed as a dashed arrow with an open arrowhead aiming from the importing namespace towards the imported package.

Package import applies to an entire package, and all the model elements of the target package are imported into the source namespace. The source package can access subpackages and their imported members of the target package to an infinite depth. The elements of an imported package are also accessible to the subpackages of the importing package.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the package import.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The visibility of a package import is either public or private. The predefined keywords are «import» for a public package import, and «access» for a private package import. <br /></span></p><p><span>A package import is displayed as a dashed arrow with an open arrowhead aiming from the importing namespace towards the imported package.<br /></span></p><p><span>Package import applies to an entire package, and all the model elements of the target package are imported into the source namespace. The source package can access subpackages and their imported members of the target package to an infinite depth. The elements of an imported package are also accessible to the subpackages of the importing package.</span></p><p><span><br /></span></p><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="package_import.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>An example of the package import.</span></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967890 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00316: Package Merge

- page_id: `243967890`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967890/Package+Merge
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:44:12.042+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Package
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A package merge is a directed relationship between two packages indicating that the contents of the two packages are to be combined. It is very similar to a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization' space=''], in that the source element conceptually adds the characteristics of the target element to its own characteristics, resulting in an element that combines the characteristics of both.

Specify the Package Merge in the Package Merge [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'].

You can format the Package Merge symbol properties in the[Symbol Properties dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Formatting+symbols).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">A package merge is a directed relationship between two packages indicating that the contents of the two packages are to be combined. It is very similar to a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Generalization" /></ac:link>, in that the source element conceptually adds the characteristics of the target element to its own characteristics, resulting in an element that combines the characteristics of both. </span></p><p>Specify the Package Merge in the Package Merge <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">You can format the Package Merge symbol properties in the<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Formatting+symbols">Symbol Properties dialog</a></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">.    </span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967572 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00317: Parameter

- page_id: `243967572`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967572/Parameter
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:35.398+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements > Class > Operation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Parameter** Specification window defines an operation argument.

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='MT'] properties in the parameter [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'], which also contains descriptions.

To add additional information about the return type of an operation

1. In the Operation Specification window, click on the Properties list, and select Expert mode. More properties for the operation are displayed.
2. In the Type Modifier property specification cell, select one of the following:
  - & - one class has a reference to another model element.
  - * - one class has a pointer to another model element.
  - [] - one class has an array of other model elements.

To define an operation as abstract, static, or query

1. In the Operation Specification window, click on the Properties list, and select Expert mode. More properties for the operation are displayed.
2. In the General property group, select one or all of the following
  - Is Abstract : the operation does not have an implementation; one must be supplied by a descendant.
  - Is Static : the values returned by the parameter have no duplicates.
  - Is Query : does not change the state of the system.

To set the operation visibility

- In the Operation Specification window, click on the Visibility property specification cell, and select one of the following:
  - Public : the operation can be accessed by any other object from the outside.
  - Package : the operation can be accessed by an element from the same package.
  - Private : the operation can be accessed only from that class.
  - Protected : the operation can be accessed from the inside of that class and the classes derived from that class.

To set an operation Concurrency

1. In the Operation Specification window, click on the Properties list, and select Expert mode. More properties for the operation are displayed.
2. In the Concurrency property specification cell, select one of the following:
  - Sequential : The callers must coordinate, so only one call to an Instance (on any sequential Operation) is made at a time. If simultaneous calls occur, the semantics and integrity of the system cannot be guaranteed.
  - Guarded : Multiple calls from concurrent threads may occur simultaneously to one Instance (on any guarded Operation), but only one is allowed to commence. The others are blocked until the performance of the first Operation is complete. It is the responsibility of the system designer to ensure that deadlocks do not occur due to simultaneous blocks. The Guarded Operations must perform correctly (or block themselves) if a simultaneous sequential Operation or guarded semantics cannot be claimed.
  - Concurrent : Multiple calls from concurrent threads can occur simultaneously to one Instance (on any concurrent Operation). All of them can proceed concurrently with the correct semantics. The Concurrent Operations must perform correctly in case a simultaneous sequential or the guarded Operation, or concurrent semantics cannot be claimed.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']

More properties for the operation are displayed.</li><li>In the <strong>Type Modifier</strong> property specification cell, select one of the following:<br /><ul><li>&amp; - one class has a reference to another model element.</li><li>* - one class has a pointer to another model element.</li><li><span>[] - one class has an array of other model elements.</span></li></ul></li></ol><p> </p><p>To define an operation as abstract, static, or query</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>Operation </strong>Specification window, click on the <strong>Properties</strong> list, and select <strong>Expert</strong> mode. More properties for the operation are displayed.</li><li>In the <strong>General</strong> property group, select one or all of the following<br /><ul><li><strong>Is Abstract</strong>: the operation does not have an implementation; one must be supplied by a descendant.</li><li><strong>Is Static</strong>: the values returned by the parameter have no duplicates.</li><li><strong>Is Query</strong>: does not change the state of the system.</li></ul></li></ol><p> </p><p>To set the operation visibility</p><hr /><ul><li>In the <strong>Operation</strong> Specification window, click on the <strong>Visibility</strong> property specification cell, and select one of the following:<ul><li><strong> Public</strong>: the operation can be accessed by any other object from the outside.</li><li><strong>Package</strong>: the operation can be accessed by an element from the same package.</li><li><strong>Private</strong>: the operation can be accessed only from that class.</li><li><strong>Protected</strong>: the operation can be accessed from the inside of that class and the classes derived from that class.</li></ul></li></ul><p><br class="_mce_tagged_br" />To set an operation Concurrency</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>Operation </strong>Specification window, click on the <strong>Properties</strong> list, and select <strong>Expert</strong> mode. More properties for the operation are displayed.</li><li>In the Concurrency property specification cell, select one of the following:<br /><ul><li><strong>Sequential</strong>: The callers must coordinate, so only one call to an Instance (on any sequential Operation) is made at a time. If simultaneous calls occur, the semantics and integrity of the system cannot be guaranteed.</li><li><strong>Guarded</strong>: Multiple calls from concurrent threads may occur simultaneously to one Instance (on any guarded Operation), but only one is allowed to commence. The others are blocked until the performance of the first Operation is complete. It is the responsibility of the system designer to ensure that deadlocks do not occur due to simultaneous blocks. The Guarded Operations must perform correctly (or block themselves) if a simultaneous sequential Operation or guarded semantics cannot be claimed.</li><li><strong>Concurrent</strong>: Multiple calls from concurrent threads can occur simultaneously to one Instance (on any concurrent Operation). All of them can proceed concurrently with the correct semantics. The Concurrent Operations must perform correctly in case a simultaneous sequential or the guarded Operation, or concurrent semantics cannot be claimed.</li></ul></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36e20852-22dd-420b-8d4b-52b136b22981"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967891 space=MED version=1 -->
## PAGE 00318: Part

- page_id: `243967891`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/243967891/Part
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:43.848+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

735980902

735980904

735990417

**On this page**

43

735980903

##### Creating a part

To create a part, do one of the following

- In the Composite Structure diagram palette, select Part and click on the diagram.
- In the Containment tree, select an element, which can be defined as a part type, and drag it to the composite structure diagram.

##### Creating a new composite structure diagram for the type of the part

To create a new composite structure diagram for the type of the part, do one of the following

- Select a part and from the smart manipulator toolbar, choose the New Composite Structure Diagram button. A new diagram is created under the part type.If type was not assigned for the selected part then a new type is created automatically.The created diagram is named after the part type.

- From the part shape’s shortcut menu, select Create Diagram and then diagram from the list. This accelerates the creation of composite structure diagram (the Create Diagram menu item is available only if the type is specified for the part).

##### Defining a type of the part

To define a type of a part, do one of the following

- On a diagram pane, draw a new part and in the opened Select Type list, select a type. The **Select Type** list is not displayed if the **Type Selection Mode** is disabled. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting port and part types' space='MT']>]]>
- On a diagram, select a part and press Ctrl+T (for MAC OS X press Cmd+T) and in the opened Select Type list, select a type.
- On a diagram, select a part and from the smart manipulator toolbar, select the Specify Type button. In the Select Type dialog, select a type.
- On a diagram, select a part and then type the name of the type after a ":". The type will be created with the specified name.
- In the part [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'] , Type property specification cell, select a type.

When creating a port on a part, if a part does not have a specified type, a new type for the part is created automatically.

The rake icon[IMAGE alt='' src='']is displayed on the shape if the interaction use refers to another composite structure diagram. The rake icon is not shown by default therefore you need to set the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying rake icon' space='MT']property value to true in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MT']dialog.

##### Hiding the multiplicity

To hide the multiplicity next to the part's type

1. Right-click the part shape and select Symbol Properties .
2. In the Symbol Properties dialog, set the Show Multiplicity property value to false .
3. Click Close .

735980901

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=9919468 space=MED version=4 -->
## PAGE 00319: Part Property

- page_id: `9919468`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/9919468/Part+Property
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:46:29.687+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > SysML v1 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Part Property is a property that specifies a part with strong ownership and coincidental lifetime of its containing Block. It describes a local usage or a role of the typing Block in the context of the containing Block. Every Part Property has composite AggregationKind and is typed by a Block. Part Properties are displayed in the **parts** compartment.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After setting a Block as a type for a Part Property the Composite Association is automatically created. You can see it in the Compartment area under the Relations node.

#### PANEL: Related diagrams

Related diagrams

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='MT']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

It describes a local usage or a role of the typing Block in the context of the containing Block. Every Part Property has composite AggregationKind and is typed by a Block. Part Properties are displayed in the <strong>parts</strong> compartment.<span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><br /></span></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Part Property" ac:alt="Part Property"><ri:attachment ri:filename="part_property.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2ab3bfb9-15b2-4025-822b-44daca5d92e9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>After setting a Block as a type for a Part Property the Composite Association is automatically created. You can see it in the Compartment area under the Relations node.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ad1d2f29-7ddc-4427-be11-2022073a6b8a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related diagrams</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="SysML Internal Block Diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 00320: Participant Property

- page_id: `9919403`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/9919403/Participant+Property
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2025-09-13T12:46:26.219+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > SysML v1 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1448744391

1448744393

1448744392

Participant properties can be the ends of connectors owned by an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association Block' space='']. The keyword «participant» before a property name indicates the property is stereotyped by *ParticipantProperty*. The types of participant properties can be elided if desired. They are always the same as the corresponding association end type.

1448744390

- Creating Association Block
- Using Association Block as Connector type

## PAGE 00321: Performance Requirement

- page_id: `249005170`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/249005170/Performance+Requirement
- version_number: 2

- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > Requirement-related elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Performance Requirement refers to a requirement that quantitatively measures the extent to which a system or a system part satisfy a required capability or condition.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<p class="auto-cursor-target">A Performance Requirement refers to a requirement that quantitatively measures the extent to which a system or a system part satisfy a required capability or condition.</p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=228986021 space=MED version=2 -->
## PAGE 00322: Performs In Context

- page_id: `228986021`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228986021/Performs+In+Context
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:30:57.010+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An abstraction relationship that relates an Operational Action to a Operational Role, or a Function Action to a Resource Role. It indicates that the action can be carried out by the role when used in a specific context or configuration.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

Abstraction

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">An abstraction relationship that relates an Operational Action to a Operational Role, or a Function Action to a Resource Role. It indicates that the action can be carried out by the role when used in a specific context or configuration.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Architecture</strong> <strong>Framework</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Abstraction</p><p><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00323: Person

- page_id: `228986022`
- space_key: `MED`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MED/pages/228986022/Person
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-05-14T10:30:57.290+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v1/UAF/UML Model Element Descriptions > UAF 1.3 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A type of a human being used to define the characteristics that need to be described for Actual Persons (e.g. properties such as address, telephone number, nationality, etc).

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.3, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Class

A Physical Requirement specifies the physical characteristics and/or physical constraints of a system or a system part.
